title: Toward Goal-Oriented Communication in Multi-Agent Systems: An overview
authors: Themistoklis Charalambous, Nikolaos Pappas, Nikolaos Nomikos, Risto Wichman
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: As multi-agent systems (MAS) become increasingly prevalent in autonomous systems, distributed control, and edge intelligence, efficient communication under resource constraints has emerged as a critical challenge. Traditional communication paradigms often emphasize message fidelity or bandwidth optimization, overlooking the task relevance of the exchanged information. In contrast, goal-oriented communication prioritizes the importance of information with respect to the agents’ shared objectives. This review provides a comprehensive survey of goal-oriented communication in MAS, bridging perspectives from information theory, communication theory, and machine learning. We examine foundational concepts alongside learning-based approaches and emergent protocols. Special attention is given to coordination under communication constraints, as well as applications in domains such as swarm robotics, federated learning, and edge computing. The paper concludes with a discussion of open challenges and future research directions at the intersection of communication theory, machine learning, and multi-agent decision making.

title: Optimal Prediction-Correction Algorithm Using Sparse Linear Extrapolation for Time-Varying Optimization
authors: Zhonghao Lin, Jie Hou, Xianlin Zeng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal Processing
abstract: This paper introduces an optimal prediction-correction algorithm leveraging sparse linear extrapolation for strongly convex, unconstrained time-varying optimization problems, which are prevalent in dynamic systems and online learning. The proposed method constructs the prediction phase as a sparse linear combination of past iterates, with extrapolation coefficients derived by solving an l1-norm minimization problem under tractable constraints. By promoting sparsity in the predictor, the algorithm reduces the frequency of correction steps and the associated computational cost of gradient evaluations. We establish the existence of an l1-optimal sparse predictor and derive closed-form solutions for second- and third-order tracking accuracy cases. Theoretical analysis confirms that the method achieves state-of-the-art tracking accuracy with improved computational efficiency compared to existing prediction-correction approaches. Numerical experiments validate the theoretical results, demonstrating the advantages of the proposed algorithm in reducing computational overhead while maintaining high accuracy.

title: Feature Extraction and Selection in Walking Surface Classification: Evaluating the Effectiveness of Multiple Sensor Configurations
authors: Mingfei Feng, Jiaqi Liu, Jian Xu, Hongbin Fang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Accurate classification of walking surfaces is critical for wearable biomedical and health informatics systems, particularly in applications such as gait analysis, fall prevention, and prosthetic control. However, a systematic evaluation of how different sensing modalities and their feature representations contribute to this measurement task remains lacking. In this study, we propose a multi-sensor evaluation framework to quantify the effectiveness of diverse signal features and sensor fusions for surface classification. We conduct biomechanical tests on healthy individuals walking naturally across four terrains: concrete road, synthetic track, grass, and sand. After performing signal processing, feature extraction, feature-level data fusion, and feature selection, we employ a random forest classifier to identify the surfaces. We thoroughly examine the effects of various sensor and training configurations on classification performance. Experiment results show that meaningful feature extraction and selection can enable accurate surface detection with a minimal feature set. Additionally, sensor fusion combined with feature selection can enhance performance, with the exception of the combination of insole and inertial sensors. Key features from different sensor configurations are identified, achieving a classification accuracy of 95.2 ± 3.3% with inertial sensors alone and 97.8 ± 2.4% with the combination of all sensors.

title: Enhancement of PECVD Chamber Dry-cleaning with Carbonyl Fluoride by Additive Gases
authors: Se Yun Jo, Sang Jeen Hong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Semiconductor Manufacturing
abstract: In the semiconductor industry, the reproducibility and repeatability of manufacturing process are most important to ensure the product quality to be consistent. The in-situ dry-cleaning (ISD) process of plasma enhanced chemical vapor deposition (PECVD) is performed between the material deposition steps to eliminate contaminants and improve reproducibility. COF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> is one of the candidates for the alternative cleaning gas of conventional use of NF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> because of its low GWP value. In our previous studies, we investigated COF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> cleaning gas with remote plasma source (RPS) dry-cleaning process and proposed additive gas O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> to reduce cleaning inhibitor and enhance cleaning rate. In this study, a further investigation with additive gases of O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O to enhance cleaning efficiency. We found that the mixture of O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O can enhance the dry-cleaning efficiency with COF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>. Chemical reaction mechanisms and production rate of species changes with numerical analysis were investigated to enhance the cleaning efficiency. We postulate that the higher cleaning rate gears from the increased amount of oxygen atom and radical production rate. Cleaning rates of SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> films with NF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> and COF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> were also experimentally compared, and we suggested that the use of COF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> with the additive mixture of O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> and N<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O is evident to replace NF<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> for the dry-cleaning gas for the environmental consideration with low GWP.

title: Tuning Access Class Barring Factor for Machine-Type Devices in Random Access: A Trade-Off Between Preamble Detection and Collision Resolution
authors: Zahra Alavikia, Atefeh Hajijamali Arani, Omar Alhussein
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Access control mechanisms for massive Machine-Type Communication (MTC) devices are essential for reducing the collision probability caused by the large number of access attempts during the contention-based Random Access (RA) procedure, which begins with preamble transmission. These mechanisms play a critical role in ensuring reliable communication services in fifth-generation (5G) cellular networks. However, the optimum number of admissible devices under given channel conditions that maximizes RA throughput, referred to in this paper as the effective access capacity, and its role in computing the Access Class Barring (ACB) factor have largely been overlooked. This study presents a detailed evaluation of RA throughput under varying channel conditions, focusing on two primary sources of failure during RA, namely undetected preambles due to weak signal strength and unresolved collisions resulting from mismatched Timing Advance (TA) values. These impairments degrade RA performance and constrain the achievable throughput. The trade-off between these two types of errors is analyzed to determine the effective access capacity that maximizes throughput. Simulation results show that, given specific transmission and reception capabilities of MTC devices and the Base Station (BS), wireless channel characteristics significantly influence the optimal admission level and overall system performance.

title: Enabling Full-State Observability in Pedestrian Localization via Active Kinematic Excitation
authors: Sizhu Ren, Zhe Li, Ping Zhang, Zhihong Deng
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Pedestrian Inertial Navigation Systems (PINS) enable ubiquitous localization for the Internet of Things (IoT), particularly in GPS-denied environments. However, low-cost inertial sensors suffer from significant error accumulation. While the Zero-Velocity Update (ZUPT) is widely adopted to constrain this drift, the standard ZUPT framework struggles to estimate sensor biases effectively. This limitation stems from the high repeatability of foot attitude during the stance phase, which provides insufficient kinematic excitation. Consequently, critical bias states remains weakly observable, leading to rapid positioning drift. To break the observability bottleneck, this paper proposes a novel gait-coupled discrete rotation method. Unlike complex continuous rotation systems, we utilize the swing phase to actively change the IMU attitude. A quantitative observability analysis framework is established to derive a mathematically optimal rotation sequence. To validate this approach, a prototype is constructed using consumer-grade IMUs. The comprehensive 30-minute 2-kilometer walking test shows an end-to-end precision of 1.35%D, demonstrating a 62.47% improvement over conventional configurations. This work confirms that active kinematic excitation significantly enhances low-cost PINS performance, offering a new paradigm for affordable high-precision localization.

title: vS-Graphs: Tightly Coupling Visual SLAM and 3D Scene Graphs Exploiting Hierarchical Scene Understanding
authors: Ali Tourani, Saad Ejaz, Hriday Bavle, Miguel Fernandez-Cortizas, David Morilla-Cabello, Jose Luis Sanchez-Lopez, Holger Voos
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Current Visual Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (VSLAM) systems often struggle to create maps that are both semantically rich and easy to interpret. While incorporating semantic scene knowledge helps build richer maps with contextual associations among mapped objects, representing them in structured formats such as scene graphs has not been widely addressed, leading to complex map comprehension and limited scalability. This paper introduces vS-Graphs, a novel real-time VSLAM framework that integrates vision-based scene understanding with map reconstruction and comprehensible graphbased representation. The framework infers structural elements (i.e., rooms and floors) from detected building components (i.e., walls and ground surfaces) and incorporates them into optimizable 3D scene graphs. This solution enhances the reconstructed map's semantic richness, comprehensibility, and localization accuracy. Extensive experiments on standard benchmarks and real-world datasets demonstrate that vS-Graphs achieves an average of 15.22% accuracy gain across all tested datasets compared to state-of-the-art VSLAM methods. Furthermore, the proposed framework achieves environment-driven semantic entity detection accuracy comparable to that of precise LiDARbased frameworks, using only visual features. The code is publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/snt-arg/</uri>visual sgraphs and is actively being improved.

title: Investigation of Mechanical Properties of Kraft Paper Insulation of Transformer Windings by Digital Image Correlation Technique
authors: N. Maheswara Rao, Hitesh Meghnani, S. V. Kulkarni, Krishna N Jonnalagadda
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: The mechanical characteristics of cellulosic insulation paper used in transformer windings are critical for ensuring reliable operation, as their degradation or failure can ultimately lead to electrical breakdown. Hence, accurate stress–strain estimation is very much needed. In this study, standard kraft paper specimens are experimentally characterized along the rolling direction (RD) and transverse direction (TD), whereas epoxy-dotted kraft paper raw specimens (uncured) are characterized along RD, TD, and along the dotted direction (DD), corresponding to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$45^\circ$</tex-math></inline-formula> to the rolling direction. A digital image correlation (DIC) technique is employed to obtain high-resolution, non-contact strain measurements. The resulting stress–strain responses are analyzed to extract mechanical parameters, which further improve the accuracy of the input parameters to the transformer FEM model, particularly the Young's modulus (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$E$</tex-math></inline-formula>) and the constitutive response derived from this study, thereby contributing to more representative stress predictions.

title: Event-Driven Double Dueling Deep Q-Learning for Efficient Serverless Edge Function Offloading and Scheduling
authors: Manish Pandey, Young-Woo Kwon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing
abstract: Serverless edge computing combines the scalability and cost-efficiency of serverless platforms with the low-latency benefits of edge systems. However, the limited computational capacity of individual edge nodes and frequent cold starts often lead to long waiting times and missed deadlines. Although horizontal offloading can alleviate local overload, conventional heuristics rely on static metrics and neglect critical conditions such as queue buildup, warm containers, and memory allocation, which frequently shift bottlenecks to neighboring nodes. This paper proposes a cooperative serverless edge architecture that integrates intelligent horizontal offloading with an adaptive in-node scheduling layer. A Double Dueling Deep Q-Learning (D3QL) agent guides task placement based on real-time feedback, while the scheduler at the chosen node prioritizes requests to increase warm-start reuse and protect deadline-sensitive workloads. The scheduler's decisions flow back into the agent, forming a continuous improvement loop between global and local control. Evaluations using production Azure and Alibaba traces show that our method consistently outperforms baseline approaches, achieving higher task completion and guarantee rates, reduced memory consumption, and improved warm-start ratios. Using a one-hour Azure invocation trace on OpenWhisk, our method achieves 67.46% higher task completion than local execution.

title: Synthetic Audiences as Proxies for Human Users: A Systematic Literature Review
authors: Theodoros Lappas, Apostolos Filippas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Synthetic audiences, defined as groups of AI agents powered by Large Language Models (LLMs),are a promising new approach to user research, enabling rapid, low-cost studies and experimental designs that are often infeasible with human participants. As interest in synthetic audiences accelerates across disciplines, the literature is rapidly expanding- making a systematic literature review both timely and necessary. Our work addresses this need by presenting the first comprehensive systematic review of LLM-based synthetic audiences, conducted in accordance with established PRISMA and Kitchenham guidelines. We searched major academic databases and preprint repositories and analyzed a large and diverse set of studies identified through a transparent, multi-stage screening and quality appraisal process. First, we outline their main applications in behavioral replication, experimental extension, market research, and large-scale simulation. Second, we review their key limitations, including hallucinations, bias, prompt sensitivity, and anthropomorphic overgeneralization. Third, we explore proposed solutions to these problems, including prompt-conditioning techniques, fine-tuning on human data, and hybrid human-LLM frameworks. Finally, we map out future research directions, including open-source auditing, standardized benchmarking and tooling, and mechanistic interpretability. We conclude that, even though synthetic audiences hold transformative promise, they also face significant limitations. Therefore researchers should draw on the growing literature and apply rigorous safeguards and methods to produce valid and trustworthy insights.

title: A Joint Spectral-Spatial Adapter for Domain Generalization in High-Resolution Remote Sensing Image Segmentation
authors: Yihui Liu, Yuning Fu, Chuanyao Li, Zuoxuan Zhang, Jianfei Cao
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Remote sensing semantic segmentation is a core task in Earth observation and plays an important role in urban planning and land-use classification. The emergence of vision foundation model (VFM) has significantly boosted the segmentation performance. However, in real-world deployment, a pretrained VFM must handle remote sensing images from other domains that it has never encountered. In this work, we advance domain generalization in remote sensing image segmentation by concentrating on the central challenge, namely, large cross-domain appearance shifts. A novel joint spectral-spatial adapter (SSA) is proposed to implement parameter-efficient fine-tuning on a frozen VFM, enabling better generalization capability. Technically, the proposed adapter consists of three components. Layer-wise spectral factorization combines a Haar-wavelet pyramid with spectrum-aware splitting to stabilize the content and structure-preserving details under cross-domain variations. Spectral-spatial mixture-of-expert modeling then refines these features through both spectrum and spatial updates, each of which is empowered by an expert model. Before decoding the segmentation map, energy-aware dynamic routing adaptively fuses expert outputs to suppress domain-specific styles without discarding boundaries and fine structures. On the ISPRS Vaihingen→Potsdam and Potsdam→Vaihingen settings, the proposed method achieves 62.6% and 71.0% mIoU, respectively, improving over the strongest comparable DINOv2-based baseline by 1.5 and 3.8 percentage points. On WHU Aerial→Satellite II and Satellite II→Aerial, it further improves F1/IoU to 46.9%/43.2% and 74.1%/72.3%, respectively.

title: Model-Free Predictive Current Control Under Ultralocal Model of FTPMV-RDM Based on Extended State Observer with Optimized Feedback Gains and Precise Input Gains
authors: Qingcheng Meng, Jingwei Zhu, Yunqi Qiu, Shukuan Zhang, Yechi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Traditional model-based predictive current control (MBPCC) is highly dependent on motor parameters. Although model-free predictive current control (MFPCC) based on a ultralocal model extended state observer (ESO) reduces this dependence, the optimality of feedback gains and the accuracy of input gains remain major obstacles to achieving high robustness. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel MFPCC for a six-phase fault-tolerant permanent magnet vernier rim-driven motor (FTPMV-RDM). The first innovation is a new approach for optimizing the ESO feedback gains using artificial neural networks, which leverage strong data learning and generalization capabilities to improve parameter coverage density and coordinate performance indicators with global optimality through a flexibly designed fitness function. The second is a novel ESO driving strategy that not only uses estimation error to estimate total disturbance, but also employs a high-update-rate prediction error model to solve the system input gain in real time. The third is the introduction of a reward-and-punishment mechanism to adaptively adjust the filter coefficient, ensuring its stability and optimization after input gain estimation. Furthermore, the candidate voltage vector set is screened to mitigate adverse effects of zero-sequence excitation, and the input gain is alternately solved by x-axis and y-axis prediction errors in the x-y harmonic plane, effectively addressing secondary control objectives. Experimental results, compared with traditional MBPCC and MFPCC, confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Effect of Interaction Circuit Configuration on Heat Dissipation for High Power Traveling Wave Tube
authors: Guangjiang Yuan, Jun He, QingLun Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: As the output power of helix traveling wave tubes increases, microwave loss within the interaction circuit increases correspondingly. The microwave loss, coupled with electron interception, leads to a rise in helix temperature, which is detrimental to the reliable and stable operation of the traveling wave tube. This paper investigates the influence of the interaction circuit’s structural dimensions on the thermal dissipation of the helix. Furthermore, it is established that determining the beam-wave interaction scheme for a high power traveling wave tube must be predicated on an interaction circuit structure possessing excellent thermal dissipation capabilities.

title: Synchronization Control of Fractional-Order Hyperchaotic Systems Based on Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Yan-Qiao Wei, Da-Yan Liu, Chang-Chun Hua, Zhi-Bo Wang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel synchronization control method for fractional-order hyperchaotic systems based on the fully actuated system (FAS) approach. First, the Caputo fractional derivative is adopted, and the synchronization error system between the drive and response systems is established. The error system is then transformed into an fractional-order equivalent FAS model via algebraic operations. Control inputs are designed to stabilize the closed-loop synchronization error system, and appropriate parameters are selected to guarantee the global stability of the error system. Simulation results verify the convergence of synchronization errors, which demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed FAS-based method.

title: Interpretable Regression Tree Analysis of EM Characteristics of Cavity Slow-Wave Structures
authors: Mita Jana, Nani Medicherala, Abhishek Kumar Jha, Sk Datta, Mv Kartikeyan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Accurate prediction of beam-wave interaction is key to efficient cavity SWS design. Conventional EM simulations are computationally intensive and offer limited insight into parameter effects. A regression tree framework is developed to relate militron or square cavity sws geometry to its EM characteristics using design samples. Evaluation with standard metrics and feature importance analysis highlights the dominant geometrical parameters. Results demonstrate that regression trees deliver accurate, interpretable predictions consistent with beam-wave interaction theory.

title: Fully Distributed Optimization for Multiple Flexible-Joint Robots: An Adaptive Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Rui Shang, Wei Sun
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This work develops a fully distributed control scheme to address the cooperative optimization problem of multiple flexible-joint robotic systems within a fully actuated framework. To better represent the real physical behavior, a high-order nonlinear model that incorporates the armature electrical dynamics is first constructed. Building on this model, a distributed controller is designed by combining fully actuated system techniques with distributed optimization principles, where only local neighbor information is required. The proposed scheme effectively mitigates the complexity issues typically encountered in high-order nonlinear systems, while ensuring that all agents collaboratively minimize a global cost function and asymptotically reach the optimal solution without dependence on global network information. Furthermore, Lyapunov-based analysis is employed to rigorously guarantee the stability of the closed-loop system. Numerical simulations are finally provided to validate the performance of the proposed approach in distributed optimization scenarios.

title: Predefined-Time Spacecraft Attitude Tracking with Tunable Anti-Fragile Performance Boundaries
authors: Guanning Zhuang, Tianrui Bao, Ying Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a predefined-time attitude tracking control strategy for a single spacecraft by integrating tunable anti-fragile performance boundaries. To ensure attitude tracking errors remain within predefined limits, an anti-fragile transformation method is developed to dynamically adjust performance boundaries, effectively preventing singularity issues under abrupt disturbances. Furthermore, a novel non-singular predefined-time sliding mode surface is designed to eliminate chattering and ensure tracking errors converge to a neighborhood of the origin within a predefined time. The practical predefined-time stability is rigorously proven via Lyapunov theory. Numerical simulations demonstrate the effectiveness, rapid convergence, and superior anti-fragility of the proposed approach.

title: Decentralized Fractional-Order Control for an Interconnected Nonlinear System via Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Yan-Qiao Wei, Yan-Hai Wang, Da-Yan Liu, Guo-Pin Liu, Chang-Chun Hua
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In this paper, a decentralized fractional-order stabilization controller is proposed via the fully-actuated system approach and fractional-order control theory for a class of coupled inverted pendulum systems modeled by interconnected nonlinear systems. The equivalent fully-actuated system model is derived for the controlled plant, followed by the design of decentralized fractional-order controller based on the fully-actuated system approach. Numerical simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed control method.

title: Kyujin Cho Elected as IEEE Robotics and Automation Society President for 2028–2029 Term [Society News]
authors: Kyujin Cho
publish: IEEE Robotics & Automation Magazine
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: A Weighted Projection Renormalization for Networked SIS Epidemic Dynamics Model
authors: Chen Zhou, Qingsong Liu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Opinion dynamics is a significant research direction in the field of control science. Existing research on opinion-driven epidemiological dynamics has primarily focused on traditional networks, while insufficient attention has been paid to the simultaneous interactions among multiple individuals in non-face-to-face settings. This paper presents a methodology for transforming it into a continuous-time opinion-driven SIS linear dynamic epidemic model, by projecting the simultaneous interactions among multiple individuals into a directed graph and reassigning weights, the coupling mechanism between opinion interaction and epidemic transmission are analyzed in combination with the opinion-dependent reproduction number. Furthermore, the analysis of equilibrium stability under different scenarios demonstrates that public opinion has a significant impact on the eradication of the epidemic. Finally, theoretical validity is established using the cont-hospital network.

title: Stabilization of Rotary Inverted Pendulum via an Improved Fully Actuated System Approach with Velocity Damping Injection
authors: Xujie Zhang, Guangren Duan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: We investigate the stabilization of the rotary inverted pendulum (RIP) through a novel fully actuated system (FAS) approach. Implementing this approach solely for the pendulum subsystem results in unstable zero dynamics of the rotary arm. To address this, we propose an improved FAS approach that injects velocity damping into the baseline controller. Then, a Lyapunov-based analysis is conducted to guarantee the asymptotic stability of the system. Furthermore, the controller parameters are optimized through an LMI-driven strategy with the objective of maximizing the domain of attraction. A numerical simulation based on the Quanser device parameters demonstrates the effectiveness of our proposed method, achieving stabilization from an initial pendulum angle of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5 \text{rad}\left(\approx 28.65^{\circ}\right)$</tex>.

title: High Frequency Characteristics of the Output Cavity of X-band Klystron Amplifier
authors: Zhang Yangyi, Xiong Shouqi, Zeng Zaojin, Jiang Yi, Sun Dimin, Ma Guowu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper presents a 3-D model for an output cavity of X-band klystron amplifier. By using 3-D electromagnetic simulation software, the high frequency characteristics including resonant frequency and loaded quality factor $(Q)$ of the output cavity are calculated. The effect of the output cavity size on the resonant frequency and loaded Q value are discussed. The results show that the frequency mainly depends on the opening angle of the coupling hole. Thickness of the coupling rods, length of the cavity and the gap within the cavity have a relatively small impact on the resonant frequency. As the opening angle of the coupling hole increases, the loaded Q value gradually decreases. With the increase of the thickness of coupling hole, the loaded Q value increases. The research result can provide theoretical references for the design of the output cavity.

title: Simulation of a transformed folded waveguide slow wave structure for D-band traveling wave tube
authors: Lei Wenqiang, Zhang Luqi, Jiang Yi, Ma Guowu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: As Terahertz communication technology developed, the research for D-band folded waveguide Traveling Wave Tube (FWTWT) has become increasingly important. A transformed folded waveguide slow wave structure (TFWSWS) in the D-band is designed. By the optimized design, the beam-wave interaction simulation is calculated in beam voltage 22.7kV and current 60mA. The maximal output power is improved to 48W in center frequency 154GHz. In the range of $142 \mathrm{GHz} \sim 164 \mathrm{GHz}$, the output power is above 20W. The -3dB bandwidth is 22GHz. The hot-test experiment will be done in next step.

title: Attitude Maneuver and Vibration Suppression for Dumbbell-Shaped Spacecraft Using a Fully-Actuated System Approach Based on Modified Rodrigues Parameters
authors: Yuehang Li, Feng Zhang, Zhaohui Gao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper addresses the large-angle attitude maneuver and vibration suppression problem of a dumbbell-shaped spacecraft under a fully-actuated control scheme. Distinct from conventional flexible spacecraft, the dumbbell-shaped spacecraft exhibits dynamic features with significantly higher nonlinearity and intense coupling. To this end, a four-order fully-actuated dynamic model of the dumbbell-shaped spacecraft is formulated. Subsequently, a parameterized full-state feedback controller is designed, accompanied by a state and disturbance observer to estimate unknown states and external disturbances. The proposed approach offers a simple yet effective solution from a practical perspective. Finally, comparative numerical simulations are conducted to validate the superiority of the developed method.

title: Development of Traveling Wave Tube Amplifiers for Photonic–Assisted Millimeter–Wave Communications System
authors: Ingeun Lee, Yoonseon Choi, Minje Song, Jinwoo Shin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A millimeter wave (mmW) communications system requires a broad bandwidth and a high radio frequency (RF) output power to realize high speed data rate and longdistance data delivery. Due to inherent characteristics of millimeter wave, which shows high atmospheric attenuation and vulnerability to rain rate, there is a strong thirst of compact, high frequency and high-power RF sources in communications field. To address this issue, we propose the photonic-assisted transmitter for mmW communication system. The mmW signal generation and modulation can be done by the photonic circuit. The modulated signal showing low noise and low power is amplified by traveling wave tube (TWT) so that quench the thirst of high RF output power. In this paper, we focus TWT designed- and measured-results for the mmW communications system.

title: Actor-Critic Enhanced Model Predictive Control with Dual-Tournament Online Evolution for Efficient Robotic Control
authors: Jiashuo Liang, Jingwei Xu, Shiqi Zou, Xuanbin Xu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel Model Predictive Control with Actor-Critic Learning (MPC-ACL) scheme equipped with a Dual Tournament Mechanism for simultaneous consideration of real-time efficiency and high-precision tracking for robotic manipulators working in complex dynamic environments. This approach alleviates a fundamental limitation of classical NMPC, namely its heavy computational burden and sensitive dependence on initial conditions which is characteristic of the cold start problem. The paper build an online evolutionary architecture based on Dataset Aggregation (DAgger) approach with expert demonstrations and on-policy data to fine-tune Actor-Critic networks and reach a good nonlinear motion prior.Subsequently, an embedded dual tournament mechanism links data-driven inference to physical constraints.Input Tournament optimizes the warm-start initialization by competing neural predictions against time-shifting strategies, tuning the optimization within the basin of attraction of the global optimum.Simultaneously, the Terminal Tournament incorporates both long-term value functions estimated by Critic network and Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR) stability constraints for avoiding finite horizon impact.The experimental results show that compared with the traditional MPC framework, the proposed algorithm improves the computational efficiency by 75 % while maintaining the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) tracking error of about 0.046 m. This performance gain is derived from the deep integration of data-driven learning and physical constraint optimization, which effectively verifies the synergistic effect of the proposed method on the robustness and computational efficiency of robotic manipulator in a random disturbance environment.

title: Multi-Task Fault Diagnosis in Distributed Power Grids with Lightweight CNN
authors: Wei Xu, Fuxiao Tan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Fault diagnosis in modern distribution networks has become more challenging with the increasing integration of distributed energy resources. The presence of multiple generation units and bidirectional power flow leads to more complex fault characteristics, making accurate identification of fault location and fault type essential for reliable system operation. This study develops a multi-task learning framework for simultaneous fault location and fault type identification. A modified IEEE 13-node test feeder with integrated distributed energy resources is implemented in MATLAB/Simulink to generate fault data. To improve feature representation, a time-frequency feature fusion strategy is adopted, where frequency-domain features are extracted using Fast Fourier Transform and transient characteristics are captured through wavelet analysis. Based on the fused features, a lightweight convolutional neural network incorporating depthwise separable convolution is designed to reduce model parameters and computational complexity. Experimental results show that the proposed approach achieves an average <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{F}_{1}$</tex> score of 0.99, demonstrating high accuracy in both fault location and fault type classification.

title: Consensus of Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Systems Under Deception Attacks: Dynamic Event-Triggered Intermittent Impulsive Control
authors: Yanjun Geng, Changchun Hua, Ruixue Cui, Yanyun Yang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This article is concerned with the quasi-consensus control problem of heterogeneous multi-agent systems (MASs) under deception attacks. We propose a discontinuous control strategy integrating impulsive control and intermittent control: Dynamic event-triggered intermittent impulsive (ETIIC) strategy, where impulse instants within the control interval are determined by a designed dynamic event-triggered mechanism (ETM). Compared to existing methods, our strategy reduces the number of impulse instants while maintaining control performance, thereby saving control resources. Moreover, the quasi-consensus error upper bound dependent on heterogeneity and attack energy is estimated. Finally, a numerical example is provided to verify the superiority of the dynamic ETIIC.

title: Distributed Cooperative Control of Multi-Impact Oscillator Systems Under Multi-Strategy False Data Injection Attacks
authors: Ruohan Mi, Yang Cao, Jiafeng Yu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Secure consensus tracking of multi-impact oscillator systems under false data injection (FDI) attacks is investigated in this paper. To describe malicious corruption of transmitted sensing data, a randomized multi-strategy FDI attack model is proposed, where different bounded attack signals are injected according to Bernoulli variables. Based on the compromised-state representation, a distributed feedback controller is designed for leader-following consensus tracking. Lyapunov analysis shows that, under bounded attacks, the resulting tracking errors are uniformly ultimately bounded. Simulation examples demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively attenuate the impact of FDI attacks and maintain the desired tracking performance of the multi-impact oscillator network.

title: A Review of Space-Based Infrared Detection of Aerial Targets
authors: Genggen Xuan, Yanqi Zhu, Guogong Li, Dong Chen
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Space-based infrared detection technology, offering advantages such as long detection range, good concealment, and strong anti-interference capability, holds significant application value in both military and civilian fields. It plays a particularly crucial role in high-value scenarios like missile warning, space target surveillance, and maritime and aerial situational awareness. This paper systematically reviews the current research status and development trends of space-based infrared detection of aerial targets. It first traces the evolution of U.S. space-based infrared early warning satellites, outlining the progressive advancements in detector performance and orbital deployment. Subsequently, it focuses on analyzing research progress in the detection capability for aerial targets, including analyses of target and background radiation characteristics, optimization of detection spectral bands, and signal-to-noise ratio evaluation. Concurrently, the paper systematically summarizes related technological and algorithmic research, such as dim small target detection, background clutter suppression, multispectral/polarimetric detection, and target altitude estimation. Finally, it discusses future development directions, identifying target recognition, multi-sensor cooperative detection, cross-modal data fusion, and intelligent processing as key factors for advancing space-based infrared aerial detection towards practical application.

title: Adaptive Cooperative Control for Nonlinear High-Order Fully-Actuated Multi-Agent Systems Under Full-State Interval Constraints
authors: Yuke Jia, Wei Sun
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper addresses the adaptive cooperative control problem for nonlinear high-order fully-actuated multi-agent systems with full-state interval constraints, where the state constraints are extended to be implementable over arbitrary intervals. Departing from traditional global constraint methods, we introduce a new interval constraint function into the barrier Lyapunov function, which allows state constraints to be active during a selected time period. This design overcomes the rigidity of fixed constraints and weakens the initial-state assumptions. Based on redefined error variables, an adaptive cooperative control scheme is constructed to fulfill the MAS cooperative control goals. Through Lyapunov stability analysis and the properties of the proposed barrier function, we rigorously prove that all closed-loop signals remain globally bounded. Finally, theoretical analysis confirms the efficacy and superiority of the control strategy. This method not only solves the full-state interval constraint problem for arbitrary time ranges but also enriches the control methodologies for nonlinear high-order fully actuated MASs.

title: Event-Triggered Output-Constrained Consensus of Nonlinear Multi-Agent Systems with Switching Topologies
authors: Ruixue Cui, Changchun Hua, Yafeng Li
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the output-constrained consensus problem for nonlinear multi-agent systems (MASs) under jointly switching communication topologies. To address tracking error jumps induced by topology switching, a distributed event-triggered observer is designed to reconstruct the leader's state, and the stability of the observation error is rigorously proved using integral inequalities and Gronwall's lemma. Furthermore, high-order filters are developed to process the discontinuous signals arising from switching and triggering, thereby facilitating the backstepping control design. Subsequently, by employing nonlinear transformations, a constrained output controller is developed to ensure that the follower outputs strictly adhere to predefined safety boundaries. Finally, simulation results are provided to validate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: Underwater Acoustic Doppler Velocity Estimation Based on Matched Filtering
authors: Cheng Li, FuXiao Tan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Utilizing the Doppler effect for velocity estimation is one of the key technologies in underwater acoustic sensing systems. High-precision velocity estimation is of great significance for applications such as underwater navigation, ocean current measurement, and sonar target tracking. However, in practical underwater environments, acoustic signals are often affected by factors such as ambient noise, propagation attenuation, and multipath effects, posing significant challenges to the accuracy and reliability of velocity estimation. Matched filtering, as a classical signal processing method, maximizes the output signal-to-noise ratio when the transmitted signal waveform is known. Therefore, this method has been widely applied in underwater acoustic detection and related parameter estimation fields.In this paper, a basic Doppler velocity estimation method based on matched filtering is investigated. First, the Doppler effect in underwater acoustic propagation is analyzed and a mathematical signal model is established. Then, a matched filtering receiver is designed to detect the echo signal and estimate the Doppler frequency shift. The velocity is calculated from the estimated Doppler shift. Simulation experiments are conducted using Linear Frequency Modulated (LFM) signals under different signal-to-noise ratio conditions. The results show that the proposed method can effectively estimate target velocity and maintain stable performance in moderate noise environments. The study demonstrates that matched filtering provides a reliable basis for underwater acoustic Doppler velocity estimation.

title: MPHINE-KT: Heterogeneous Information Network Embedding With Metapath Knowledge Tracing
authors: Meizi Li, Zhijie Shao, Li Xu, Maozhen Li, Yunwen Chen, Ru Yang, Bo Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Education
abstract:
Contributions: This article proposes a new knowledge tracing (KT) model called heterogeneous information network embedding with metapath KT (MPHINE-KT) to model the knowledge state of learners and predict the accuracy of learners’ future academic performance. To mine the transfer relationships between different knowledge points in sequences, the learning experience of learners is designed. The effects of individuality and commonality between learners’ learning experience and abilities through heterogeneous information networks are analyzed, learners’ individual attributes are introduced, and the problem of data sparsity is alleviated. The design improves the prediction effect of KT models on learners’ future performance.

Background: Online education platforms use KT models to analyze learners’ online study. These models assess and predict learning effects, providing multidimensional feedback and guidance. They fail to adequately extract the features of the transfer relationships between different knowledge points for learners. Moreover, they lack discrimination regarding the differences in the characteristics of individual attributes of learners, and does not fully integrate the multiattribute features of learners, resulting in the sparsity of input data. This restricts the KT model’s ability to predict learners’ performance in answering future exercises.

Intended Outcomes: The MPHINE-KT model analyzes the historical interaction sequence data of students and exercise problems, fully explores the relationships among learners, learning experience, and learning abilities, and more accurately predicts whether learners can answer new exercise problems correctly in the future, thus providing support for key educational aspects such as personalized education.

Application Design: The MPHINE-KT model first designs a learner’s learning experience recognition module based on a learner behavior graph for capturing learners’ complex representations in the learning process, and then constructs a learner heterogeneous information network. Finally, as an additional input, the learner’s feature vector is put into the gated recurrent unit (GRU) temporal prediction model, so as to improve the accuracy of predicting learners’ future learning performance.

Findings: Experiments are conducted on several real-world datasets, and the experimental results prove that the proposed model has a better performance compared to existing popular KT models.

title: A Fuzzy Logic-Based Adaptive Fusion Algorithm for GNSS/IMU in Conductor Galloping Monitoring
authors: Deshan Kong, Hao Chen, Bo Yu, Bowei Yao, Xiaoning Shen
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To address the long-standing trade-off between high accuracy and low power consumption in existing GNSS/IMU fusion systems for transmission line galloping monitoring, this paper proposes an intelligent adaptive fusion algorithm based on Fuzzy Logic Control (FLC). The proposed method designs a dual-input fuzzy controller that dynamically schedules the GNSS update process of a two-stage Extended Kalman Filter (EKF), using external motion intensity and filter state uncertainty as decision variables. This enables an intelligent, demand-driven allocation of sensing resources. To evaluate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed approach, a series of comparative experiments were conducted on a high-fidelity simulation platform under various wind-speed conditions, using fixed-frequency and conventional threshold-triggered methods as benchmarks. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed FLC-based algorithm achieves nearly the same tracking accuracy as the high-power 10 Hz baseline while reducing GNSS updates by more than 40 %. Moreover, it delivers significantly higher accuracy compared with the 5 Hz baseline under similar power budgets. These findings confirm that the FLC-based adaptive scheduling strategy effectively optimizes the accuracy-power trade-off in GNSS/IMU fusion, providing a feasible and generalizable solution for long-term, energy-efficient online monitoring of transmission line galloping.

title: A Review of Cooperative Guidance Technology for High-Speed Moving Targets
authors: Xiaoyu Zhuang, Mingrui Hao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: When facing high-speed and time-varying moving targets, traditional single-aircraft guidance modes are constrained by the detection range and overload capacity of the platform, making it difficult to ensure guidance accuracy. Multi-aircraft cooperative guidance has garnered widespread attention as a primary approach to break through the platform's own capability bottlenecks and achieve a multiplication of effectiveness. However, most existing cooperative guidance law design methods are focused on stationary or low-speed moving targets, showing insufficient adaptability to high-speed dynamic targets. In nonlinear environments, this leads to issues such as inaccurate estimation of remaining flight time, difficulty in maintaining stable cooperative configurations under overload constraints, and lack of adaptability to the target's game behavior. In response to these challenges, based on the current mainstream cooperative guidance methods, this paper considers introducing the theory of HighOrder Fully Actuated System (HOFA). While preserving the high-order structure of the system, establishing a complete matching relationship between the control input and the dynamic dimension provides a new approach for the analytical design of complex nonlinear systems. In summary, this paper systematically reviews the existing cooperative guidance methods and research status for dealing with the time-varying motion state of the target. On this basis, the theory of HOFA is introduced to further explore methods for improving the accuracy and robustness of cooperative guidance. The aim is to provide a systematic reference and theoretical support for the in-depth research and application of cooperative guidance theory for multiple aircraft in complex environments.

title: Output-Dependent FASs and Output Feedback Control, Part 3: Performance Control
authors: Guang-Ren Duan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper examines the performance control of output-dependent fully actuated systems (OD-FASs). Through control transformations, problems such as asymptotic tracking control, observer-based control, optimal control, and the control of uncertain OD-FASs can be converted into equivalent problems for linear systems. The proposed approach is demonstrated through the output feedback control of a manipulator with a flexible joint. In the presence of disturbances generated by a dynamical model, the closed-loop system achieves globally uniformly exponential stabilization without requiring feedback from the rotor position via the FAS approach.

title: Distributed Adaptive Optimal Secondary Control of DC Microgrids: A Fully Actuated System Approach Using an Identifier-Actor-Critic Architecture
authors: Sijie Zhang, Zhiqiang Zuo, Peng Li, Yijing Wang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a distributed adaptive optimal secondary control strategy for DC microgrids with nonlinear and unknown dynamics by incorporating the fully actuated system (FAS) approach. First, a large-signal model of the microgrid is reformulated into an equivalent FAS representation. To circumvent a direct analytical solution of the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation, an identifier-actor-critic architecture is developed based on the FAS representation. Within this framework, a neural network-based identifier is utilized to estimate and compensate for lumped unknown system dynamics. Meanwhile, critic and actor networks are employed to evaluate control performance and generate control actions, respectively, thereby achieving voltage regulation and proportional current sharing. Subsequently, the stability of the closed-loop system is rigorously proven. Finally, simulation results are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy.

title: Octopus-Inspired Embodied Tactile Intelligence: A Universally Calibrated Miniature Vision-Based Suction Cup_supp5-3694100.mp4
authors: Rob B.N. Scharff
publish: N/A
abstract: The integration of high-resolution tactile sensors in suction cups is a key requirement for the realization of octopus-inspired control strategies in soft continuum robotic arms. However, existing solutions lack simultaneous force and orientation detection and either provide insufficient resolution or are too bulky for integration into soft continuum robotic arms. Moreover, inconsistent manufacturing often necessitates labor-intensive individual calibration for each suction cup. This article introduces a miniature suction cup (22 mm × 40 mm) with integrated vision-based detection of pressing force and contact orientation. Through consistent multimaterial additive manufacturing and a robust model architecture trained on augmented, cross-device datasets, this predictive model can be universally applied across devices of identical design, thus eliminating the need for individual calibration of each suction cup. High-resolution and high-accuracy orientation [pitch/roll mean absolute error (MAE): 1.43/1.45 degrees] and force detection (Fx/Fy/Fz MAE: 0.254/0.256/0.763 N) enable closed-loop, perpendicular approaches for optimal sealing. Real-world tests confirm robust handling of small, delicate, misaligned, and curved objects. These results represent an important step toward scalable, high-resolution tactile sensing for octopus-inspired soft robotic manipulation.

title: Embroidery Actuator Utilizing Embroidery Patterns to Generate Diverse Fabric Deformations_supp1-3703257.mp4
authors: Yuki Funabora
publish: N/A
abstract: This paper presents a novel Embroidery Actuator, a fabric-integrated pneumatic actuator that enables diverse and controllable deformations through embroidery pattern design. Unlike conventional fabric actuators that rely on fiber- or thread-shaped actuators, the proposed actuator is fabricated by directly stitching an inflatable tube onto the fabric using a cord-embroidery technique. The embroidered thread and the fabric jointly form a sleeve that constrains the expansion of the inflatable tube, converting internal pressure into targeted bending or stretching deformations. By varying the embroidery pattern, such as zigzag or cross configurations, different geometric constraints can be realized, allowing for flexible control of deformation direction and magnitude. Analytical deformation models based on the Neo-Hookean model and Lagrange's equations were developed to predict the relationship between pneumatic pressure and bending angle. And then,experiments demonstrated that the actuator achieved 47 degrees of flexion on the fabric surface side and 165 degrees on the reverse side by altering the embroidery pattern. Additionally, the created model expressed deformation with an error margin of several degrees.

title: The Editors’ Desk [Editors' Desk]
authors: Bing J. Sheu, Shao-Ku Kao
publish: IEEE Nanotechnology Magazine

title: Compact Low-Loss Slot Array Antenna Based on Strip-on-Substrate Coaxial Multi-Layer Waveguide for 77 GHz Automotive Radar
authors: Enlin Wang, Abbas Vosoogh, Carlo Bencivenni, Sadegh Mansouri Moghaddam, Jian Yang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A compact, ultra-thin, low-loss and low-cost slot array antenna is presented for 77-GHz automotive radar, offering high polarization purity. Using strip-on-substrate coaxial multi-layer waveguide (SCMLW) technology, the design features a linear dogbone-shaped non-tilted centrally aligned slot array fed by a meandered metallic strip of the SCMLW. The proposed coaxial line-fed slotted antenna eliminates the angular and positional offset of slot employed in earlier designs, which are known to increase cross-polarization. Four prototypes, two 1×6 and two 2×6 slot arrays, were fabricated by chemical etching on a single platform with an overall thickness of only 0.9 mm, where long slits are introduced to improve radiation patterns. Measurements over 76–81 GHz show reflection coefficients better than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$-10$</tex-math></inline-formula> dB and relative cross-polarization levels below -29 dB for all prototypes, confirming their suitability for millimeter-wave automotive radar applications.

title: Enabling Efficient Parkinson’s Detection: Comparative Study of Dorsum and Wrist, Left and Right Side Hand-Worn Inertial Measurement Unit Placement
authors: Mohammed Hammoud, Andrey Somov
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Parkinson’s disease (PD) is a disorder that affects movement and worsens over time. Common PD symptoms include tremor and difficulty with coordination. Typical sensor-based systems for PD detection rely on fixed sensor placements, limited subsensor inputs, or narrow task sets. Unlike the state-of-the-art that relies on static sensor configurations, in this study, we assess how sensor placement (wrist vs. dorsum), modality, exercise selection, and data processing affect the upper-limb detection of PD under the Leave-One-Subject-Out validation. Data from 55 participants (34 with PD) performing 11 standardized tasks were processed with a 0.2-13 Hz and 0.2-20 Hz band-pass filtering and a 279-feature multimodal representation (per one Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) sensor). Statistical comparisons employed the Friedman/Nemenyi tests for paired, unbiased evaluation. Dorsum sensors (notably the left one) surpassed wrists. Fusion of accelerometer (A), gyroscope (G), and magnetometer (M) data outperformed single modalities. The posture-holding exercise, hand-opening/closing exercise, and chest-height postural exercise produced the most robust PD-specific signatures, and performance remained stable across the bandwidths. The best configurations obtained ROC of 0.952 and F1-binary of 0.957 with a low Brier of 0.042, demonstrating strong discrimination and reliable calibration. Sensors-only and Sensors+Age models were statistically indistinguishable, suggesting that the discriminative power primarily arises from movement-derived features rather than demographic differences. Overall, the results identify the minimal yet highly effective sensor–exercise combinations suitable for routine clinical or remote monitoring and are validated on this dataset, for practical, interpretable PD assessment.

title: Equilibrium Point Construction for Linearized EMT State-Space Model of Grid-Connected Power Converter Systems
authors: Yuhong Wang, Miaohong Su, Shilin Gao, Ying Chen, Jianyu Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: In contrast to the phasor-based electromechanical transient model, the electromagnetic transient (EMT) model is an instantaneous value-based model. Many state variables in the EMT model exhibit periodic time-varying behavior, making it difficult to construct a linearized state-space model at an equilibrium point. To address this problem, the equilibrium point in EMT state-space modeling is analyzed and discussed for grid-connected power converter systems. Subsequently, an equilibrium point construction method based on shifted-frequency analysis (SFA) is proposed, and its effectiveness for unbalanced systems is proved. Finally, the correctness of the theoretical analysis is validated through a grid-connected doubly-fed induction generator (DFIG) system and a wind power integrated modular multilevel converter-based high voltage direct current (MMC-HVDC) system.

title: Further Results on Trajectory Tracking Control of Vertical Take-Off and Landing Aircraft
authors: Tingting Qiao, Junchao Wei, Zhongcai Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In this paper, a new tracking control approach is proposed for vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) aircraft. A globally invertible coordinate transformation is introduced to decompose the underactuated, strongly coupled VTOL system into independent attitude and position subsystems. For the attitude subsystem, a finite-time tracking controller is designed to ensure the convergence of roll angle and angular velocity errors within finite time. For the position subsystem, a backstepping-based tracking controller is developed to achieve the asymptotic tracking of reference trajectories. Stability analysis confirms that all system errors converge to zero. The proposed approach effectively mitigates complexity induced by strong coupling and underactuation, thereby enhancing the tracking performance and robustness of VTOL aircraft systems.

title: Front Cover Page
authors: N/A

title: Fully Actuated System Approach-Based Practical Prescribed-Time Robust Control for Unmanned Helicopter
authors: Junjie Zhu, Wanglei Cheng, Ke Zhang, Bin Jiang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates a practical prescribed-time robust control strategy for unmanned helicopter with uncertainties based on fully actuated system approach (FASA). First, the dynamics for the position outer loop and attitude inner loop of helicopter are established, and the original underactuated system is transformed into a fully actuated system using the FASA. Then, a practical prescribed-time controller is proposed and implemented for the fully actuated helicopter system. In the proposed strategy, a hyperbolic tangent (tanh) signum function is adopted as the robust term to effectively suppress external disturbances. Finally, simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Transport & Stability of a Polarized Electron Beam
authors: Carter M. Armstrong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Results of a previous experiment on the transport and stability of a low-energy, magnetically-confined nonneutral electron beam are reported and compared with recent theoretical analysis. The effect of partial beam neutralization, obtained through ionization of a controlled background gas, is examined. The findings are seen to be consistent with the excitation of a long wavelength ion-resonance transverse dipole instability.

title: Energy-Information Coupled Hierarchical Path Planning for Multi-Auv Fixed Nodes Access and Maximum Seek
authors: Senjie Qiao, Song Han, Jiaao Zhao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: A hierarchical collaborative planning framework for multiple Autonomous Underwater Vehicles (AUVs) is proposed to address the coupling of heterogeneous tasks: fixed-node inspection and maximum seeking. The framework adopts a SourceSeeking First, Node Access Second paradigm. It first employs a Power Diagram-based dynamic partitioning mechanism that integrates residual energy, Max-value Information (MVI) seeking pressure, and node density to achieve self-organized task handover and load balancing. Then, a Budget-Aware Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) algorithm with hard constraints is designed to ensure node inspection while maximizing extremum exploration. Simulations confirm that the method enhances source localization efficiency and multi-vehicle robustness while maintaining safety.

title: A High-Order Fully Actuated System Approach for Discrete-Time Periodic Affine Nonlinear Systems
authors: Ruijie Fu, Lingling Lv
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Focusing on the stabilization of a special class of discrete-time nonlinear systems, this paper proposes a parameterized design approach based on the high-order fully actuated system approach. First, assuming the input matrix is invertible, an input transformation and structured feedback are employed to convert the original system into an equivalent, designable high-order linear periodic time-varying closed-loop model. Using the lifting technique, this model is then transformed into a first-order linear periodic system. On this basis, a periodic similarity transformation is constructed to recast the pole-assignment problem as the solution of a high-order periodic Sylvester matrix equation, thereby enabling exact placement of the closed-loop poles. The numerical example shows that the proposed method delivers state responses with both strong stability and the desired convergence rate, confirming its feasibility and effectiveness.

title: Tube Based Incremental Model Predictive Attitude Control for Morphing Aircraft: An Approach to Enhancing Robustness With Weak Model Dependence
authors: Hui Zheng, Guang Bin Cai, Yong Chao Wang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In this article, a tube based incremental model predictive attitude control for morphing aircraft with variable wingspans and sweepback angles is proposed. This method does not require a concrete mathematical model of the system, thus reducing the dependence on the system model. Further, By leveraging the Tube technique, the system robustness is significantly enhanced. First, the longitudinal dynamics model of the aircraft is introduced by considering the wingspan and sweepback angle deformation as additional control inputs. Subsequently, the dynamics equation is approximated by the incremental system, which reduces the dependence on the model information. Then, the incremental model is further simplified according to the time-scale separation (TSS) principle, and finally the global linearized system is obtained. By leveraging the model predictive control method, the optimal control problem (OCP) is constructed, and then a weak model-dependent predictive control method is obtained. Then, with the aircraft state constraints taken into account, the Tube technique is introduced to further enhance the system robustness. Finally, the numerical simulation has been conducted, the results verify great performance of the algorithm in terms of strong robustness, high computational efficiency and low model dependence.

title: Suppressing False Positives in Web Application Firewalls with Program Trace Anomaly Detection
authors: Min-Chieh Wu, Yu-Wei Liao, Yu-Sung Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Web Application Firewalls (WAFs) have become the standard line of defense against web security attacks in production systems. However, due to the diverse and continuously evolving nature of web applications, WAF detection rules (or models) must remain sufficiently generic to accommodate attack variants and reduce maintenance costs, resulting in a substantial number of false positives. We propose program-trace-based anomaly detection as a means to suppress the false positives. Our prototype, TraceSense, analyzes the control flow of PHP-based web applications at adjustable granularity levels to detect attacks without re lying on manually crafted rules. The evaluation based on real world Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs) demonstrates that TraceSense can accurately identify successful at tacks—comparable to rule-based WAFs—while reliably ignoring unsuccessful attacks that would trigger false positives in conventional systems. The alerts from program-trace-based anomaly detection are inherently self-explanatory. System administrators can use the built-in visualization tool to dismiss residual false positives and investigate unknown vulnerabilities.

title: A Novel Magnetic Gear Integrated Segmented Rotor Switched Reluctance Motor with Improved Performance
authors: Saptarshi Dey, Baylon Godfrey Fernandes, Kishore Chatterjee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Footprint constraint has always been a concern in industrial installations leading to the development of increasingly compact equipment, electric machines being no exception. The volume of an electric machine is a function of its torque rating and for a given torque rating, the machine volume can be reduced by improving its cooling (thus higher current density), using stronger permanent magnet (if any) and better electrical steel. This improvement is also limited by the fundamental laws of electrodynamics. However, the volume of the motor for a given torque can be reduced (in other words, the volumetric torque density can be increased) by integrating a passive torque amplifier (i.e., a gear) which itself is highly torque dense. Magnetic gear integrated switched reluctance motors offer substantial torque density with limited usage of permanent magnets. In this paper, a novel magnetic gear integrated segmented rotor switched reluctance motor is proposed. A 3.5 kW, 900 RPM topology is designed, simulated by finite element method, and its performance is optimised by genetic algorithm. The performance improvement is achieved by manipulation of the flux path with appropriate modification of its magnetic circuit. This complicated structure is fabricated and tested up-to its rated load current. Fabrication and assembly challenges arising out of this multirotor structure are discussed and their possible solutions are suggested.

title: Event-Triggered Attitude Tracking Control for Flexible Spacecraft
authors: Jiuyang Huang, Xinuo Li, Ying Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of attitude tracking control for flexible spacecraft under resource constraints and vibration suppression requirements. A novel control scheme integrating nonsingular terminal sliding mode (NTSM) and event-triggered mechanism is proposed to reduce communication and computational burden while ensuring high-precision tracking performance. A modal observer based on auxiliary variables is designed to accurately estimate unmeasurable flexible modes using angular velocity measurements. A fuzzy logic system (FLS) is employed to online approximate and compensate lumped uncertainties, including external disturbances and model uncertainties. A simple event-triggering condition based on the control torque error is designed to determine when the control signal should be updated. Rigorous Lyapunov analysis proves that all signals of the closed-loop system are uniformly ultimately bounded. A simulation comparison between continuous control and event-triggered control is conducted to verify the advantages of event-triggered control over traditional periodic control.

title: Flexible Planar-Structured Nanoscale Air Channel Devices Via Oblique Deposition
authors: Haiquan Zhao, Wanying Tang, Yuzhe Zheng, Feiliang Chen, Mo Li, Jian Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper presents a planar-structured nanoscale air channel device based on a near-ballistic transport mechanism, and demonstrates for the first time the fabrication of such a device on a flexible substrate. Using an oblique deposition process, an asymmetric angled electrode structure composed of $\mathrm{Au} / \mathrm{Cr}$ with a channel length of only 45 nm has been successfully constructed. Owing to its unique design, the device achieves a high output current of $24.6 \mu \mathrm{~A}$ at a bias voltage of 2 V, with a turn-on voltage as low as 0.61 V. These results confirm the device’s significant potential for lowpower, high-performance flexible electronic applications.

title: Research on Hierarchical Fault-Tolerant Control Strategy Based on Fault Factors
authors: Xu Qian, Liu Yuena, Liu Haitao, Wu Zengbin, Lu Yang, Zhang Tongzhou
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of high redundancy but frequent faults in the independent electric drive system of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> distributed drive vehicles, as well as complex model reconstruction, delayed response and insufficient compensation accuracy of traditional rule-based fault-tolerant control, a hierarchical fault-tolerant control strategy based on fault factors is proposed. Firstly, a fault factor is introduced to quantify the health state of electric wheels, and an 11-degree-of-freedom unified dynamic model covering 3 degrees of freedom of the vehicle body and 8 degrees of freedom of wheels is constructed to accurately describe multiple fault modes. Secondly, a hierarchical control architecture is designed: the upper layer dynamically determines the target total driving torque and target yaw moment based on regional pole placement combined with fault factors; the lower layer achieves optimal torque distribution under multiple constraints with the goal of minimizing tire adhesion utilization rate. Finally, verification is carried out on an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8 \times 8$</tex> vehicle simulation platform. The results show that the proposed strategy can control the 100 m lateral displacement deviation within 0.7 m under the straight-line driving fault condition at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \text{km} / \mathrm{h}$</tex>, effectively ensuring driving stability.

title: Research on Autonomous Perception Path Planning and Fully Actuated Control of UAVs
authors: Bai Yuzhe, Duan Guangren
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To improve the autonomous flight capability of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), this paper proposes an autonomous perception and path planning scheme for UAVs, and integrates it with fully actuated control. The scheme constructs a complete framework including perception and mapping, path planning, and trajectory tracking control: In the perception and mapping stage, an Intel RealSense D435i depth camera is used as the visual sensor, combined with the Vins-Mono algorithm to collect environmental data. After denoising and simplifying the point cloud through statistical filtering and voxel filtering, the occupancy grid mapping and map expansion are completed via Octomap to construct an environmental map suitable for path planning. In the path planning stage, based on the expanded occupancy grid map, the A* algorithm is used to plan a safe flight trajectory from the current position to the target position. In the trajectory tracking control stage, by establishing the UAV motion state equation, the system is divided into a position layer and an attitude layer, and controllers are designed respectively based on fully actuated control. The Routh-Hurwitz stability condition is used to ensure system stability. Simulation verification results show that the UAV can track the reference displacement signal quickly and accurately, which proves the effectiveness of the trajectory tracking control method in the proposed scheme. The full-process scheme designed in this paper realizes the autonomous perception, mapping and path planning of UAVs, providing technical support for UAVs to autonomously complete complex flight tasks. In the future, the application of this method in complex environments with uncertainties, disturbances and dynamic obstacles will be further explored to improve the robustness and applicability of the system.

title: Higher-Order Sliding Mode Control for the Fully-Actuated LCL Grid-Connected Inverter
authors: Yanyu Zhao, Xuemei Zheng, Yingming Wang, Paolo Mattavelli, Weizhen Liu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In renewable energy grid-connected power generation systems, LCL grid-connected inverters demonstrate outstanding high-frequency harmonic suppression performance. However, LCL inverters constitute complex third-order coupled nonlinear systems, making precise parameter tuning difficult with traditional PI control and failing to effectively address inherent resonance issues. To address this challenge, this paper employs fully-actuated control theory to reduce the order of the LCL inverter system model, thereby simplifying controller design. Based on this theory, a high-order fully-actuated (HOFA) model for the LCL grid-connected inverter system is derived. To enhance system dynamic response performance, this paper proposes a high-order sliding mode control (HOSMC) strategy to regulate the HOFA model of the LCL inverter system. Simulation results demonstrate that compared to conventional PI control, this strategy significantly improves grid-connected current control accuracy, accelerates system dynamic response, enhances disturbance rejection capability, and improves steady-state power quality. It also effectively reduces controller design complexity, making it more suitable for applications demanding high grid-connected power quality and robust control performance.

title: Insights from the 2025 Global Robotics Industry Funding Report [Industry Activities]
authors: Ali Shehadi, Andra Keay, Jim Ostrowski, Kaden Wince, Michael Harries
