authors: Nora Leuning, Thomas Nußbaum, Danilo G. Aurich, Sandra Korte-Kerzel
publish: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics
abstract: The magnetic properties of electrical steel are apart from chemical composition and purity, largely determined by microstructural characteristics, e.g., crystallographic orientation, grain boundaries and lattice defects. These characteristics affect both macroscopic magnetic properties and microscopic domain processes. While fundamental relationships between individual material parameters and magnetic behavior are well established, their complex interactions remain insufficiently understood. In this work, a coupled multi-scale experimental approach is presented to investigate microstructure-related magnetic properties of electrical steel. The methodology combines global magnetic measurements with local microstructural characterization and observation of magnetic domain evolution during magnetization. Oligocrystalline samples are employed as model systems to provide a controlled microstructure with a limited number of well-defined grains and grain boundaries to separate the effect of microstructure on the magnetic properties. Furthermore, samples are subjected to deformation. The presented approach establishes a direct link between microstructure, domain structure and magnetic properties and contributes to an improved understanding of magnetization mechanisms in electrical steel.

title: Secure-Guaranteed Gaussian Approximation Fusion Filtering for Multi-Sensor Nonlinear Systems with Intermittent Interruption Induced by Hybrid Attacks
authors: Ruonan Luo, Junying Hu, Jun Hu, Hongxiang Liu, Hui Yu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper focuses on the Gaussian approximation fusion filtering (GAFF) issue for multi-sensor nonlinear systems in the presence of malicious hybrid attacks. Unlike common random attacks of a single type, the malicious hybrid attacks considered in this work consist of intermittent random denial of service attacks and deception attacks, where a set of binary variables and Bernoulli random variables are introduced to model the occurrence of attacks. Considering the impact from attacks, a general local filtering method is designed in the Gaussian approximation filtering framework, the implementation relies on the numerical calculation of nonlinear weighted integrals. Subsequently, a diagonal covariance intersection fusion strategy is provided to fuse the available local information. Ultimately, the feasibility of presented GAFF scheme is validated via a comparative simulation example.

title: Crosstalk-Mapping Digital Predistortion for Fully Connected Hybrid Beamforming MIMO Transmitters Using Piecewise Approximate Vector Synthesis
authors: Qihao Shi, Xin Liu, Huanhuan Jia, Tong Shen, Jiahua Li, Chen Long, Xianzhen Lu, Yang Lu, Ziyue Zhao, Chupeng Yi, Ting Feng, Lang Tong, Hailong Wang, Wenhua Chen, Xiaohua Ma, Yue Hao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: A low-complexity digital predistortion (DPD) technique is proposed to address the linearization issue in the fully connected hybrid beamforming (FC-HBF) multiple input multiple output (MIMO) transmitters. The proposed DPD model is developed based on a piecewise approximate vector synthesis (PAVS) approach, with third-order polynomials integrated to enhance the linearization accuracy. A comprehensive analysis of the radiation characteristics of the crosstalk components is conducted, enabling the model to activate relevant crosstalk mapping (CM)-intermodulation distortion (IMD) terms selectively. In this way, the proposed DPD technique can substantially reduce the computational complexity while preserving excellent linearization performance. The proposed DPD is evaluated through simulations and measurements against the state-of-the-art DPDs. Simulations on a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16\times 16$</tex-math> </inline-formula> FC-HBF array show that the proposed DPD achieves superior linearization performance while reducing complexity by about 90% for four-stream configuration. Proof-of-concept tests on a linear array to verify its effectiveness, indicating that the proposed DPD can achieve competitive linearization performance and less complexity in comparison to the other models. These results confirm the strong potential of the proposed DPD for effectively linearizing FC-HBF arrays.

title: No Resource, No Benchmarks, No Problem? Evaluating and Improving LLMs for Code Generation in No-Resource Languages
authors: Alessandro Giagnorio, Alberto Martin-Lopez, Gabriele Bavota
publish: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have significantly advanced the automation of software engineering tasks. One prominent example is code generation, where an LLM produces code in a specified programming language based on a natural language description. Most research in this area has focused on high-resource languages, such as Python or Java, which benefit from abundant training data in repository platforms like GitHub. A smaller body of work has explored low-resource languages (e.g., Lua, Racket), which are underrepresented in training corpora. In contrast, no-resource languages for which LLMs have seen virtually no training data remain largely unstudied. These languages often emerge in industry, where organizations develop proprietary or domain-specific languages unsupported by commercial tools like GitHub Copilot. This results in the need for companies to deploy their own in-house code recommenders. To investigate possible solutions in this context, we build and release three code generation benchmarks for no-resource languages, based on two recently proposed programming languages for which very little training data is available. Using these benchmarks, we experiment several solutions to teach LLMs about no-resource languages, including prompt-based techniques (e.g., few-shot) as well as pre-training and fine-tuning exploiting the little data available. While further pre-training gives the largest performance gains for no-resource languages, applying it directly to instruction-tuned models harms their ability to follow instructions. To address this, we start from a base model, further pre-training it on the target language, and then inject instruction-following capabilities via weight diff transfer from an instruction model. Such an approach significantly improves code generation capabilities in no-resource settings, allowing companies to cheaply deploy an instruct model specialized on the language of interest without dealing with the computational cost of instruction fine-tuning.

title: WIMNet: A Whole-Image-Based Mamba Network for Joint Hyperspectral and LiDAR Classification
authors: Jiangyun Li, Jiaheng Han, Hao Wang, Xiaochen Zhang, Peixian Zhuang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Although recent advances have improved joint hyperspectral images (HSIs) and light detection and ranging (LiDAR) data classification, most existing approaches adopt patch-based processing strategy that leads to substantial computational redundancy for high-resolution data. Moreover, existing architectures usually process spectral and spatial features independently, making them difficult to capture their relationships and thus limits the fusion performance. To address the above challenges, we propose WIMNet, a Whole-Image-Based Mamba Network that performs image-level fusion for joint hyperspectral and LiDAR classification. Unlike existing patch-based approaches, the proposed WIMNet consists of two dedicated branches: Joint Spectral–Spatial Mamba Branch (JSMB), incorporating a Spa–Spe Mamba Block (SSMB) with a Spectral Modulation Mechanism (SMM) for adaptive spectral–spatial joint learning, and the Multiscale LiDAR Mamba Branch (MLMB) for LiDAR feature extraction. Then their features are integrated via a Multimodal Feature Fusion Module (MFFM) to produce the final classification map. Final extensive experiments on Houston2013, MUUFL, and Trento datasets demonstrate that WIMNet achieves state-of-the-art accuracy while reducing GFLOPs and inference time by up to 99% compared with existing 11 leading methods, confirming its remarkable efficiency in joint hyperspectral and LiDAR classification. Code will be available at https://github.com/Dufu9/WIMNet.

title: Characterization of Region of Exponential Attraction for Substabilization of FASs
authors: Guangren Duan, Xiubo Wang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper addresses the explicit characterization of the region of exponential attraction (RoEA) for sub-fully actuated systems (sub-FASs). By exploiting the eigenstructure of the fully actuated system (FAS) framework, the feasibility conditions of the closed-loop system are formulated as a family of time-dependent linear inequalities with respect to the initial states over an infinite time horizon. The proposed RoEA characterization method transforms the semi-infinite constraints into an equivalent finite set of active constraints, comprising the interval endpoints and, when they exist, the interior stationary points of the associated time-dependent functions. This transformation provides an exact finite-dimensional representation of the RoEA, converting the original infinite-horizon characterization problem into a tractable finite constraint description. In the two-dimensional case, the RoEA admits an explicit piecewise geometric characterization, where the boundary is composed of linear segments and nonlinear curves induced by the active constraints. The framework is further extended to higher-dimensional systems by establishing a finite active-set characterization under mild assumptions. Numerical examples, including a pendulum system and a Lorenz system, demonstrate that the proposed method accurately captures practically meaningful attraction regions while significantly reducing the complexity of RoEA characterization.

title: A Hierarchical Physics-Guided Surrogate Modeling Method for Electron Guns in Small-Sample Scenarios
authors: Guoxin Ren, Wanli Shi, Shilong Zhu, Luanfeng Gao, Xiaofang Zhu, Bin Li, Yulu Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: As a key component in vacuum electronic devices such as traveling-wave tubes (TWTs), electron gun design often relies on expensive high-fidelity simulations and experience-driven iterative tuning. Under limited data availability, conventional purely data-driven surrogate models are prone to overfitting and exhibit poor generalization. To address this issue, this paper proposes a hierarchical physics-guided surrogate modeling approach for electron guns in small-sample scenarios. Experimental results show that with only 394 samples in total, the proposed method achieves test-set Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of within approximately $2 \%$ for both the total current and the perveance, about $3 \%$ error for beam-waist radius prediction, and reduces the beam-waist position MAPE to around $4 \%$–5%.

title: Fixed-Time RBF Observer-Based Dynamic Event-Triggered MPC for UAV Formation Encirclement and Obstacle Avoidance
authors: Mengqi Sun, Ran Zhen, Jiangbo Jia, Zhizhe Zhao, Kuo Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper presents a robust model predictive control (MPC) scheme integrating a dynamic event-triggered mechanism (DETM) and a fixed-time radial basis function (RBF) observer for UAV formation. The framework addresses the critical challenges of 3D target encirclement, obstacle avoidance, and limited onboard computation in disturbed environments. To enhance predictive model robustness, a fixed-time RBF observer is developed for the rapid estimation and feedforward compensation of lumped disturbances. Additionally, a 3D artificial potential field is embedded within the MPC to guarantee safe obstacle avoidance, enabling flexible formation adjustments during critical maneuvers. To optimize computational efficiency, a DETM replaces the traditional time-driven paradigm, strategically allocating resources during dynamic events like abrupt wind variations. The closed-loop system's fixed-time stability is rigorously proven, with Zeno behavior explicitly excluded. Extensive simulations verify that the proposed algorithm successfully executes complex formation tasks under severe disturbances while dramatically reducing controller updates and computational burden compared to conventional time-driven MPC.

title: Matrix-Weighted Consensus of Second-Order Discrete-Time Multi-Agent Systems Without Velocity Transmission
authors: Tianyi Tang, Huaibin Tang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper studies the matrix-weighted consensus issues for second-order multi-agent systems under directed network topologies, where velocity information is not transmitted. A distributed control protocol is designed using only relative position information and each agent's own velocity. By employing matrix stability theory, necessary and sufficient conditions for achieving consensus are established, and a more concise criterion is derived for undirected topologies. The influence of coupling gains on consensus is also analyzed. Numerical simulations verify the effectiveness of the proposed protocol.

title: IEEE Transactions on Broadcasting Publication Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Broadcasting

title: A Multi-Dimensional Situation Assessment Method for Multi-UAV Cooperative Search
authors: Xinheng Xu, Xingwang Liu, Junting Dong, Lifeng Ma, Chen Gao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In this article, a situation assessment problem for multi-UAV cooperative search missions is investigated. To characterize the overall operational state of a UAV swarm during mission execution, a multi-dimensional situation representation framework is constructed. The system situation is described from three aspects, including task progress, search efficiency, and swarm health. Based on this framework, several quantitative metrics are established to characterize key properties of cooperative search, such as coverage performance, information timeliness, coordination efficiency, and system reliability. Furthermore, a unified mathematical model is developed using the analytic hierarchy process to integrate these metrics and provide a structured description of the mission situation. Finally, simulation experiments are conducted to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Nanoscale Vacuum Channel Logic Gate
authors: Anthony Tricker-Reissig, Marcus Pearlman, Deepak Sharma, Jim Browning, Shruti Nirantar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Nanoscale vacuum channel diode (NVCD) based logic gates are presented here. Lumped-element circuit model of NVCD unit cell is proposed using a combination of experimental data from fabricated devices and finite element modelling. Further, NVCD unit cell circuit is verified using SPICE model to determine current-voltage and rectification performance. NVCDs unit cell models are arranged to form a NOR gate.

title: Linear Quadratic Regulation for 2-D Systems With Synchronous Input Delay Via L-Shaped Transformation
authors: Yaning Wang, Xueyang Li, Chunyan Han, Wei Wang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the linear quadratic regulator (LQR) problem for both delay-free and input-delay twodimensional (2-D) systems via a dimensionality reduction approach. To address the dimensionality variation issue inherent in existing semi-1-D transformation methods, this paper proposes an improved model reduction strategy within the L-shaped modeling framework, optimizing the construction of equivalent one-dimensional (1-D) systems. To solve the two types of 2-D LQR problems, it is proven that under a given quadratic performance index, the original 2-D LQR problem can be converted into an equivalent 1-D LQR problem. On this basis, the explicit optimal state feedback controller is derived.

title: A Fully Actuated System Approach to Prescribed Performance Control of $n$-DOF Interconnected Nonlinear Systems via Cascaded Extended State Observers
authors: Tan Wang, Jinpeng Fan, Guang-Ren Duan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper studies prescribed performance control for uncertain generalized <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-degree-of-freedom (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex>-DOF) interconnected nonlinear systems (INSs) under the fully actuated system (FAS) approach. The considered systems contain nonlinear couplings, modeling and input-gain uncertainties, actuator nonlinearities, and external disturbances, while only position measurements are available. Cascaded first-order extended state observers (ESOs) are introduced to estimate the unmeasured velocities and lumped uncertainties. Then, a prescribed-time performance function is designed to impose predefined tracking constraints with a user-specified convergence time and terminal bound. By using an error transformation, the constrained tracking problem is converted into a transformed error system. Based on the resulting FAS model, a compact control law is developed, yielding assignable closed-loop eigenstructure. It is proved that all transformed tracking errors are uniformly ultimately bounded and that the prescribed performance constraints are satisfied.

title: Prescribed Performance Group Consensus of Heterogeneous Multi-Spacecraft Systems Via Adaptive Fuzzy Pinning Control
authors: Zimo Sun, Min Ma, Yuyang Gao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the group consensus tracking control problem for second-order heterogeneous multi-spacecraft systems subject to stringent transient and steady-state constraints. To explicitly restrict the tracking error within a pre-assigned decaying envelope, a novel finite-time control scheme incorporating a prescribed performance mechanism is proposed. Specifically, an error transformation technique is employed to convert the inequality constraints of the spacecraft attitude into an equivalent unconstrained stabilization problem, thereby guaranteeing the desired dynamic performance. To mitigate the effects of unknown system nonlinearities and external disturbances, a fuzzy logic system (FLS) is utilized with adaptive tuning laws derived strictly from Lyapunov stability theory. Furthermore, a pinning control protocol is constructed within the backstepping framework under a directed communication topology, ensuring the attainment of group consensus by controlling only a small fraction of the spacecrafts. Numerical simulations are presented to validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed control architecture.

title: Electron Beam Degenerate Band Edge Oscillator in a Double-Corrugated Waveguide
authors: Miguel Saavedra-Melo, Robert Marosi, Nelson Castro, Eva Rajo-Iglesias, Filippo Capolino
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A degenerate band-edge (DBE) oscillator based on the interaction between a linear electron beam and four degenerate electromagnetic modes is presented. The DBE is an exceptional point realized in a lossless and gainless periodic slowwave rectangular waveguide with staggered double corrugation, yielding an extremely flat modal dispersion, and vanishing group velocity and its two derivatives. Full-wave particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations demonstrate stable single-frequency millimeter-wave oscillation with robustness to beam and cavity variations.

title: Event-Triggered Predefined-Time Performance Attitude Tracking Via Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Tao Guan, Bin Li
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This article presents an event-triggered predefined-time controller for rigid spacecraft attitude tracking. First, by utilizing Modified Rodrigues Parameters (MRPs), the rotational dynamics are mapped into a second-order Fully Actuated System (FAS) framework, inherently avoiding the complexity of conventional backstepping. Second, to ensure strict performance constraints, a novel boundary function is designed, converting the constrained error system into an unconstrained one. Third, an event-triggered mechanism is integrated to reduce communication frequency. Finally, a robust FAS-based controller is synthesized to actively reject external perturbations. Lyapunov analysis strictly guarantees that the tracking errors precisely converge within a user-defined time and all closed-loop signals remain bounded.

title: UAV Electro-Optical Pod Stabilization Control with ADRC and Elastoplastic Friction Compensation
authors: Qi Xu, Yue Zhang, Haichao Peng, He Wang, Yuhang Liu, Benfa Zhang, Chenyu Dong
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To address the issues of unstable optical axis and low tracking accuracy in electro-optical pods during live-line operations of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), this paper investigates a two-axis, four-frame electro-optical pod. It systematically analyzes three types of disturbance characteristics-model, torque, and noise-and establishes an accurate system dynamics model. An elastoplastic friction model is employed to design a friction feedforward compensation loop. This is integrated with adaptive disturbance rejection control (ADRC) to form a composite control strategy, whose effectiveness is validated through simulation experiments. Hardware experiments demonstrated a peak tracking error of only 0.14 mrad under a 1 rad/s constant-speed input signal, with multi-target tracking errors consistently below 1.44 mrad. These results confirm that the proposed method effectively suppresses visual axis jitter, significantly enhances pod visual axis stability and target tracking accuracy, and provides reliable technical support for UAV power line maintenance operations.

title: Security Weakness of MASs With Event-Triggered Control for Distributed Replay Attacks
authors: Jingbo Wang, Changda Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Multi-agent systems (MASs) are increasingly vulnerable to cyberattacks. This paper investigates security weakness of leaderless discrete-time MASs with event-triggered control (ETC) for distributed replay attacks (dRAs). Firstly, an ETC strategy for leaderless discrete-time MASs is proposed, where the update of controller and the triggering times of any agent are independent of triggering time instants of all other agents. Secondly, the destructiveness of dRAs against MASs under ETC is analyzed, where the state of each agent and its historical state achieve input-to-state stability. Furthermore, under three specific conditions, if dRAs persists long enough, the state of each agent will coincide with its historical state. Finally, numerical examples are provided to verify the security weakness.

title: Based on Particle Swarm Optimization for Load Mutation Suppression Strategy in Rectifier-Motor Systems of Islanded Power Grids
authors: Depeng Zeng, Junfeng Ou, Xiaoqian Feng, Qingwei Zheng, Junjie Hong, Bowen Wei, Liheng Chen, Zhiguang Feng
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Due to their limited capacity and complex dynamic impedance, isolated offshore grid systems are highly prone to severe voltage fluctuations and harmonic distortion when high-power loads such as electric motors are connected, thereby threatening grid stability and power quality. To address this issue, this paper constructs a simulation model comprising a diesel generator, transformer, rectifier, inverter, and electric motor. First, the shortcomings of isolated power grids under traditional direct loading methods were analyzed. Subsequently, a coordinated control strategy combining dual-loop voltage and current control of the rectifier with dual-loop vector control of the motor was adopted, effectively reducing grid-side voltage fluctuations and improving the system's dynamic response speed. To further mitigate the impact of sudden load changes on the grid side, this paper designs a stepwise, tiered load-connection strategy. Simulation results indicate that this strategy significantly shortens the grid-side voltage instability duration and reduces the total harmonic distortion (THD) of the current. Building on this, a particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm is introduced to optimize the connection times for the stepwise load-connection strategy. Comparison results show that, following optimization by the particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm, the voltage instability duration during the loading process is further reduced, grid reliability is enhanced, and THD is better controlled, thereby improving the operational quality of the ship's isolated power grid.

title: Networked $H_{\infty}$ Control for Nonlinear Fully Actuated Systems Under Round-Robin Scheduling and Multirate Sampling
authors: Jian Zou, Dawei Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the networked <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> control problem for a class of nonlinear fully actuated systems subject to multi-rate sampling, Round-Robin (R-R) scheduling, and bounded communication delays. By deploying heterogeneous sensors with distinct sampling rates, the R-R protocol is employed to orchestrate the transmission instants of the multi-rate sampled data, thereby enabling a time-sliced update of the control signal which is anticipated to enhance control performance. A fully actuated sampled-data controller is designed, and the resulting closed-loop system is rigorously modeled as a multiple input time-delay system with asynchronous state error constraints. By constructing a Lyapunov-Krasovskii functional that explicitly depends on the bounds of the multiple input delays, a delay-dependent sufficient condition for the existence of the controller parameters is derived, ensuring that the closed-loop system is exponentially stable with a prescribed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> performance index. The effectiveness of the proposed control methodology is validated through a nonlinear networked control problem involving vehicle steering.

title: Pole-Dynamics Attacks Detection in MASs by Controller-Side Identical Watermarking
authors: Xuanfeng Shi, Changda Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Distributed pole-dynamics attacks (dPDAs) tamper with inter-agent communication to hijack multi-agent systems (MASs) consensus while maintaining stealthiness, which conventional methods fail to detect effectively. To address these issues, a controller-side identical watermark (CIW) scheme is proposed, which embeds consistent watermark signals into all agents' control inputs. Based on the Laplacian matrix properties of undirected graphs, the scheme eliminates false alarms under attack-free conditions and identifies dPDAs by detecting watermark feature deviations. Theoretical analysis proves that the detection function is zero in normal operation and nonzero under dPDAs, thus removing attack stealthiness. Finally, numerical simulations verify the effectiveness of the proposed method in dPDAs detection.

title: Descriptor: Synthetic Underground Hydrogen Storage Measurement, Reporting and Verification Dataset (SUHS-MRV)
authors: Sreekanth Muktevi, Yogesh Nagpal, Rajesh Leela, Jyotsna Muktevi
publish: IEEE Data Descriptions
abstract: This paper presents the Synthetic Underground Hydrogen Storage Measurement, Reporting, and Verification dataset (SUHS-MRV), a synthetic, rule-based dataset developed to support early-stage Measurement, Reporting, and Verification (MRV) workflows for underground hydrogen storage (UHS). The dataset simulates cyclic hydrogen injection, withdrawal, losses, cushion-gas behavior, methane co-production, and compression energy across three reservoir types: salt caverns, depleted gas reservoirs, and aquifers. Records are provided at daily, monthly, and annual resolutions, where the daily interval serves as a bookkeeping construct rather than an operational assumption. Data are generated using deterministic, open-source Python workflows with fixed-seed reproducibility and validation checks for mass balance and reservoir-specific logic. All fields are mapped to the Open Footprint (OFP) environmental data model and the Open Subsurface Data Universe (OSDU) Well-Known Schemas (WKS) framework to enable schema-driven interoperability and MRV workflow prototyping. The dataset, codebase, and documentation are openly archived to support research, education, and digital integration testing.

title: Semi-Global Output-Feedback Control for Underactuated Nonlinear Systems Under Sensor Faults
authors: Yana Yang, Wenqi Wang, Xiaoshuang Zhou, Changlong Liu, Junpeng Li
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper addresses the semi-global output feedback tracking control problem for a class of underactuated nonlinear systems with non-strict feedback structure and sensor faults. Firstly, an adaptive estimation mechanism is designed to reconstruct both the system states and the sensor fault parameters using only the faulty output measurements. Then, by introducing transformation functions and a time varying scaling function, a performance constraint method independent of restrictive initial conditions is proposed, which guarantees that the tracking errors evolve within the prescribed semi-global performance bounds. On this basis, a new output feedback fault tolerant controller is constructed by integrating a fault detection mechanism with the prescribed performance requirements. Theoretical analysis shows that, in the presence of both sensor faults and parametric uncertainties, all signals of the closed-loop system remain bounded and the tracking errors always satisfy the predefined semi-global prescribed performance constraints. Finally, simulation studies on an inertia wheel pendulum (IWP) system demonstrate that the proposed approach possesses satisfactory tracking performance, robustness and fault tolerance capability.

title: Camera-Parameter-Independent Visual Servoing for Mobile Robots
authors: Yu Zhang, Tong Yao, Licui Zhao, Yixu Cai
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Visual servoing mobile robots have attracted significant attention due to their wide range of applications. Although existing works have achieved notable success in handling unknown camera parameters, most still rely on online parameter estimation, which introduces computational complexity and potential instability. To overcome these limitations, this paper presents a kinematic control framework for camera-based visual servoing of mobile robots with uncalibrated cameras. By selecting the robot's position coordinates along with two additional non-collinear points, we directly replace the elements of the depthindependent image Jacobian matrix that depend on unknown camera parameters with measurable state information. The proposed approach is rigorously proven to ensure exponential convergence of the closed-loop system without requiring camera calibration or parameter estimation, making it suitable for many real-world scenarios. Simulation results are provided to demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed scheme.

title: Adaptive Non-Singular Fast Terminal Sliding Mode Control for Upper-Limb Rehabilitation Robots
authors: Peng Zhang, Xiangai Ji
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: During rehabilitation of patients in the spastic phase, upper-limb rehabilitation robots face a major challenge: the affected limb exhibits strong, nonlinear and time-varying muscle tone, which can induce pronounced oscillations and frequent posture adjustments, undermining trajectory tracking accuracy. To address this, this study proposes an Adaptive Non-Singular Fast Terminal Sliding Mode Controller (AN-FTSMC). To enhance sliding-surface performance, the controller incorporates an AN-FTSMC manifold that adaptively adjusts its exponential coefficients according to the system's state, thereby accelerating convergence and improving precision. To further mitigate the chattering observed during the reaching phase, the design integrates a Composite Power Reaching Law (CPRL) and an adaptive boundary saturation function. By employing a hyperbolic tangent function to constrain disturbance amplitudes and avoid singularities, the controller achieves smoother trajectory tracking and higher overall control accuracy. System stability is rigorously validated through Lyapunov analysis. Comparative simulations demonstrate that this sliding-mode approach outperforms alternative control strategies, providing superior robustness and enhanced tracking precision, underscoring its practical potential in rehabilitation robotics.

title: Diffusion Disambiguation Models for Partial Label Learning
authors: Jinfu Fan, Xiaohui Zhong, Kangrui Ren, Jiangnan Li, Linqing Huang, Min Gan, C. L. Philip Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Learning from ambiguous labels is a long-standing problem in practical machine learning applications. The purpose of partial label learning(PLL) is to identify the ground-truth label from a set of candidate labels associated with a given instance. Inspired by the remarkable performance of diffusion models in various generation tasks, this paper explores their potential to denoise ambiguous labels through the reverse denoising process. Therefore, this paper reformulates the label disambiguation problem from the perspective of generative models, where labels are generated by iteratively refining initial random guesses. This perspective enables the diffusion model to learn how label information is generated stochastically. By modeling the generation uncertainty, we can use the maximum likelihood estimate of the label for classification inference. However, such ambiguous labels lead to a mismatch between instance and label, which reduces the quality of generated data. To address this issue, this paper proposes a diffusion disambiguation model for PLL (DDMP), which first uses the potential complementary information between instances and labels to construct pseudo-clean labels for initial diffusion training. Furthermore, a transition-aware matrix is introduced to estimate the potential ground-truth labels, which are dynamically updated during the diffusion generation. During training, the ground-truth label is progressively refined, improving the classifier. Experiments show the advantage of the DDMP and its suitability for PLL.

title: A Novel Modified Logistic Function Based PV Source Modeling for PV Power Applications
authors: Durgesh Chandra Nautiyal, Himanshu Sekhar Sahu, Vijay Kumar Chakka
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: In this paper, a novel modified logistic function-based explicit model for PV modules is proposed for accurate modeling of PV modules with minimal current-voltage (I<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$-$</tex-math></inline-formula>V) curve data points. The proposed model requires only three I<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$-$</tex-math></inline-formula>V coordinates: open circuit voltage, short circuit current, and maximum power points provided by the manufacturer or obtained experimentally. The shape parameters of the proposed model are calculated using a newly derived simplified expression based on hyperbolic identities. Therefore, the proposed model significantly reduces the computational complexity compared to the conventional benchmarks, such as implicit single diode and double diode models of PV module. The proposed model is validated for various PV modules of different cell types of technologies under different environmental conditions in terms of % error in maximum power point. The method eliminates the complex calculations, dependence on several PV parameters and the laboratory-based analysis of solar modules.

title: A Non-Unit DC Line Protection Based on Extremum Time of Fault Forward Traveling Wave
authors: Tianshu Bi, Yang Dai, Ke Jia, Kunpeng Yao, Miao Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Delivery
abstract: Compared with the traditional derivative-based traveling wave (TW) protection, the non-unit TW protection based on fault TW waveform analysis can effectively enhance sensitivity when high-resistance faults occur. However, protection based on fault TW waveform analysis may operate incorrectly due to inaccurate characterization of the initial fault TW, as well as the influence of subsequent TWs under line-port faults and the fault resistance associated with external faults. To address this issue, a non-unit DC line protection based on the extremum time of forward TW is proposed. Specifically, the differences in initial fault TWs associated with different fault locations are fully considered, together with the influences of fault resistance and subsequent TWs on waveform characteristics. The distinctions among different fault locations are then captured through criteria based on the extremum time of the forward TW and the fault voltage amplitude. Therefore, the proposed protection can effectively detect internal line-port and remote faults. The detailed model of the modular multilevel converter-based high voltage direct current (MMC-HVDC) transmission system is built in Real Time Digital Simulator (RTDS), and the proposed protection algorithm is programmed in the DC protection device. Hardware-in-the-loop test and simulation results show that this protection method can effectively address the problem of protection refusal under line-port or remote fault, and can withstand 400Ω transition resistance and noise with a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of 30dB.

title: BiXFormer: A Robust Framework for Maximizing Modality Effectiveness in Multi-Modal Semantic Segmentation
authors: Jialei Chen, Xu Zheng, Danda Pani Paudel, Luc Van Gool, Hiroshi Murase, Daisuke Deguchi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Multi-modal perception is especially important in scenarios like autonomous driving, where single-modal inputs may fail under challenging conditions. Utilizing multi-modal data enhances scene understanding by providing complementary semantic and geometric information. Existing methods fuse features or distill knowledge from multiple modalities into a unified representation, improving robustness but restricting each modality's ability to fully leverage its strengths in different situations. We reformulate multi-modal semantic segmentation as a mask-level classification task and propose BiXFormer, which integrates Unified Modality Matching (UMM) and Cross Modality Alignment (CMA) to maximize modality effectiveness and handle missing modalities. Specifically, BiXFormer first categorizes multi-modal inputs into RGB non-RGB modality, e.g., depth, allowing separate processing for each. This design leverages the well-established pretraining for RGB, while addressing the relative lack of attention to non-RGB modalities. Then, we propose UMM, which includes Modality Agnostic Matching (MAM) and Complementary Matching (CM). MAM assigns labels to features from all modalities without considering modality differences, leveraging each modality's strengths. CM then reassigns unmatched labels to remaining unassigned features within their respective modalities, ensuring that each available modality contributes to the final prediction and mitigating the impact of missing modalities. Moreover, to further facilitate UMM, we introduce CMA, which enhances the weaker queries assigned in CM by aligning them with optimally matched queries from MAM. Experiments achieve great performance on many benchmarks and demonstrate the effectiveness of our method.

title: Multidimensional Analysis of the Diagnostic Performance of Machine Learning Models in Disease Classification: Implications for Clinical Decision Support Systems_supp1-3703248.docx
authors: Miroslaw Plaza
publish: N/A

title: Concept of Online In-Service Partial Discharge Echo Based Diagnostics for Motor Winding Insulation in Transportation Applications
authors: Marek Florkowski
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: Electric motors are fundamental components of the transportation sector. Hence, online diagnostics of these elements is of the highest priority from a safety and reliability standpoint. This paper presents an original concept of built-in motor winding insulation diagnostics approach, as an online and in-service method. The method is based on the accumulation of partial discharge echoes (PDEs) for trend analysis, anomaly detection, and other diagnostic evaluations. The methodology and switching sequence are embedded into the motor controller. There are various application scenarios considered, in which diagnostic cycles can be attributed to the motor start-up phase, normal operation, idle or no-load operation, as well as off-line diagnostics or material testing. The concept is presented on the permanent magnet brushless motor control sequence based on a 3-phase pulse width modulation (PWM). The PD-echo related signals are acquired synchronously during injected micro slots into the control sequence. The methodology can extract information online about the condition of the winding insulation. Operating in the voltageless part of a motor supply makes it more immune to excitation-driven disturbances. This article presents the results that were obtained on twisted pair specimens that were representative of electrical-machine insulation used in transportation applications. The aim of this paper was to demonstrate a novel concept based on PD echo for electrical insulation diagnostics. This approach may offer new possibilities for assessing electrical insulation in online, in-service diagnostic applications for electric motors.

title: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning-Based Cooperative Energy Efficiency-Thermal Safety-Battery Health Management for Distributed 4-wheel Independent Drive Electric Vehicles
authors: Jiankun Peng, Yunpeng Li, Tianyi Yang, Hongliang Wang, Changcheng Wu, Dawei Pi, Chunye Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: As electric vehicle architectures evolve toward distributed configurations, distributed four-wheel independent drive electric vehicles (4WID-EVs) have gained significant attention for independent wheel torque control. To enhance energy efficiency and mitigate health degradation of the battery and motor, it is necessary to adopt torque distribution strategies (TDS) and integrate thermal management systems (TMS) to regulate the temperatures of batteries and motors. Nevertheless, the vehicle longitudinal dynamics and the TMS are characterized by complex nonlinear couplings, which makes it difficult to achieve effective cooperative control through a single integrated strategy. To address this issue, a hierarchical control framework is proposed that combines the soft actor-critic (SAC) with the estimation of distribution algorithm. The upper layer optimizes torque distribution to improve energy efficiency, while the lower layer employs thermal management method to regulate temperatures. Simulation and hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves approximately 93.5% of the driving energy-saving performance of dynamic programming (DP), improves the accuracy of battery and motor temperature control, and effectively mitigates battery health degradation. Moreover, HIL results further validate that the hierarchical control framework provides favorable real-time performance and generalization capability, highlighting its strong potential for engineering applications.

title: Active Sensing and Deferred-Decision Trajectory Optimization for Robust Target Identification
authors: Farbod Siahkali, Mengxue Hou, Vijay Gupta
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: We study trajectory optimization in mobile sensing systems that must identify which member of a finite candidate set is the true target, while maintaining reachability to all potential candidate targets, under resource constraints. Deferred-Decision Trajectory Optimization (DDTO) addresses this setting by computing trajectories that reach individual targets but remain coincident for as long as possible before separating toward different targets. We propose Active-Sensing DDTO (AS-DDTO), which extends DDTO by adding a trajectory-dependent information-acquisition term to the planning objective. The resulting planner maintains reachability to candidate targets while biasing the coincident portion of the trajectories toward regions that enable earlier target identification. The framework supports Bayesian updates and conformal candidate-set updates for distance-dependent sensing. We derive a mixed-integer conic reformulation and provide guarantees on recursive feasibility, belief concentration, and fixed-time coverage for the raw conformal candidate set. Numerical simulations show improved target identification compared with standard DDTO under distance-dependent sensing uncertainty and limited sensing budget.

title: TFRL-MD: Transformer-enhanced Federated Reinforcement Learning for Intelligent Microservice Deployment and Resource Allocation
authors: Keli Liu, Jing Yang, Xiaoli Ruan, Yuling Chen, Xu Wang, Jianhong Cheng, Yulong Yang, Minyi Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing
abstract: Edge computing provides low-latency execution environments for delay-sensitive microservices by deploying computational resources at the network periphery. However, microservice deployment and resource allocation in geographically distributed, heterogeneous edge environments must simultaneously navigate challenges such as data privacy preservation, heterogeneous resource constraints, and dynamic workload fluctuations. Therefore, we propose TFRL-MD, a Transformer-enhanced federated reinforcement learning framework for privacy-preserving and intelligent microservice deployment and resource allocation. The framework models each edge node as an independent Actor-Critic agent to learn deployment policies via local training, enabling cross-node collaborative optimization without sharing raw environmental data. To improve the quality of knowledge fusion among heterogeneous edge nodes, we design a Transformer-based Embedding-Space Aggregation Mechanism (EASM) that leverages multi-head self-attention to adaptively fuse their intermediate-layer knowledge representations. Furthermore, a dual-channel hybrid objective training mechanism is constructed to synchronously inject the aggregated global representations into the optimization targets of both Actor and Critic networks as differentiable semantic embeddings, thereby smoothly guiding local gradient descent and eliminating the policy oscillations inherent in traditional parameter overwriting.Dynamic simulation experiments, conducted using the EUA dataset from the Australian Central Business District (CBD) region, demonstrate that TFRL-MD outperforms various baselines in both average response time and load balancing, achieving maximum improvements of 46.01% and 61.11%, respectively.

title: Multi-Channel Quasi Resonant Proportional Integral Observer for Output Current Ripple Attenuation of Full Bridge Buck Converter Fed Coreless DC Motor
authors: Xinghe Fu, Chenggang Jiang
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: In Full bridge Buck converter fed Coreless DC Motor (CDCM) system, the commutation effect of the motor brings output current ripple which degrades the operational performance. In this scenario, the commutation effect is an unmatched periodic disturbance whose suppression requires the compensation of itself and its derivatives under non-cascaded control architecture, which is challenging for traditional high order nonlinear disturbance observer (HONDOB) even with a large bandwidth. Therefore, a multi-channel quasi resonant proportional integral observer (MCQRPIO) is proposed in this article. One quasi-resonant controller (QRC) is embedded in each of the four state estimation channels of MCQRPIO, which brings extra regulation terms in the estimation error transfer functions of unmatched periodic disturbance and its derivatives. Through specific gain selection of the QRCs, the extra regulation terms can reduce the estimation error of unmatched periodic disturbance and its derivatives, thus more effectively attenuate the current ripple. Simulations and experiments are conducted to verify the effectiveness of current ripple attenuation of MCQRPIO, it was verified that MCQRPIO can attenuate the current ripple magnitude by about 40% and the magnitude of fundamental component by more than 50% over a wide range of speed.

title: SEN2NEON: Enabling Quantitative Benchmarking of Sentinel-2 Super-Resolution for all Multi-Spectral bands
authors: Simon Donike, Cesar Aybar, Julio Contreras, Luis Gómez–Chova
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: The super-resolution (SR) of Sentinel-2 (S2) imagery has been constrained by the absence of reliable high-resolution reference data for its 20- and 60-meter spectral bands. In this letter, we introduce SEN2NEON, a cross-sensor benchmark dataset that rigorously harmonizes high-resolution AVIRIS-NG hyperspectral imagery with real Sentinel-2 Level-2A reflectances through spectral response convolution and radiometric correction. SEN2NEON provides physically aligned low- and high-resolution (LR–HR) pairs for all Sentinel-2 bands (except the cirrus band) at the original S2 and 2.5m reference resolution, respectively. Compared to existing cross-sensor datasets, it offers a larger number of spectrally aligned bands, a finer effective reference resolution, and stronger LR–HR consistency. We quantify harmonization quality via a cross-dataset alignment analysis based on LR–HR resampling consistency metrics. By preserving the original Sentinel-2 observations as the LR input and deriving the HR reference via sensor-aware harmonization rather than synthetic degradation, SEN2NEON establishes a reproducible and physically grounded benchmark for multispectral Sentinel-2 super-resolution research. The sensor-aware harmonization strategy underlying SEN2NEON further provides a transferable and physically grounded framework for super-resolution benchmarking that can be extended to a broader range of multispectral satellite sensors.

title: Lightweight Image Forgery Detection via Tucker Decomposition for Efficient Edge Deployment
authors: Yongling Huang, Laurence T. Yang, Xianjun Deng, Debin Liu, Shuilong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract:
In recent years, the rapid advancement of deepfake generation techniques has escalated security risks, prompting the development of detection methods based on image domain, frequency domain, and pre-trained models. However, existing approaches predominantly prioritize cross-domain generalization, which has driven the adoption of increasingly large and complex neural networks. These models incur substantial parameter overhead and computational costs, hindering their deployment on resource-constrained edge devices. To address these deployment challenges, this paper proposes the integration of Tucker decomposition to construct lightweight forgery detection networks. We introduce a unified Tucker compression method that can be applied to mainstream architectures, and validate its effectiveness by compressing three state-of-the-art detectors across 24 diverse forgery datasets. Experimental results show that the proposed method substantially reduces model size and computational cost while preserving cross-dataset generalization.

In particular, the proposed PID-Tucker method attains performance comparable to the original model using only 0.17M parameters. It realizes an 88.03% reduction in parameters and an 87.4% decrease in FLOPs, with a negligible accuracy drop of only 0.2%.

title: Robust Lane Change Decision for Autonomous Vehicles: A Causal-Invariant Adversarial Reinforcement Learning Approach
authors: Yongjie Wang, Kaiyue Wang, Yuchen Niu, Zixuan Zhang, Qiong Li, Xinhua Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Despite the rapid development of autonomous driving technology, the lane-changing polices of autonomous vehicles still face two major challenges: perceptual uncertainty and vulnerability to adversarial attacks. Existing approaches often rely on empirical regularization or adversarial training to handle input perturbations, but they lack a systematic modeling of the causal relationship between perturbation mechanisms and policy deviations, making it difficult to fundamentally improve robustness. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel causal-invariant adversarial reinforcement learning (CIARL) approach to enhance the robustness and safety of autonomous vehicles under optimal observational perturbations. The approach primarily comprises two core modules: the attacker employs a closed-box perturbation generator based on Bayesian optimization to construct optimal adversarial perturbations in the feature representation mapping space; the defender incorporates a causal-invariant representation learning mechanism to mitigate attacks by reducing the influence of non-causal features. This mechanism constrains the impact of adversarial perturbations on policy and cost within a controllable range while maximizing rewards, thereby enabling robust policy learning. Finally, the proposed CIARL approach is evaluated in the SUMO simulator across complex highway and on-ramp scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms several state-of-the-art baselines in both lane-change robustness and collision safety, verifying the core role of the causal-invariant mechanism in adversarial robust policy modeling.

title: Regularization-Augmented Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Electromagnetic Inverse Scattering
authors: Zengkai Wu, Kaikun Niu, Sheng Sun, Ping Li
publish: IEEE Journal on Multiscale and Multiphysics Computational Techniques
abstract: This paper proposes a regularization-augmented physics-informed neural network (RAPINN) framework based on the volume integral equation (VIE) for electromagnetic inverse scattering problems. By incorporating the VIE, the scattered field is evaluated through the Green's function, allowing the neural networks to reconstruct the total field only inside the domain of interest and reducing the computational burden for far-field problems. To avoid repeated forward solves, the inverse problem is reformulated as an optimal-control-inspired physics-constrained optimization problem. For each inverse problem, a complex-valued field module and a real-valued contrast module are jointly optimized through the data-equation and state-equation residuals, replacing explicit matrix inversion with residual-based optimization. The resulting vanilla PINN framework achieves accuracy comparable to NN-based inversion with lower computational cost. To improve robustness in challenging cases, squared total-variation-like (squared TV) smoothness regularization and Tikhonov regularization are further incorporated into the PINN loss to form RAPINN. By introducing prior information on the scatterer, these explicit regularization terms complement the implicit constraints imposed by the physics residuals and help mitigate unstable reconstructions produced by the vanilla PINN in high-contrast and noisy scenarios. Numerical results show that RAPINN provides more stable and accurate reconstructions than conventional methods and vanilla PINN in the tested cases.

title: Efficient Data-driven Reference Governor Design for Safe Evasive Manoeuvring
authors: Petar Velchev, Alberto Bertipaglia, Felipe Santafe, Mohammad Khosravi, Barys Shyrokau
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: This paper presents a novel data-driven Reference Governor with Model Predictive Control, integrating local motion replanning and path following for collision avoidance. Employing a model-free Reference Governor, the proposed framework utilises system knowledge through Bayesian Optimisation to augment predetermined evasive trajectories, minimising pathfollowing errors and simultaneously ensuring obstacle safety margins. A single-track vehicle model in combination with a nonlinear tyre model is used to capture the vehicle's dynamics. The optimised control action is the vehicle steering angle, whilst the Reference Governor optimises parameters of a sigmoid reference signal to minimise the tracking error and guarantee safety with respect to obstacles in emergency manoeuvres. The proposed approach is evaluated on a single lane change using a highfidelity simulation environment, and its performance is compared to a baseline controller integrating path following and obstacle avoidance. The results demonstrate a 14% reduction in safety critical overshoot, maximising obstacle safety distance and a four times lower controller cycle time compared to the baseline. Furthermore, through a robustness analysis, it is demonstrated that the proposed approach is more robust towards model mismatches and perception-based errors, as seen by average 30% and 40% reductions in near-miss and collision rates.

title: DPL: Depth-only Perceptive Humanoid Locomotion via Realistic Depth Synthesis and Cross-Attention Terrain Reconstruction
authors: Jingkai Sun, Gang Han, Pihai Sun, Wen Zhao, Jiahang Cao, Jiaxu Wang, Qiang Zhang, Yijie Guo
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Recent advancements in legged robot perceptive locomotion have shown promising progress. However, terrain-aware humanoid locomotion remains largely constrained to two paradigms: depth image-based end-to-end learning and elevation map-based methods. The former suffers from limited training efficiency and a significant sim-to-real gap in depth perception, while the latter depends heavily on multiple vision sensors and localization systems, resulting in latency and reduced robustness. To overcome these challenges, we propose a novel framework that tightly integrates three key components: (1) Terrain-Aware Locomotion Policy with a Blind Backbone, which leverages pre-trained elevation map-based perception to guide reinforcement learning with minimal visual input; (2) Multi-Modality Cross-Attention Transformer, which reconstructs structured terrain representations from noisy depth images; (3) Realistic Depth Images Synthetic Method, which employs self-occlusion-aware ray casting and noise-aware modeling to synthesize realistic depth observations, achieving over 30% reduction in terrain reconstruction error. This combination enables efficient policy training with limited data and hardware resources, while preserving critical terrain features essential for generalization. We validate our framework on a full-sized humanoid robot, demonstrating agile and adaptive locomotion across diverse and challenging terrains.

title: Research on Graphite-CuCrZr Brazing Technology and Mechanical Properties of Base Material
authors: Hongqi Zhang, Hongbin Chen, Jinyu Zhao, Bofeng Wang, Cong Wang, Ning Tian
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: In high-energy beam injection, a CuCrZr scraper is used to remove the beam halo in the core area. The scraper will gradually be damaged under the long-term bombardment of the beam. Graphite can be welded on the CuCrZr scraper using active brazing filler to enhance corrosion and thermal resistance. This paper investigates the shear strength at the welded joint of graphite and CuCrZr, as well as the base material strength under multiple thermal cycles in different environments. Research indicates that graphite can be brazed to CuCrZr using AgCuTi active brazing filler metal. The use of an interlayer effectively increases the shear strength of the weld compared to no interlayer, with values of 34.9MPa and 30.2MPa respectively. Multiple thermal cycles barely affect the base material strength; however, the base material strength significantly decreases in a $150^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ testing environment compared to room temperature, with values of 270MPa and 226MPa respectively.

title: A Hybrid-Action PPO-Based Adversarial Algorithm for Fixed-Wing UAVs Under Radar Constraints
authors: Cheng Song, Jiajun Zhao, XiuWei Huang, Kai Zhang, Jie Tao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Autonomous decision-making and survivability in complex electromagnetic environments are critical for fixed-wing unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in dynamic confrontations. Based on a three-degree-of-freedom (3-DOF) kinematic model, this paper proposes a hybrid-action reinforcement learning framework for UAV confrontation and radar evasion. A physics-based probabilistic radar detection model is developed to construct a multi-radar joint threat field, enabling realistic electromagnetic environment modeling. To address the conflict between target tracking and threat avoidance, a hybrid action space combining discrete tactical decisions with continuous flight control is designed. The confrontation process is formulated as a Markov decision process incorporating radar information and solved using an improved hybrid-action proximal policy optimization algorithm. Simulation results show that the proposed method outperforms standard PPO in convergence speed and final performance, achieving an average reward improvement of about 33 % and exhibiting advanced tactical maneuvers and robust 3D obstacle avoidance capability.

title: Diagnosis and Data Restoration Method for Stochastic Signal Dropout in Electron Guns
authors: Hengxu Peng, Yulu Hu, Shilong Zhu, Jiaxiang Zhou, Xiaofang Zhu, Luanfeng Gao, Bin Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Precise measurement of electron beam crosssectional current density is critical for Vacuum Electronic Devices (VEDs). Practical experiments, however, often encounter stochastic signal dropout caused by signal link instability. This paper characterizes these anomalies as transient measurement voids through repetitive testing. A gradient-preserving cubic spline interpolation algorithm is proposed to accurately reconstruct missing data. Results demonstrate high-fidelity restoration ($\mathrm{R}^{2}=0.9780$, $\mathrm{MRE}=3.58 {\%}$), effectively recovering the Gaussian-like beam profile and enhancing diagnostic reliability without hardware modifications.

title: A Clustering Driven Prediction Method for On-orbit Operation of TWTAs
authors: Yiyao Qian, Rui Sun, Xixian Feng, Chunjiang Bai, Wenkai Deng, Xiaolin Jin, Tao Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper proposes an HDBSCAN-clusteringoptimized Informer method for operation state prediction of on-orbit telemetry data from traveling-wave tube amplifiers (TWTAs). By adaptively partitioning the data using HDBSCAN, modeling bias caused by feature distribution differences is alleviated. Experimental results demonstrate that the cluster-optimized input significantly improves the model’s performance in terms of mean absolute error (MAE) and mean squared error (MSE), with predicted curves closely matching the true values, providing effective support for on-orbit health prediction of TWTAs.
