title: Performance Review of More Than 400 Sheet Electron Beam mmWave TWTs
authors: Aaron Jensen, Scott Ballinger, Noah Glick, Max N. Mankin, Rich Kowalczyk, Diana Gamzina
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Manufacturing of millimeter wave and sub-THz TWTs has several challenges due to small feature sizes. The electron optics system, with a high-power density beam, introduces significant hurdles to mass manufacturing. We report how manufacturing of sheet electron beam technology, to include modern processes, and modern fabrication techniques, reduces vacuum electronics device manufacturing time and, as a result, cost. More than 400 mmWave sheet beam TWTs have been manufactured to date utilizing the Elve process. Simulation tools, fabrication philosophy, and test results are presented and reviewed.

title: Reduced-Order Extended State Observer-Based Sliding Mode Control for Loader Working Device
authors: Chuancheng Yao, Guanglei Zhao, Jiannan Chen
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: A novel control strategy is developed in this work to resolve the conflict between uncertain payload dynamics and model inaccuracy. It features a nonlinear integral sliding mode design, which is paired with a refined Reduced-Order Extended State Observer (RESO) to enhance robustness. Conventional Sliding Mode Control (SMC) architectures often struggle with inherent chattering issues. To overcome this limitation, the proposed framework integrates an innovative reaching law with a redesigned sliding surface. These modifications are proven to drastically reduce chattering intensity and optimize the system's transient behavior. The effectiveness of the proposed control strategy can be verified through Lyapunov theory and simulation results.

title: Design of a Terahertz Frequency-Multiplier Amplifier with Large Beam Tunnel Based on Groove Loaded Subwavelength Hole Array
authors: Youfeng Yang, Ping Zhang, Yuan Zheng, Shaomeng Wang, Zhanliang Wang, Yubin Gong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: To address the design challenges of electron optical systems (EOSs) in the terahertz (THz) wave band, a THz frequency-multiplier amplifier with a large electron beam tunnel, based on a groove-loaded subwavelength hole array (GLSHA) structure, is proposed. When the beam tunnel diameter is larger than the hole width, with an input power of 1 W at 330 GHz, the proposed amplifier can achieve an output power of 1.69 W at 660 GHz.

title: Efficient & Lightweight Classification of Rotor Bar Faults in Induction Motors by Convolutional and Spiking Neural Networks
authors: Haihang Xia, Xinyu Zhao, Zihao Song, Yuqin Zhao, Lazaros S. Sofikitis, Aristeidis Karnezis, Georgios Tsialiamanis, Lyudmila Mihaylova, Tiantai Deng, Panagiotis A. Panagiotou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: Fault detection and diagnostics for electrical machines have attracted significant research interests over the last two decades. At the same time, recent advances in artificial intelligence and smart technologies for diagnostics are utilised in tandem with machine learning for purposes of condition monitoring. However, most existing methods in this direction rely on resource-intensive large-scale networks and often require additional data preprocessing, such as the extraction of characteristic features in the frequency domain. This work proposes a lightweight framework incorporating an emerging spiking neural network (SNN) and an optimised lightweight convolutional neural network (CNN) for the classification of induction motors suffering from rotor electrical faults, notably broken rotor bar faults, using only raw time-series of sensor signals. The proposed methods are evaluated in several bar fault scenarios and loading levels. The data are acquired from 2D FEA simulation of two induction motors with the same power rating but different numbers of rotor bars, and the results are validated by experimental measurements. The proposed frameworks are used to achieve a reliable diagnostic outcome regardless of the machine load. This includes the no-load case and relies solely on non-intrusive monitoring with minimally processed stator current and stray flux signals. Moreover, this is the first application of a SNN architecture for rotor fault diagnostics, while the proposed lightweight CNN significantly reduces the network scale and resource consumption compared to existing methods.

title: Efficient, Robust, and Anti-Collusion Fingerprinting of Image Diffusion Models
authors: Jianwei Fei, Yunshu Dai, Zhihua Xia, Xiaochun Cao, Jiantao Zhou, Alessandro Piva, Benedetta Tondi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Model fingerprinting, embedding user-specific identifiers (fingerprints) into generated outputs, has recently emerged as a popular solution to protect the intellectual property rights (IPR) of generative text-to-image (T2I) models and prevent unauthorized redistribution. In this work, we reveal a previously unexplored systematic vulnerability in existing generative model fingerprinting methods: they lack robustness against collusion attacks, where multiple attackers combine their models to remove or obscure the fingerprints. To address this issue, we take the first step towards a robust fingerprinting method for T2I models with anti-collusion capabilities. The proposed method encodes strings of bits, namely fingerprints, into the coefficients of a personalized normalization module (PNM) incorporated into T2I models, so that fingerprints can be reliably recovered from any generated image. To defend against collusion attacks and prevent unauthorized model redistribution, we introduce an anti-collusion mechanism based on lossless function-invariant parameter transformations. This mechanism significantly degrades the image generation quality of colluded models, making them effectively unusable. Moreover, our method allows developers to efficiently create multiple copies of fingerprinted T2I models by reparameterizing the PNM without the need for retraining. We also introduce a worst-case optimization strategy to improve robustness against model-level attacks. Our experiments demonstrate that the proposed method achieves high fidelity and robustness across multiple T2I image generation and editing tasks, with fingerprint extraction accuracy exceeding 99.5%. Compared with existing methods, our method demonstrates, for the first time, a notable proactive robustness to collusion attack by significantly increasing the FID of colluded models.

title: Preparation and Properties of Diamond/SiC Microwave Attenuation Ceramics
authors: Kai Zhao, Fan Yang, Yingjian Cao, Ran Mo, Guo Liu, Yong Luo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: To address the demand for high-thermal-conductivity attenuation ceramics in high-performance vacuum electronic devices, Diamond/SiC composites were fabricated and characterized. The thermophysical and dielectric properties of the composites with varying diamond contents were systematically investigated to determine the influence of diamond loading on material performance. Experimental results show that the room-temperature thermal conductivity of the samples reaches approximately $380 \mathrm{~W} /(\mathrm{m} \cdot \mathrm{K})$, and remains as high as $200 \mathrm{~W} /(\mathrm{m} \cdot \mathrm{K})$ at $500^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$, significantly outperforming the conventional $\mathrm{BeO}-\mathrm{TiO}_{2}$ ceramics. At a frequency of 90 GHz, the real part of the complex permittivity is approximately 20 with a dielectric loss of 0.55, demonstrating excellent electromagnetic attenuation capabilities. This study provides a promising material solution for thermal management and parasitic oscillation suppression in high-power microwave applications.

title: Event-Triggered Optimized Tracking Control for Fully Actuated Single-Link Flexible Joint Manipulator Systems via Reinforcement Learning
authors: Xueling Wan, Ruopeng Li, Qingrong Liu, Xianfu Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the event-triggered optimized tracking control problem for a single-link flexible joint manipulator system. It adopts a reinforcement learning backstepping design method and a fully actuated system method for achieving the optimized control. Notably, the proposed scheme ensures that all signals within the closed-loop system are bounded, and the tracking error can converge to a bounded vicinity of the origin. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed event-triggered optimized control scheme is illustrated through a simulation example.

title: A Unified Fault-Tolerant Control Strategy Based on Asymmetric Modulation for IPOS DAB Converters Under Open-Circuit Faults
authors: Hanhui Che, Xiangyang Xing, Yan Li
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In high-power DC-DC conversion applications, the input-parallel output-series (IPOS) architecture is widely adopted for its modular scalability and high-voltage output capability. However, open-circuit faults (OCFs) in the constituent dual active bridge (DAB) modules pose severe risks of transformer core saturation and inter-module output voltage imbalance, critically compromising system reliability. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a unified fault-tolerant control strategy based on cross-side asymmetric modulation. By actively reconstructing the voltage duty cycle of the faulted module and introducing a compensatory inner phase-shift angle, volt-second balance is re-established at the modulation level, eliminating the DC bias in the inductor current and preventing transformer core saturation. Building on this, a multi-stage adaptive voltage balancing strategy is developed by exploiting the inherent voltage coupling of the IPOS architecture. Through dynamic reconfiguration of the outer-loop voltage reference-guided by real-time operating-state monitoring-the strategy achieves full-range adaptive coverage across soft-start, steady-state, and active fault-tolerant derating modes. Simulation results demonstrate millisecond-level suppression of the inductor current DC bias and well-balanced output voltages under both primary- and secondary-side OCF conditions, significantly enhancing system reliability and fault-tolerant capability.

title: Beyond Screen Time: Inferring Everyday Life Context from Diverse Smartphone Data
authors: Gujun Chen, Xinyao Yang, Yutong Han, Shuning Zhang, Yan Kong, Dafei Yin, Hu Sun, Xin Yi, Hewu Li

title: Experimental Validation of a TWT-Driven X-ray Source for Communications
authors: Brandon E.J. Cortez, Halil Topözlü, Melisa Bozdemir, John Lambrecht, John H. Booske, Nader Behdad
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Traditional RF telemetry faces critical limitations in hypersonic environments and secure space applications, including signal blackout and susceptibility to interception. X-ray Communication (XCOM) overcomes these challenges by enabling plasma sheath penetration during hypersonic flight and reentry, while also offering ultra-narrow beams for high-bandwidth, low-probability-of-intercept (LPI) deep-space links. We experimentally validated an XCOM transmitter that uses a helix traveling wave tube (TWT) to generate X-ray pulses from a titanium target. We selected Ti to produce high-intensity characteristic X-rays at accessible energies (${\lt}10 \mathrm{keV}$). We first measured 50 $\mu$ S X-ray pulses to verify the link. Simulations of the aluminumfiltered spectra supported these measurements, confirming that the signals matched the physics of X-ray transmission. We also drove the TWT with a 500 MHz modulation signal. By comparing the signal response under thin ($17 \mu \mathrm{~m}$) and thick $(1.8 \mathrm{~mm})$ filters, we identified electromagnetic interference (EMI) that currently obscures the high-frequency X-ray signal. We present our methodology for isolating these signals. In the coming months, we anticipate presenting data from our exploration into high-frequency modulation of these X-ray signals.

title: Attitude-Orbit Coupled Fault-Tolerant Control for Spacecraft Formation Based on Fault Estimation
authors: Annan Wang, Jihe Wang, Yu-Yao Wu, Xin Long, Hui-Jie Sun
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the attitude-orbit coupled fault tolerant control problem for a dual-quaternion-based spacecraft formation subject to actuator faults. A fault observer is designed to estimate actuator faults, and its stability is rigorously proven. Subsequently, based on the designed observer, an attitude-orbit coupled fault tolerant controller is developed. Moreover, the stability of the overall closed-loop formation system under the proposed controller is strictly established. Finally, numerical simulations are performed to validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed control scheme.

title: LiDAR-Guided Learned Image Compression for Machine Vision_supp1-3703332.pdf
authors: Alessandro Gnutti
publish: N/A
abstract: Learned image compression is increasingly being used not only for human viewing but also to support machine understanding. This has led to the development of Coding for Machines (CfM), which focuses on compressing visual data for downstream tasks. While some methods improve CfM by leveraging semantic cues or recognition outputs at the encoder side, they often assume access to high-level information that is not available in practice and require costly computation to extract. In this paper, we propose a learned image coding framework for machine vision that uses LiDAR depth as a low-cost and widely available auxiliary signal. Instead of re-training the entire codec, we introduce lightweight adapters at the encoder side, keeping the base model fixed. We also avoid full image reconstruction by transforming the latent representation through a bridge module that connects directly to pruned recognition networks. Experiments across various tasks, including standard recognition models and Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), show that our method achieves strong rate-accuracy performance, while lowering computational cost at the decoder side.

title: Guest Editorial In-Memory Computing (IMC): From Technology to Applications
authors: Alberto Bosio, Deliang Fan, Xueqing Li, Leticia Maria Bolzani Poehls

title: Training-Free PCB Anomaly Detection with Adaptive Difference Guidance in Multimodal LLMs
authors: Huiqi Chen, Yanxia Liu, Kunting Luo, Zihe Yu, Huawei Wu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Printed Circuit Board (PCB) anomaly detection faces significant challenges due to the scarcity of defect samples, high annotation costs, and the limited generalization of models trained exclusively on target domain data. Existing unsupervised methods require extensive training on normal samples and suffer from poor transferability, while few-shot methods remain sensitive to prompt design and rely on additional trainable modules. To overcome these challenges, this paper proposes DiffGuide, a novel training-free PCB anomaly detection framework based on Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). DiffGuide utilizes PCB Gerber design files as precise reference images, eliminating the need for any training or extra modules. To guide the MLLM’s attention without manual prompt engineering, we design an adaptive difference-guided prompting mechanism that dynamically identifies potential defect regions via semantic similarity comparison and injects this information into natural language prompts. Furthermore, we introduce a confidence ensemble strategy with consistent augmentation inference, which enhances decision robustness by aggregating model outputs across multiple augmented views of the input pair. Comprehensive experiments on a real-world industrial PCB dataset show that DiffGuide achieves state-of-the-art performance, with 93.24% AUROC and 86.00% AP, without any training. We demonstrate its superior accuracy and robustness by comparing it with existing unsupervised and few-shot methods.

title: The Fusion Augmentation Method for Underwater Acoustic Target Recognition
authors: Ming Chen, Feng Wang, Yang ze Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: In the field of underwater acoustic target recognition, scarce acoustic samples can cause models to overfit. To address this issue, we employ a combination of traditional data augmentation methods, the feature-based SpecAugment method, and the model-based conditional deep convolutional generative adversarial network (cDCGAN) data augmentation method to mitigate overfitting. For the feature-based SpecAugment method, we replace frequency deletion with filter-based bandwidth reduction. This approach not only preserves frequency information in the spectrogram but also better simulates the acoustic environment. For the model-based cDCGAN method, we draw inspiration from ensemble learning to enhance the quality of generated data and improve training stability. Finally, the augmented datasets are fed into an enhanced MobileViT network for recognition. Experimental results demonstrate that this method achieves recognition rates of 96.63% and 95.22% on the ShipsEar and DeepShip datasets respectively, outperforming both classical and state-of-the-art models. This approach provides valuable insights for applications such as speech recognition, audio processing, and high-precision sonar detection.

title: Fuzzy-Guided Integration with Cognitive Heuristics for Entity Matching under Uncertainty
authors: Xin Liu, Xiaojun Li, Junping Yao, Qinggang Fan, Hao Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: Real-world entity matching (EM) decisions are often made under uncertainty due to incomplete information, fine-grained ambiguity, and unseen entities. However, most existing methods still treat EM as a deterministic binary classification problem, assuming sufficient and reliable evidence, which is often violated in practice. In this paper, we reformulate EM as a decision-making problem under uncertainty induced by bounded rationality, and propose FiHEM, a fuzzy-guided framework that integrates cognitive heuristic principles with holistic semantic reasoning for EM decision-making under uncertainty. Fuzzy neural networks perform semantic fusion, combining holistic semantics with attribute-specific representations, enabling informative attributes to be interpreted within a broader multi-attribute context rather than in isolation. Additionally, heuristic decisions based on representativeness and frequency cues are explicitly designed and adaptively integrated with the core decision via fuzzy residual fusion, allowing effective refinement when the core prediction is affected by uncertainty. Extensive experiments on the DeepMatcher and WDC benchmarks demonstrate that FiHEM consistently outperforms recent state-of-the-art (SOTA) EM methods and representative large language model (LLM)-based approaches, with particularly pronounced gains under varying dataset scales, corner cases, and higher proportions of unseen entities. To the best of our knowledge, this work represents the first attempt to systematically formulate EM as a decision-making problem under uncertainty and address it through fuzzy-guided holistic fusion and heuristic decision integration.

title: Aging-Informed Dynamic Graph Network for Aero-Engine RUL Prediction
authors: Zeeshan Abbas, Hao Wang, Nasir Ali, Xiaobao Song, Wenming Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Remaining useful life (RUL) prediction for safety-critical systems, such as aero-engines, is a fundamental challenge in prognostics and health management. While existing deep learning methods have achieved success in modeling spatiotemporal patterns, they often overlook the physical essence of system degradation: the evolution of intersensor dependencies is intrinsically driven by the aging progression. To address this, we propose aging-informed dynamic graph network for RUL prediction (AIDGN-RUL), which formulates RUL prediction as an aging-guided dynamic graph construction problem. Specifically, the model derives an aging representation from the operational cycle count and integrates it with multivariate sensor features to generate adaptive graph structures that evolve with system aging. A graph convolutional architecture is then employed to propagate information over aging-guided graph structures to obtain refined sensor representations for RUL prediction. Extensive experiments on the CMAPSS and N-CMAPSS datasets demonstrate that AIDGN-RUL significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, achieving a maximum improvement of 27.83% in prognostic score and 15.94% in root mean square error (RMSE). Furthermore, the model exhibits superior robustness to sensor noise and maintains high computational efficiency, highlighting its potential for real-world industrial deployment.

title: Discovery of Thermionic Electrons Diffraction in ReB6 Cathodes
authors: Victor Katsap

title: Occlusion and Noise Robust Video Action Recognition Based on Enhanced Spatiotemporal Graph Convolutional Networks
authors: Liting Lei, Gejun Xu, Lanmei Qian, Xiujing Li
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Robust skeleton-based action recognition in real-world environments remains a critical challenge due to occlusion, joint detection noise, and temporal instability. This paper proposes an enhanced spatiotemporal graph convolutional network (E-STGCN) that improves robustness through adaptive graph learning, feature-level denoising, and spatiotemporal attention integration. The model introduces a confidence-aware adaptive adjacency matrix that dynamically adjusts joint connectivity, reducing the influence of unreliable or occluded nodes. A feature enhancement module combining temporal smoothing and anomaly correction effectively suppresses coordinate noise, achieving an average reduction of 39.29% in trajectory error. Meanwhile, a dual attention mechanism selectively emphasizes discriminative joints and key temporal frames, improving feature representation. Extensive experiments on NTU RGB+D 60 and NTU RGB+D 120 datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves Top-1 accuracies of 91.73% and 88.42%, respectively, maintaining competitive performance under standard conditions. More importantly, under perturbation settings, the model outperforms the baseline ST-GCN by 5.27% under Gaussian noise and 6.85% under joint occlusion, while limiting performance degradation to less than 4.30% across increasing noise levels. Compared with existing approaches such as 2s-AGCN and MS-G3D, the proposed method exhibits superior robustness with improved stability and lower variance. These results confirm that the proposed framework effectively enhances both accuracy and reliability, making it suitable for practical deployment in complex and dynamic environments.

title: SAGNet: Spherical Anchor-Based Graph Neural Networks for Raw Point Cloud Detection
authors: Chenhao Ma, Xu Zhang, Zhiyun Lin
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper introduces SAGNet, a novel framework for efficient 3D object detection directly on raw point clouds. To address the inherent sparsity and irregularity of point cloud data, SAGNet bypasses the traditional conversion into dense, regular grids-a process that often incurs information loss and computational inefficiency. Instead, our model leverages anisotropic spherical anchors to construct local graphs directly from the point cloud. By exploiting the uniform angular distribution of LiDAR sensors, this approach preserves the original spatial topology and significantly enhances feature extraction, particularly for distant objects. Furthermore, SAGNet integrates an RGB semantic gating mechanism to adaptively fuse cross-modal information, enabling the selective enhancement of foreground features for improved detection accuracy and efficiency. Comprehensive experiments on the nuScenes dataset demonstrate that SAGNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, marking a significant advancement in 3D object detection.

title: Distributed Load Frequency Control in Multi-Area Power Systems With Q-Learning Enhanced Adaptive Event-Triggered Control
authors: Xiangkun Wang, Kaibo Shi, Yanlin Li, Xiao Cai, Ming Zhu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper addresses the distributed load frequency control (LFC) problem of multi-area power systems by developing a Q-learning-enhanced adaptive event-triggered mechanism (AETM). First, a distributed LFC framework is established, where each control area exchanges only local information with its neighbors, thereby improving scalability and robustness. Second, an adaptive event-triggered mechanism is introduced to reduce excessive data transmissions by dynamically adjusting the triggering threshold according to the system error level. Third, Q-learning is incorporated to optimize the threshold scaling factor, so that the trade-off between regulation performance and communication efficiency can be improved online. Finally, Lyapunov-based analysis is employed to guarantee asymptotic stability and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> performance of the closed-loop system. Simulation results on a multi-area power system demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Fas-Based Composite Fault-Tolerant Control for Robotic Manipulators with a Fifth-Order Disturbance Observer
authors: Mingyu Dai, Changhui Jiang, Miao Cai
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In this paper, a fault-tolerant control strategy is proposed for robotic manipulators in the presence of unknown dynamics, friction effects, external disturbances, actuator faults, and physical constraints. Unlike conventional approaches based on first-order state-space models, which may require precise dynamic information and weaken the physical interpretability of the Euler-Lagrange structure, a composite control strategy is developed within the High-Order Fully Actuated System (FAS) framework. A fifth-order disturbance observer (5ODO) is incorporated in the inner loop to estimate lumped uncertainties with an FAS-based controller designed in the outer loop to ensure closed-loop stabilization performance. In addition, a constrainthandling mechanism is introduced to guarantee safe operation under actuator saturation and physical limitations. By integrating model identification with disturbance estimation, the proposed approach achieves effective suppression of faults and disturbances. Simulation results illustrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Mixup-Augmented Teacher-Guided Knowledge Distillation for Noise-Robust Keyword Spotting
authors: Ju-Seok Seong, Joon-Hyuk Chang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: Keyword spotting (KWS) is a key component of voice interfaces and is typically deployed on resource-constrained devices. To improve the generalization performance of small-footprint models, knowledge distillation is widely adopted to transfer dark knowledge from an over-parameterized teacher to a compact student model. In addition, mixup-based data augmentation has been widely applied in KWS to enhance robustness to input perturbations by exposing models to diverse training inputs. However, despite their potential effectiveness, the systematic integration of these two techniques has not been sufficiently explored in small-footprint KWS. We propose MixTKD (Mixup-Augmented Teacher-Guided Knowledge Distillation), a practical and efficient distillation framework that leverages supervision from a teacher model to effectively integrate mixup augmentation for KWS. Specifically, teacher predictions serve as explicit guidance to formulate the cross-entropy and distillation losses. For the cross-entropy term, conventional mixup relies on linearly interpolated one-hot labels, which may introduce a discrepancy with the teacher output distribution used in the distillation term. To address this issue, MixTKD introduces teacher-guided soft targets that are aligned with the teacher outputs, providing semantically coherent supervision for the student. For the distillation term, in contrast to conventional decoupled knowledge distillation, we perform a teacher-guided decomposition of the Kullback-Leibler divergence into target and non-target components based on teacher predictions, improving distillation effectiveness under mixed inputs. To further stabilize distillation, we propose teacher-confidence-based curriculum learning and a runner-up masking strategy. Experimental results show that the proposed method consistently outperforms conventional distillation methods across the Google Speech Commands and Multilingual Spoken Words Corpus datasets, without increasing model size or inference latency. Furthermore, the method demonstrates robustness on noisy datasets and remains effective under limited distillation data.

title: Filter-Induced Distortion Analysis of GALMS-Based Self-Interference Cancellation
authors: Keda Xu, Anh Tuyen Le, Xiaojing Huang
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: This letter investigates the impact of filter-induced distortion on generalized analog least mean square (GALMS)-based self-interference cancellation (SIC) in in-band full-duplex systems. Considering practical analog filter nonidealities, including amplitude attenuation and frequency-dependent group-delay mismatch, we derive closed-form expressions for the resulting excess modeling error relative to a delay-matched tapped-delay-line (TDL) reference. The analysis reveals that such distortions degrade channel–based projection efficiency and lead to an irreducible modeling error that dominates the steady-state residual interference (RSI) power. In contrast, the excess meansquare error (EMSE) is relatively insensitive to the specific filter realization. Simulation results further confirm the analysis’s accuracy and show that filter-induced distortions fundamentally limit the achievable RSI suppression.

title: Single Channel Receiver Architecture for Auto-calibrated Passive Reflectometry Using Wideband Signals
authors: Serni Ribó, Xavier Canalda, Estel Cardellach, Weiqiang Li, Antonio Rius
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: A compact single-channel receiver architecture for passive reflectometry using signals of opportunity is presented. Its aim is to provide a simple receiver architecture that can be employed for surface and root-zone soil moisture measurements. The main characteristic of this architecture is that direct and reflected signals are collected with a single antenna and down-conversion chain, and that this has the advantage that the system is auto-calibrated, as direct and reflected signals are affected identically by the instrument (antenna pattern needs to be characterized). The use of a single antenna and receiver channels allows to reduce the mass and power consumption, which is especially important for small-format platforms such as drones and nano-satellites. In addition, the processing method provides means to reduce the effect of the receiver noise on the measurements. The paper presents the instrumental concept, simulation results and a laboratory verification of the proposed architecture and methods.

title: Robust Unknown Input Observer based Estimation and Mitigation against Sensor Spoofing Attacks in Cyber-Physical Microgrids
authors: Yuqi Wang, Mengxiang Liu, Milijana Odavic, Xin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: Microgrids are increasingly dependent on real-time sensing and network communication for the monitoring and control of distributed energy resources (DERs), exposing them to numerous cyber attacks such as sensor spoofing. In such attacks, additive biases are injected into sensor measurements without directly spoofing the communication network and control logic. Conventional observer-based bias estimation methods face several challenges. They typically rely on accurate system models, and their estimation performance deteriorates in the presence of system uncertainties, including parameter uncertainties, process and measurement noise, and unknown inputs. These factors make sensor spoofing-induced biases difficult to distinguish from normal state variations, causing interaction between sensor biases and system uncertainties and leading to degraded control performance in practical microgrid operation. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a Robust Unknown Input Observer (RUIO) for the accurate estimation of sensor spoofing-induced biases in the presence of various system uncertainties. The sensor bias is modeled as an augmented state, and the observer gain is synthesized using Linear Matrix Inequality (LMI) optimization under an H∞ performance criterion to ensure robustness against system uncertainties. Given the structural observability limitation of the Unknown Input Observer (UIO) formulation, which prevents simultaneous estimation of independent current and voltage sensor biases, an event-triggered bias estimation and mitigation framework is designed to counter any single sensor spoofing attack. The dual-RUIO scheme is switched adaptively based on the current-sharing error after mitigation, with an appropriate holding interval in place to ensure stable operation. The effectiveness of the proposed RUIO-based framework is validated through extensive experiments on a cyber-physical microgrid testbed. Comparative evaluations against conventional state estimation methods demonstrate improved estimation accuracy and mitigation performance.

title: Leader-Following Consensus Control for High-Order Fully Actuated Nonlinear Mass Under Communication Link Faults
authors: Xiaosong Wang, Liuliu Zhang, Changchun Hua, Songsong Liu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the leader-following consensus control problem for the high-order fully actuated(HOFA) multiagent systems (MASs) under communication link faults(CLFs). Firstly, by the fully actuated method, a fault-tolerant distributed observer is proposed for each follower to estimate the leader's state information. Unlike the previous studies on CLFs, this paper relaxes the assumption by allowing the derivative of the CLFs weights to be unbounded. Then, with the help of the proposed observer, a fully actuated controller is proposed for each follower, by which the follower can asymptotically track the leader's state. Ultimately, the performance of the control protocol is verified via simulation.

title: Design Methodology of a Novel Slow-Wave Structure for 0.34 THz Traveling-Wave Tube
authors: Zugen Guo, Xinhua Yu, Qixiang Zhao, Yiying Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: In this article, a novel slow wave structure (SWS), Weibull bending waveguide (WBWG), has been proposed for terahertz traveling wave tube amplifiers (TWTAs). The outer outline of WBWG is described by a set of Weibull functions, enabling flexible adjustment of the slow-wave structural shape during the design phase of TWTAs. Particle-in-cell simulations are performed at 20 kV with a beam current of $30~\text{m A}$, and the WBWG TWTA is predicted to have a maximum output power of 18 W and a saturated gain of $37.8~\text{d B}$ at the operating frequency 336 GHz. The $3~\text{d B}$ bandwidth of saturated power is predicted to be 12 GHz, ranging from 329 to 341 GHz. The beam-wave interaction analysis demonstrates that the WBWGSWS with favorable transmission characteristics can indeed enhance output power and efficiency, making it a preferred choice for interaction circuits in THz TWTAs.

title: Prescribed-Time Stabilizing Controller Design for a Class of Uncertain Non-Affine FASs
authors: Xue Zhang, Guang-Ren Duan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Based on the fully actuated system (FAS) approach, this paper focuses on the prescribed-time control problem of a class of non-affine FASs with nonlinear coupled uncertainties. For FASs satisfying the linear growth condition, the function permitting stability within a prescribed time is constructed through a class of parameterized Lyapunov equations, and a sequential time-varying state feedback controller is proposed to ensure prescribed-time stabilization of the closed-loop system. With the help of a class of Lyapunov-like functions and the piecewise analysis strategy, the theoretical verification of system stability is completed. Simulation results on a variable-stiffness flexible-joint robot fully demonstrate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Spatiotemporal Control of Electron Emission Using Nanoscale Photoemitters with Integrated Nanophotonics and Plasmonics
authors: Siddharth Karkare
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Precise control of electron emission in space and time is essential for next-generation electron sources used in accelerators, electron microscopy, and vacuum nanoelectronics. Conventional photoemission sources achieve transverse beam shaping by shaping the drive laser at the cathode surface, which limits spatial control to the optical diffraction limit and temporal control to the laser pulse duration. Here, we report nanoscale photoemitters that overcome these limitations by integrating nanophotonic and plasmonic structures directly at the photocathode, enabling correlated spatio-temporal control of electron emission at the nanometer-femtosecond scale. By engineering nanoscale photoemission contrast through material patterning, near-field confinement, plasmonic field enhancement, and integrated nanophotonic components, we generate electron emission from sub-micron regions with arbitrary transverse profiles. These approaches can enable exquisite control of both the spatial extent and temporal dynamics of emission simultaneously. We further show that these nanoscale photoemitters can achieve beam brightness exceeding that of conventional field-emission tips. Together, these results establish a new paradigm for spatiotemporal control of electron sources, opening new capabilities for high-brightness electron sources in advanced vacuum electronic, microscopy, and accelerator applications.

title: Co-Optimized Semiverse Solutions for Under-Etched Cavities in Silicon Photonics
authors: Jiayu Sun, Chris Caigan Chen, Yanlong Guo, Yushan Chi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: Silicon photonics is advancing rapidly due to high-speed, low-power optical communication needs in data centers and artificial intelligence (AI). Fiber-to-chip coupling loss is a major bottleneck for integrated circuits, especially in high-speed and coherent systems. To address this, cavities under edge couplers are designed to reduce optical loss but face process challenges such as limited depth and depth uniformity, high lateral etching, and residue-free requirements. This study investigates an extremely lateral etching process to minimize cavity depth while achieving a higher lateral profile with residue-free. Lam Semiverse Solutions, including SEMulator3D and AI/Machine learning (ML)-assisted recipe optimization platforms, are used to simulate and optimize etch parameters, improving process design and robustness. The single-facet coupling loss is 1.4 dB in the O-band (1310 nm) and supports scalable manufacturing of silicon photonic circuits.

title: Adaptive Dual-Bandwidth Cooperative Control Solution for Optimizing Transient Response of Energy Storage Converters
authors: Hao Zhang, Yiyina Teng, Qingyao Xu, Ning Wang, Xiaoqiang Guo, Changchun Hua
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Single-phase energy storage converters face a fundamental tradeoff between rapid dynamic responsiveness and steady-state current quality, especially under load disturbances and low-voltage conditions (e.g., 110V@60Hz). To overcome this problem, this paper proposes an Adaptive Dual-Bandwidth Cooperative Control (ADCC) framework for front-stage rectifiers, progressively developed in two stages. First, Proposed Solution I establishes an adaptive bandwidth switching and integrator-reset mechanism to significantly accelerate transient bus-voltage recovery. To resolve the ensuing false state judgments caused by the second-harmonic component, Proposed Solution II refines the framework via ripple extraction and compensation, ensuring optimal steady-state performance. Comprehensive comparisons with state-of-the-art methods confirm the superiority of the ADCC framework. Experimental results at 220V@50Hz show Dc-bus voltage fluctuations are reduced to -16V/+9V, with settling times shortened to 0.18s/0.227s. At 110V@60Hz, the framework successfully suppresses bus-voltage collapse, restoring the full 1000W theoretical load capacity (up from 800W) while maintaining a low grid-side current THD of 2.70%. Overall, the ADCC provides a highly cost-effective, software-driven solution that effectively breaks the dynamic/steady-state tradeoff in consumer electronics applications.

title: End-to-End Autoencoder Framework for Misalignment-Resilient OAM-based OWC Systems
authors: Rizwana Ahmad, Arttu Nieminen, Hossein Safi, Iman Tavakkolnia, Humeyra Caglayan, Harald Haas
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Orbital angular momentum (OAM) enables multiplexed optical wireless communication (OWC) with high spectral efficiency but suffers from severe degradation under misalignment-induced power loss and intermodal crosstalk. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first work that embeds experimentally validated, first-principles misalignment-dependent modal coupling with finite-aperture effects directly into a differentiable end-to-end autoencoder framework for BER-driven optimization under IM/DD constraints. Simulation results show that the proposed AE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">min-max</sub> model outperforms singular value decomposition and turbo-coded systems, achieving signal-to-noise ratio gains of 12.5 dB and 8.5 dB, respectively.

title: Graph Neural Koopman Operator: A Structure-Aware Lifting Framework for Free-Floating Space Robot Control
authors: Siming Zhang, Ning Li, Bicheng Cai, Xueqin Chen, Yong Zhao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a structure-aware system identification and control framework for free-floating space robots by integrating graph neural networks with Koopman operator theory. The FFSR is first represented as a graph, where the spacecraft base and manipulator links are modeled as nodes and their physical connections are encoded as edges. A residual graph neural network is then employed to learn a nonlinear lifting function that maps the original system state into a higher-dimensional Koopman space, explicitly preserving the topological structure of the robot. The resulting lifted linear model is integrated into a model predictive control scheme to achieve closed-loop trajectory tracking. Simulation results on a dual-arm FFSR with six joints demonstrate that the proposed GNN-Koopman model achieves lower prediction errors compared to conventional EDMDbased Koopman methods. When applied to MPC, the GNN-Koopman controller achieves tracking performance comparable to physics-based MPC with accurate physical modeling, while maintaining stable closed-loop behavior without requiring an explicit analytical model, validating the effectiveness of incorporating structural information into data-driven modeling for space robotic systems.

title: Tracking Control of Fully-Constrained Hovercraft Via Asymmetric Integral Barrier Lyapunov Function
authors: Haiyan Gao, Yuqiang Wu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the trajectory tracking control problem of underactuated hovercraft subject to system uncertainties and external environmental disturbances. The proposed framework integrates prescribed performance control (PPC) with a logarithmic barrier Lyapunov function (LBLF). Furthermore, a finite-time disturbance observer (FTDO) is introduced to estimate and compensate for the lumped uncertainties in real time. Under the proposed control scheme, all signals in the closed-loop system remain bounded and the trajectory tracking error achieves finite-time convergence to the prescribed performance bounds, resulting in uniformly bounded stable tracking.

title: Trajectory Tracking Control for 4WID Electric Vehicles: An Optimal Control Approach Based on HOFA Theory
authors: Kaiyang Feng, Boqiang Zhang, Guoqiang Chen, Hebo Yu, Tenglong Huang, Haohan Zhao, Xin Ren, Yahui Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Vehicle stability remains one of the core subjects in chassis control. This paper investigates the trajectory tracking precision and vehicle stability of four-wheel-independent-drive (4WID) electric vehicles equipped with active rear steering (ARS) systems under extreme conditions, such as complex split-mu roads. Through feedforward-preview trajectory planning, the driver's operations are transformed into short-term desired trajectories. A recursive least squares algorithm with a forgetting factor is employed to estimate tire cornering stiffness parameters online. Furthermore, the high-order fully actuated (HOFA) method is applied to eliminate the complex nonlinear characteristics of the system, constructing a new linear time-invariant closed-loop system for optimal control. Co-simulations verify the practical effectiveness of the designed controller.

title: Topology-Buffer Crossover of Critical Demand in Buffered Stochastic Supply DAGs
authors: Xin Chen, Kexin Liu, Haotian Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Characterizing the operational limits of stochastic supply networks is important for industrial resilience. We study the critical-demand threshold in assembly-type directed acyclic graphs with stochastic capacities and finite inventory buffers. Our analysis shows that network topology amplifies demand through path-based replication, which we formalize by the path multiplicity. This leads to two topology-compressed reference rates, the low-buffer bottleneck proxy and the topology-conditioned mean-capacity ceiling. Finite buffering is then shown to induce a smooth one-parameter crossover between these two endpoint regimes. Numerical evidence supports the proposed crossover law and reveals a universal scaling collapse, providing a low-dimensional predictive description of sustainability in complex buffered dependency systems.

title: Hybrid Predictive Control for Multi-Axis Robotic Systems with Improved Robustness Based on Fully Actuated System Theory
authors: Hao Fan, Qiyang Pan, Chaochen Gu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a hybrid predictive control framework for multi-axis robotic systems with improved robustness based on fully actuated system (FAS) theory. First, the high-order nonlinear dynamics of the multi-axis robot are transformed into a high-order fully actuated (HOFA) canonical form, which is globally equivalent to a decoupled linear chain of integrators with disturbance. Second, an extended state observer (ESO) is introduced to estimate the lumped disturbance in real-time. Third, based on this standardized linear model, an explicit model predictive control (EMPC) strategy is synthesized. By solving the multi-parametric quadratic programming (MPQP) problem offline, the optimization process is converted into a lookup table of piecewise affine (PWA) functions. The hybrid architecture combines the analytical global decoupling of FAS with the efficient constraint handling of EMPC. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior trajectory tracking accuracy and disturbance rejection.

title: Research on an Improved PID Control Algorithm for Large Time-Delay Systems
authors: Yu Huang, Yun An, Zhun Li
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Aiming at the application limitations of the traditional PID control in nonlinear large time-delay systems, this paper proposes an improved PID control algorithm. The algorithm innovatively introduces five adjustable parameters, and achieves adaptive dynamic correction of the traditional PID setpoint based on the deviation value and the dynamic variation trend of the controlled variable. Its core innovation lies in fully considering the coupling effect of the dynamic changes of the actual controlled value itself on the control system, thereby fundamentally solving the problems of slow response, weak anti-interference capability and severe oscillation in large time-delay systems. The proposed improved algorithm has the advantages of clear concept, easy understanding, and engineering promotion. It can smoothly switch with the traditional PID, has simple programming implementation, and no significant increase in the computational load of the controller, thus possessing good engineering practicality. The practical application results in the temperature control system of the plate heat exchanger in the automated workshop plant show that the improved algorithm can quickly stabilize the controlled variable within the target range, significantly enhance the anti-interference performance, and remarkably improve the system control accuracy and operation stability.

title: Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Graph Network with Tensor Enhancement for Edge AI-Empowered Traffic Flow Forecasting
authors: Honglu Zhao, Lei Yang, Laurence T. Yang, Linsong Shan, Yixuan Geng, Zecan Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: High-accuracy and low-latency traffic forecasting is a fundamental challenge in enabling edge AI-empowered traffic management systems for intelligent, green, and sustainable transportation. Although substantial advances have been made in recent studies, current prediction models struggle to balance predictive accuracy and computational efficiency, limiting their suitability for resource-constrained edge devices that require realtime responsiveness. To tackle these challenges, we propose a multi-scale temporal dynamic graph network with adaptive structure (MTDGN-AS). The proposed model is built upon three core components. First, we develop a dual-path adaptive dynamic graph generator that combines data-driven correlations with history-guided prototype matching. And a differentiable sparsity gate generates interpretable dynamic adjacency matrices to capture non-local and time-varying spatial dependencies while significantly reducing computational overhead for edge deployment. Second, for temporal modeling, parallel multi-scale temporal convolutions are employed to capture temporal patterns at multiple resolutions, where a lightweight gating network enables adaptive fusion and temporal attention enhances long-range dependency modeling. Finally, we design a parameter-efficient tensor-train-based output layer that factorizes high-dimensional weight tensors into a chain of low-rank cores. This design is well aligned with the resource constraints of edge devices, while the implicit low-rank regularization introduced by the decomposition structure enhances generalization and robustness in edge environments. Experimental evaluations on public traffic datasets indicate that our model surpasses state-of-the-art methods in prediction accuracy, while substantially reducing parameters and computational complexity, offering an effective solution for building accurate, real-time, reliable edge-intelligent green transportation systems.

title: Coupled Extended State Observer-Based Fully Actuated Robust Control of a Stewart Platform
authors: Yizhe Jiang, Ziao Guan, Yi Zhou, Rundong Wang, Jiyuan Kuang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: The 6-DOF Stewart platform is a highly coupled nonlinear system, making high-precision trajectory tracking challenging due to dynamic uncertainties and external disturbances. Conventional Active Disturbance Rejection Control often treats each degree of freedom (DOF) independently, leading to significant estimation errors in asymmetric axes (e.g., Y-axis) due to strong inertial coupling. This paper proposes a high-precision control scheme based on a Fully Coupled Extended State Observer (FC-ESO) and Exact Force Allocation. Unlike traditional methods, the proposed FC-ESO utilizes the full mass matrix to decouple the inertial terms, allowing the observer to focus solely on unmodeled dynamics and disturbances. Furthermore, an exact inverse solution is applied for force allocation to eliminate geometric mapping errors. Stability analysis based on Lyapunov theory is provided to guarantee the convergence of the closed-loop system. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves micron-level tracking accuracy under complex multi-frequency composite trajectories and effectively suppresses coupled disturbances while maintaining smooth actuator forces.

title: Dynamic Mesh Based on Delaunay Graph Mapping for Deforming Cathodes in Electron Guns
authors: Zexin Yang, Junhui Yin, Longwei Deng, Xiaofang Zhu, Xing Li, Luanfeng Gao, Yulu Hu, Bin Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: In high-power vacuum electronic devices, the electron gun undergoes thermal deformation during simulation. When the mesh cannot be updated with boundary deformation, local elements are prone to quality degradation such as stretching and twisting, and in severe cases, even flipping or crossing. Therefore, dynamic mesh generation is necessary for accurate simulation. This paper proposes a dynamic mesh generation method based on Delaunay diagram mapping. This method first constructs a coarse background Delaunay mesh around the cathode, establishing a mapping relationship between computational nodes and background mesh nodes. The structural displacement of the electron gun is applied to the background mesh, and then interpolated and applied to the computational mesh, thereby updating node positions without changing the topology. This method is applied to a traveling wave tube electron gun, and results show that it can maintain element validity and mesh quality in the emission region even with cathode deformation.

title: Global Connectivity Maintenance-Based Formation Optimization Control for Multi-Agent Systems with Input Saturation
authors: Liu Yang, Zhixin Yan, Xinchen Ma
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a cooperative control strategy for multi-robot systems subject to input saturation constrains while executing multi-task formation control in complex environments, with a focus on global connectivity preservation and shortest-path tracking. A distributed Fiedler eigenvalue estimation algorithm is developed, enabling each robot to evaluate and maintain the algebraic connectivity of the system in real time using only local interactions, thereby preserving global connectivity of the communication topology. Under input saturation constrains, a shortest-path optimization method based on global connectivity preservation is proposed, achieving optimal dynamic target tracking and formation reconfiguration. Numerical simulations are conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed strategy in terms of constraint satisfaction and task execution.

title: Multimode Operation of the THz Clinotrons
authors: Oleksiy Kuleshov, Sergey Vlasenko, Yurii Kovshov, Sergey Kishko, Sergiy Steshenko, Eduard Khutoryan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A three-dimensional simulation model for investigating multimode operation of a clinotron in the THz frequency range is presented and discussed. The simulation results are validated by comparison with experimental data obtained from a continuous-wave clinotron with a modified interaction cavity operating in the $280-330~\mathrm{GHz}$ frequency range. Both numerical and experimental studies demonstrate that the excitation of higher-order surface modes and their transformation into modes of an oversized cavity have a significant influence on the output characteristics of the 0.33-THz clinotron with the modified cavity. A theoretical analysis of the three-dimensional electromagnetic system of the $0.33-\mathrm{THz}$ clinotron reveals mode interaction and the excitation of hybrid bulk–surface modes. These modes enable an operating regime for THz oscillators that combines the advantages of devices based on either surface-wave or bulk-mode mechanisms.

title: Enhancing Contrastive Learning on Learnable Graph Augmentation
authors: Yu Xu, Guixiang Zhu, Huijia Li, Jia Wu, Jie Cao, Darko Vukovic
publish: IEEE Transactions on Big Data
abstract: Graph Contrastive Learning (GCL) has become an effective paradigm in graph representation learning by establishing contrastive relationships between nodes. Existing methods typically generate positive samples through manually designed graph data augmentation and obtain negative samples via random sampling. Suboptimal augmentation can corrupt the original graph topology, while false-negative samples introduce noise that degrades node representation quality. To address these issues, this paper proposes a novel graph contrastive representation learning framework called Enhancing Contrastive Learning with Augmentation (ECLA). First, we adopt a learnable augmentation module that generates adaptive augmented views through learnable edge weight adjustments. Second, we propose a joint metric method that integrates graph structural similarity and node feature similarity to model the deep semantic relationships between nodes, and based on this, we construct the distribution of positive and negative samples. Furthermore, we introduce a dual-weight contrastive loss function that enhances the contrastive learning intensity of semantically similar nodes through positive sample weights, while suppressing the negative impact of potential false-negative nodes through negative sample weights. ECLA jointly optimizes graph augmentation and representation learning in an end-to-end manner. Evaluations across diverse datasets show that ECLA consistently surpasses both leading graph contrastive methods and semi-supervised GNN baselines on node classification under severely limited label conditions.

title: Thermal Performance of Additively Manufactured Hollow-Conductor Windings in Electrical Machines for Propulsion
authors: Rafal Wrobel, Zaynah Ahmad
publish: IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification
abstract: High-specific-power electrical machines for transportation applications require effective thermal management to enable elevated power density and efficiency. Hollow-conductor windings offer direct internal cooling, reducing temperature rise and increasing allowable current density. This paper investigates the thermal performance of copper hollow-conductor windings fabricated using additive manufacturing (AM) and evaluated via reduced-complexity motorette demonstrators. The windings were produced using a filament-copper AM process, enabling continuous hollow channels along the full conductor length. Experiments demonstrate winding-to-coolant heat transfer exceeding 2000 W/m2K, corresponding to a 0.7l/min corresponding to a 37 × increase in allowable winding power loss relative to a baseline design employing conventional round copper conductors with liquid-cooled housing. Complementary theoretical analysis of complete machine variants shows that hollow-conductor cooling can significantly enhance machine operating envelope and efficiency, extending high-efficiency regions and torque-speed capability. The findings illustrate that direct cooling of hollow-conductor windings, enabled by AM, provides a viable route to higher specific output and improved thermal robustness in propulsion-relevant electrical machines. Practical considerations for integrating hollow-conductor windings into full machine assemblies are also discussed, including manufacturing, assembly, and thermal design implications.

title: S2R: From Screening to Recognition, Event-Driven Perception of Railway Defects in Low-Power Systems
authors: Mingxu Li, Donghai Zhai, Shichun Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Automated, real-time inspection of railway infrastructure is critical for ensuring the safety of modern transportation systems. Deploying such perception systems on edge devices, however, is constrained by stringent power limitations. While Spiking Neural Networks (SNNs) are well-suited for low-power sensing, their non-differentiable spiking dynamics complicate direct training. In contrast, Artificial Neural Networks (ANNs) achieve easier optimization convergence but incur sustained high-power computational requirements, making them ill-suited for continuous and off-grid monitoring. Thus, integrating a shallow SNN for simple yet high-frequency tasks with a deep ANN for more complex tasks emerges as a promising solution. In this work, we propose an event-driven perception framework for railway surface defect segmentation to enable real-time railway defect inspection in edge deployments. The SNN serves as a region proposal module to detect potential defects, and only the defective segments are forwarded to the ANN for finer segmentation. This design enables our model to achieve high performance on sparse target inspection tasks with minimal energy consumption. We also collected and annotated a dataset named DeTian Railway Surface Defect (DTRSD), comprising 1,126 real-world railway defect images captured under diverse natural lighting conditions. These images capture anomalies, such as dust deposits and rust stains, offering a realistic environment for evaluating segmentation methods. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves competitive accuracy while reducing energy consumption relative to existing approaches. The code and dataset will be publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/lidadadaxu/HybridRSD</uri>

title: MCR-VQGAN: A Scalable and Cost-Effective Tau PET Synthesis Approach for Alzheimer’s Disease Imaging
authors: Jin Young Kim, Jeremy Hudson, Jeongchul Kim, Qing Lyu, Christopher T. Whitlow
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Tau positron emission tomography (PET) is a critical diagnostic modality for Alzheimer’s disease (AD), but its widespread clinical adoption is hindered by radiation exposure, limited availability, high clinical workload, and substantial financial costs. To address these limitations, we propose the Multi-scale CBAM Residual Vector Quantized Generative Adversarial Network (MCR-VQGAN) to synthesize high-fidelity tau PET images from structural T1-weighted MRI. MCR-VQGAN advances the standard VQGAN architecture through three enhancements: multi-scale convolutions, ResNet blocks, and Convolutional Block Attention Modules (CBAM), which collectively improve the capture of local and global features. Using 222 paired T1-weighted MRI and tau PET scans from the ADNI database, we trained and compared MCR-VQGAN against cGAN, WGAN-GP, CycleGAN, and baseline VQGAN. MCR-VQGAN achieved superior image synthesis performance across all metrics (MSE = 0.0056±0.0061, PSNR = 30.65±4.47 dB, SSIM = 0.9263±0.0469). A CNN-based AD classifier trained on real tau PET achieved comparable accuracy on real (63.64%) and synthetic (65.91%) images, indicating that diagnostically relevant features are preserved. Regional SUVR analysis across Braak-defined ROIs further confirmed strong agreement between real and synthetic tau PET (Pearson r = 0.78–0.88; ICC = 0.71–0.84), with the strongest agreement in Braak V/VI (ICC = 0.838). Together, these results demonstrate that MCR-VQGAN offers a reliable and scalable surrogate for conventional tau PET imaging, potentially improving the accessibility of tau biomarkers for AD research and clinical workflows.

title: Multi-Scale Dynamic Receptive Field for High-Resolution Satellite Stereo Matching
authors: Wei Qin, Xin Huang, Yifan Liao, Lai Wei, Yang Yu, Wenxiang Gan, Qi Chen, Pengjie Tao
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: High-resolution satellite stereo matching is fundamental for 3D mapping. While current deep learning models achieve impressive macroscopic geometric accuracy, they inherently rely on convolutions with static receptive fields. This rigidity struggles with extreme scale variations across complex landscapes, often causing pervasive localized matching errors, such as edge-fattening artifacts at object boundaries. Furthermore, applying standard dynamic convolutions to address this within massive 4D cost volumes incurs prohibitive memory overhead. To address this trade-off, we propose a parameter-efficient, scale-adaptive cost aggregation strategy. We introduce a Multi-scale Dynamic Receptive Field (MDRF) mechanism that decouples the learned base operator from the dilation-dependent sampling support. By reusing a single shared kernel across multiple dilations via a content-aware attention gate, the network adaptively weights multi-scale context responses. This plug-and-play module is parameter-efficient, with memory and throughput explicitly reported in the evaluated configurations. Evaluated on the WHU-Stereo and US3D benchmarks, our approach consistently lowers the outlier rate, achieving absolute D1-error reductions of 0.43 and 0.49 percentage points, respectively, compared with the baseline. Error-map analysis shows that MDRF reduces localized boundary artifacts, supporting a measured accuracy-efficiency trade-off for practical remote sensing applications.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal

title: DepthPlay-Agent: Agentic Learning and Fusion for Depth Estimation
authors: Fahad Majeed, Maria Nazir, Zhihe Lu, Marco Agus, Jens Schneider
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Signal Processing
abstract: Monocular depth estimation in complex, dynamic environments remains challenging due to rapid object motion, texture repetitions, occlusions, and strong geometric constraints inherent in structured scenes. To address these challenges, we propose DepthPlay-Agent (DEAN), an agentic multi-model fusion framework that builds upon SOTA depth estimator backbones such as UNet++, HybridDepth, DINOv3, and ZoeDepth under a learnable Mixture-of-Experts controller. This controller embeds scene context through transformers and uses task priors to dynamically assign pe-pixel fusion weights. A domain-aware refinement module further enforces geometric consistency using planar and semantic segmentation cues. Beyond static fusion, DEAN introduces an agentic inference layer that dynamically regulates expert contributions and refinement strategies, enabling adaptive and interpretable decision-making. Experiments across four benchmarks (SoccerNet-Depth, KITTI, MPI-Sintel, NYU Depth V2) demonstrate that DEAN consistently achieves SOTA performance, improving AbsRel by up to 5% and reducing SILog by 8% over strong baselines. By coupling multi-modal intelligence with structured geometric reasoning, DEAN establishes a new paradigm for adaptive, context-aware depth estimation in dynamic real-world domains.

title: Dual-Helix Microwave Sensor for Oil-Water Two-Phase Flow Measurement Based on Vision Transformer with Dynamic Attention
authors: Ruiqi Wang, Hanqing Chen, Yanpeng Hao, Zhiqiang Zhao, Meng Wu, Weidong Dang, Zhongke Gao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: In the petroleum industry, accurate measurement of high-water-content oil-water two-phase flow parameters remains challenging due to the complex interfacial dynamics and flow pattern variations. This paper presents a novel dual-helix microwave sensor system integrated with an advanced deep learning framework for precise parameter measurement. We independently develop a dual-helix microwave sensor measurement system and conduct comprehensive vertically upward oil-water two-phase flow experiments. To address the limitations of existing methods in capturing complex temporal-spatial patterns, we propose a Vision Transformer with Dynamic Attention mechanism (ViT-DA) for soft measurement modeling. The dynamic attention module adaptively focuses on discriminative features across different temporal scales, while the multi-head self-attention mechanism captures global dependencies in microwave attenuation signals. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed ViT-DA model achieves recognition accuracies of 94.87% for total flow rate and 96.42% for water content, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art methods. The proposed approach provides an effective solution for real-time monitoring and parameter estimation in complex industrial oil-water two-phase flow systems.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Vision Displacement Measurement Using a Coded Virtual Sensor Array for Outdoor Drift Mitigation
authors: Shuchen Bai, Tao Cao, Baojian Ren, Zhengyang Zhang, Xinglin Zhang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Long-range vision-based displacement measurement is attractive for outdoor structural monitoring, yet its accuracy is often degraded by global imaging drift induced by camera self-heating, wind-driven micro-motions, and slow thermal variations. This paper presents a coded virtual sensor array (CVSA)-assisted framework to suppress global drift while preserving true structural dynamics. A key practical challenge is that the near-field CVSA light-emitting diodes (LEDs) appear as defocused blobs when the camera is focused on the far-field target plane. To address this, we propose a defocus-aware parametric blob localization method that estimates subpixel LED centers and an associated covariance matrix as a confidence descriptor. The covariance information is incorporated into an uncertainty-weighted similarity alignment model to estimate the global drift transform from multiple observations, followed by temporal drift filtering to enforce slow drift evolution and mitigate intermittent outliers and over-compensation. The drift-compensated target displacement is obtained by inverse warping, and uncertainty propagation can be performed for reliability interpretation. Simulation studies validate localization accuracy and uncertainty calibration over a wide range of defocus levels. Outdoor experiments demonstrate effective reduction of low-frequency drift and long-term trends in displacement time histories while preserving dominant vibration components in the frequency domain, outperforming translation-only and unweighted similarity baselines in robustness and stability.

title: Path Control Barrier Functions for Spatio-Temporal Constraint Enforcement with Performance Guarantees
authors: José P. Carvalho, A. Pedro Aguiar, Karl H. Johansson
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: We introduce Path Control Barrier Functions (PCBF), which enforce spatio-temporal state constraints in path-following tasks while guaranteeing safety, liveness, and performance under input constraints. Unlike conventional Control Barrier Functions (CBF), PCBFs parameterize constraints along a path and treat the rate of progression as a controllable variable. This additional degree of freedom relaxes strict temporal requirements and enables the controller to adapt task execution to the system’s dynamics, actuation limits, and disturbances. We demonstrate that PCBFs yield locally Lipschitz continuous controllers that ensure forward invariance of a controllable time-varying set. Leveraging this structure, we derive sufficient conditions for the feasibility of a PCBF, based on the validity of a static CBF. Finally, we demonstrate that formulating path following tasks via PCBFs guarantees liveness and yields explicit upper bounds on task completion times, which we validate through a numerical example.

title: A Bibliometric Analysis of Ambient Assisted Living Systems：Context, Trends, and Future Directions
authors: Zhou Fang, Xiaochen Fang, Hui Zeng, Jinwei Zhu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Fueled by the global demographic shift towards aging societies, Ambient Assisted Living research has undergone a profound transformation. Against this backdrop, this study conducts a systematic bibliometric and visual analysis of the AAL research field. Using CiteSpace and VOSviewer, we examined 493 core publications from the Web of Science database (2009–2025), aiming to map the research landscape, track thematic shifts, and identify emerging directions. The findings reveal a clear evolutionary trajectory: from early foundational technology construction, to mid-term integration of the Internet of Things and artificial intelligence, and more recently toward a growing emphasis on user experience, ethical considerations, and sustainable business models. The analysis identifies that state-of-the-art AAL systems are evolving with three defining characteristics: unobtrusive sensing, embodied interaction, and predictive decision-making. The analysis further highlights deep learning–based human activity recognition as a leading technological frontier. At the same time, the focus of AAL research is shifting from a pursuit of technical performance toward prioritizing user trust and fostering an integrated ecosystem. Finally, the paper outlines the emergence of AI-powered AAL systems, which embody these three characteristics, and emphasizes the need for continued innovation across theoretical, methodological, and practical dimensions to advance toward a human-centered, intelligent eldercare ecosystem.

title: A Modal Decoupling-Based Skyhook Control Strategy for Vehicle Vertical Vibration Using Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Jibin Ding, Rui Bao, Peng Wang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a novel active suspension control strategy based on modal decoupling and skyhook damping to improve vehicle ride comfort. A seven-degree-of-freedom (7-DOF) vertical vibration model is established, capturing heave, pitch, and roll motions of the vehicle body together with vertical motions of four unsprung masses. To overcome the coupling effects among different modes in conventional skyhook control, a modal decoupling approach is employed: the system dynamics are transformed into independent modal coordinates using the mass-normalized mode shape matrix obtained from the eigenvalue problem of the conservative system. Skyhook damping is then designed separately for the heave, pitch, and roll modes, and the resulting control forces are mapped back to the four actuators via the pseudo-inverse of the modal participation matrix. The optimal skyhook damping coefficients for both the conventional (single-coefficient) and the proposed modal decoupling approaches are determined by particle swarm optimization (PSO) with a weighted root-mean-square (RMS) objective function that penalizes body heave displacement, pitch angle, and roll angle. Simulation results on a random road profile show that the proposed modal decoupling skyhook control significantly outperforms both the passive suspension and the conventional single-coefficient skyhook control, achieving reductions in heave, pitch, and roll RMS responses by 7.9%, 74.1%, and 70.6%, respectively, compared to the conventional skyhook method. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of mode-selective damping allocation in enhancing multi-directional ride comfort.

title: SA-TSDTP: A State-Aware Two-Stage Framework for Logistics Drone Trajectory Prediction
authors: Chuanqi Ma, Lingshu Zhong, Huasa Zhu, Mingyang Pei, Ming Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The high flexibility and uncertainty of the flight trajectory are important factors affecting the operational safety of logistics drones. Reliable flight trajectory prediction is the core technology to solve this problem. In order to solve the multivariate time series prediction task of short-time flight trajectory prediction, this work proposes a state-aware two-stage prediction model. In the first stage, logistics drone trajectories are segmented into three flight states based on geometric characteristics and altitude change rates. In the second stage, a multi-expert fusion module incorporating flight state information is introduced to capture the underlying motion patterns of drones and enhance prediction accuracy. The proposed prediction model is evaluated using trajectory data collected from multiple real logistics drones in Shenzhen, China. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach exhibits strong potential and consistently outperforms baseline methods across multiple datasets and evaluation metrics. These findings provide valuable technical support for the future development of safe urban air traffic operations and smart cities.

title: Effective Reinforcement Learning for Remote Sensing Vision-Language Models via Supervised Knowledge Injection
authors: Zilun Zhang, Peng Liu, Haozhan Shen, Tiancheng Zhao, Zhaojun Liu, Jianwei Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated impressive capabilities in general-domain visual understanding, their performance in remote sensing tasks remains limited due to domain discrepancies and the scarcity of remote sensing data in typical vision-language pretraining corpora. Recent advances show that reinforcement learning (RL) can enhance VLMs’ reasoning capabilities by guiding models to perform intermediate “thought” steps before answering. However, applying RL directly to remote sensing scenarios is often ineffective, as VLMs lack fundamental domain knowledge to begin with. To address this, we propose a novel paradigm that first equips VLMs with remote sensing knowledge in remote sensing visual grounding task through supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on a curated dataset of CoT samples specific to remote sensing. We then conduct RL to further strengthen the learned capabilities. Experiments on the in-domain dataset show that our RL-enhanced model outperforms its SFT-only counterpart. Furthermore, evaluations on the out-of-domain dataset demon-strate that our method fosters better generalization in remote sensing reasoning. Our approach offers a promising direction for adapting general-purpose VLMs to domain-specific visual understanding tasks.
