title: Design and Optimization of the Electron Gun for a 263 GHz Gyro-TWT
authors: Zhi Yi, Taotao Mao, Tao Song, Chenghui Zhu, Yuxuan Chai, Kaichun Zhang, Zhenhua Wu, Wei Wang, Diwei Liu, Chen Zhang, Xu Qi, Jiao Jiao, Na Yao, Yanyu Wei, Yubin Gong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: In this paper, the design and optimization of a double-anode magnetron injection gun (MIG) for a 263 GHz TE01-mode gyrotron traveling-wave tube (gyro-TWT) are presented, with the objective of meeting the requirements for high-power terahertz sources. Simulation results indicate that stable operation is achieved with a beam voltage of 50 kV and a current of 3 A. A high-quality electron beam is generated, exhibiting a pitch factor of $\mathbf{1. 1}$ and a transverse velocity spread of about $2 \%$.

title: Consensus of Fractional-Order Matrix-Weighted Systems with Sampled Position Data
authors: Weiling Wang, Xiaoqiang He, Wenfeng Jin, Yanyan Ye
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper studies the consensus problem of fractional-order matrix-weighted systems (FOMWSs), where the couplings among agents are described by positive definite or positive semi-definite matrices. A distributed control protocol based on sampled position data is proposed, allowing each agent to update its state using discrete-time positional measurements. For undirected matrix-weighted networks, necessary and sufficient conditions ensuring consensus are established. The analysis reveals that the feasibility of consensus is influenced by the sampling period, the fractional order, the control gains, and the spectral characteristics of the Laplacian matrix. In particular, for undirected networks, the consensus property is closely associated with the null space of the Laplacian. Numerical simulations are finally performed to demonstrate the theoretical results.

title: Neural-Network-Based Control with Integral Barrier Lyapunov Functions for Nonlinear Active Suspension Systems with Actuator Faults and State Constraints
authors: Yuxuan Fan, Menghua Zhang, Zhengsheng Chen, Weizhong Chen, Haohan Zhao, Yahui Zhang, Tenglong Huang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the adaptive control of a nonlinear active suspension system with actuator faults, external disturbances, and state constraints. First, considering the effects of nonlinear spring force, piecewise damping characteristics, and actuator faults, a nonlinear quarter-car suspension model is established. To ensure constraint satisfaction and improve robustness, an adaptive control scheme based on an integral barrier Lyapunov function, a neural network, and a disturbance observer is proposed. The proposed design strictly enforces the preset state constraints, and it is proven that all closed-loop signals are uniformly ultimately bounded. Simulation results show that, compared with passive suspension and traditional BLFbased control methods, the proposed method provides better vibration damping, lower vehicle acceleration, and smoother control performance.

title: Upcoming Conferences
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Robotics & Automation Magazine

title: Cooperative Stabilization for High-Order Fully Actuated Switched Systems With Completely Uncontrollable Subsystems
authors: Zekun Yang, Guangren Duan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Recently, the high-order fully actuated system (HOFAS) approach has been established as a powerful framework in control theory, yet its application has primarily focused on pure dynamical systems. In this paper, a novel single-input high-order fully actuated switched systems model is established, extending the HOFAS approach from pure dynamical systems to hybrid systems. Then, a cooperative stabilization method based on the dwell time is developed for high-order fully actuated switched systems including completely uncontrollable subsystems. The proposed controllers guarantee the global asymptotic convergence of the high-order fully actuated switched system. Moreover, a new effective restriction on the switching signal based on the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varpi$</tex> frequency of activation of subsystems is proposed, which provides a refined condition for characterizing permissible switching signals. Finally, the effectiveness of the novel criterion is demonstrated by a well-studied numerical example.

title: Trajectory Tracking Control for Underactuated Autonomous Vehicles on Rough Terrain: A Fully Actuated Approach
authors: Xulei Wang, Li Li, Qing Geng, Yipeng Cao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In this paper, the trajectory tracking control is investigated for underactuated autonomous vehicles operating on rough terrain. The dynamics model of autonomous vehicles on rough terrain is a typical nonlinear underactuated system, which poses significant challenges in handling complex dynamic couplings and disturbances. To address these challenges, a trajectory tracking control strategy based on a fully actuated system approach is proposed in this paper. Specifically, the underactuated dynamics model of the autonomous vehicle is converted into a high-order fully actuated (HOFA) system framework, and a disturbance observer is introduced to estimate disturbance. The HOFA-based controller compensates for the estimated disturbance, thereby ensuring control smoothness and improving tracking accuracy. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively deal with rough terrain challenges, with the maximum lateral and heading errors limited to 0.2686 m and 0.0748 rad, respectively.

title: Global Prescribed Performance Control for Systems with Input Quantization Based on Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Zhijie Li, Wei Liu, Tianyou Xu, Jinkai Zhao, Jiaqiang Chen, Jian Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper presents a global preset performance control scheme for nonlinear systems with input quantization, based on the high-order fully actuated (HOFA) system. By introducing normalization functions, barrier functions, and time-varying scaling transformations, we construct a novel prescribed performance control framework that alleviates the constraints of initial conditions. Additionally, we design a low-complexity controller based on the fully actuated system approach, ensuring that all closed-loop signals remain bounded. Finally, the feasibility of the proposed method is verified through simulations.

title: Study of Mode Conversion in Pillbox Windows Based on 3D Full-Wave Simulations
authors: Duo Xu, Shaomeng Wang, Zhanliang Wang, Zhigang Lu, Huarong Gong, Zhaoyun Duan, Yubin Gong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper propose an approach to quantitatively analyze the mode conversion route in pillbox windows based on 3-dimensional (3D) full-wave simulator. 3 pillbox windows, operating in $\mathrm{Ka}-, \mathrm{G}-$, and $\sim 650-\mathrm{GHz}$ bands, respectively, were simulated using CST microwave studio (MWS) and analyzed using the proposed approach.

title: Performance of Planar GaN-Metal Nanoscale Air-Channel Diodes Based on Oblique Deposition
authors: Chang Su, Ruihan Huang, Junxiang Yang, Yuting Zhou, Feiliang Chen, Mo Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: The nanoscale air-channel devices combine the advantages of traditional vacuum tubes with those of solid-state devices. Our study presents a planar GaN-metal nanoscale air-channel diode (NACD) fabricated using a cost-effective oblique deposition technique. By utilizing heavily doped n-type GaN as the emitter and a metal layer as the collector, a precise air-channel length of approximately 47 nm is achieved without high-precision photolithography. The device demonstrates excellent electrical performance with a low turn-on voltage of 1.33 V and a high output current of 100 μA at 3 V. Theoretical fitting confirms that the dominant electron transport mechanism is Fowler-Nordheim (FN) tunneling.

title: Reactive-Compensation-Based Input Matching for a 0.34-THz 9-Gap EIK Using a Rectangular-Coaxial Loaded Aperture
authors: Wanli Shi, Guoxin Ren, Haiying Yuan, Shilong Zhu, Luanfeng Gao, Junhui Yin, Xiaofang Zhu, Bin Li, Yulu Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: In a $0.34-\mathrm{THz}$ nine-gap extended-interaction klystron (EIK) input cavity, the waveguide–coupling–cavity discontinuity can produce strong reactive mismatch, leading to high port reflection and inefficient field buildup. We propose a rectangular-coaxial loaded coupling aperture for impedance matching: a metallic rectangular inner conductor is inserted into a conventional directcoupling aperture without altering the outer boundary, forming a short rectangular-coaxial section that provides tunable susceptance for port-impedance shaping. This compact scheme avoids complex outer-geometry redesign and offers an independent, continuous tuning knob. Three-dimensional CST time-domain simulations demonstrate that the minimum $\left|S_{11}\right|$ near the operating frequency is reduced from -6.55 dB to -17.96 dB while preserving the longitudinal operating-mode field profile; the accompanying resonance shift is minor and can be corrected by routine dimensional trimming. The method thus provides a fabrication-friendly route to suppress terahertz input-cavity reflection.

title: Wideband L-band MPM for Satellite Navigation Systems
authors: Jens Freese, Thomas Kieschnick, Andreas Schreiber, Michael Ellermann, Björn Klingenberg
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: The development, design and test of a wideband L-Band MPM to be used in navigation payloads is summarized in this paper. The related TWT development has been performed by Thales MIS and is not subject of this article. The focus is on the complete MPM employing the before mentioned TWT and the related TESAT broadband linearizer that is required to provide the pre-distortion of the RF signal. An overview on the equipment design, development activities and the obtained performance is provided. The activity has been conducted in the frame of the European funded H2020 activity.

title: Z-Shape Metamaterial Slow-Wave Structure for Terahertz Dual Sheet Beams Traveling-Wave Tubes
authors: Jibo Dong, Shengkun Jiang, Yubin Gong, Pan Pan, Jinjun Feng, Zhaoyun Duan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A terahertz (THz) metamaterial slow-wave structure (MSWS) employing Z-shape metamaterial unit cells is proposed for dual sheet beams traveling-wave tube (TWT) applications. Numerical analysis demonstrates that the average interaction impedance exceeding $5 \Omega$ at 0.217 THz, which is more than twice that achieved by a traditional staggered double-vane slow-wave structure. To ensure efficient power transmission, impedance-matched input and output couplers with the proposed MSWS are developed. Full-wave simulations of a 20-period structure with the designed couplers show that, over the frequency band from 0.214 to 0.220 THz, the transmission coefficient $S_{21}$ remains greater than $-6 d B$, while the reflection coefficient $S_{11}$ is below −26 dB. These results indicate that the proposed MSWS combines high interaction impedance with favorable transmission performance, making it a promising candidate for high-power THz amplification.

title: Central Limit Theorems for Stochastic Gradient Descent Quantile Estimators
authors: Ziyang Wei, Jiaqi Li, Likai Chen, Wei Biao Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: This paper develops asymptotic theory for quantile estimation via stochastic gradient descent (SGD) with a constant learning rate. The quantile loss function is neither smooth nor strongly convex. Beyond conventional perspectives and techniques, we view quantile SGD iteration as an irreducible, periodic, and positive recurrent Markov chain, which cyclically converges to its unique stationary distribution regardless of the arbitrarily fixed initialization. To derive the exact form of the stationary distribution, we analyze the structure of its characteristic function by exploiting the stationary equation.We also derive tight bounds for its moment generating function (MGF) and tail probabilities. Synthesizing the aforementioned approaches, we prove that the centered and standardized stationary distribution converges to a Gaussian distribution as the learning rate η → 0. This finding provides the first central limit theorem (CLT)-type theoretical guarantees for the quantile SGD estimator with constant learning rates. We further propose a recursive algorithm to construct confidence intervals of the estimators with statistical guarantees. Numerical studies demonstrate the effective finite-sample performance of the online estimator and inference procedure. The theoretical tools developed in this study are of independent interest for investigating general SGD algorithms formulated as Markov chains, particularly in non-strongly convex and non-smooth settings.

title: Nonzero-Sum Differential Game-Based Approximate Optimal Control for Modular Robot Manipulator Systems Using Adaptive Dynamic Programming
authors: Xinye Zhu, Zebin J, Yi Qin, Bing Ma, Bo Dong, Tianjiao An
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper studies approximate optimal control for modular robot manipulator systems under a nonzero-sum differential game formulation. Based on a joint-torque-feedback (JTF) modeling technique, the dynamics of each joint module are expressed in a state-space form, where inter-joint coupling effects and unmodeled terms are treated as bounded uncertainties. Each joint subsystem is considered as a player, and a quadratic performance index is defined for subsequent optimal control design, balancing tracking accuracy and control input energy while accounting for coupling among players. From the Nash equilibrium conditions of the nonzero-sum game, an analytical form of the optimal control policy for the robotic system can be derived. Within the adaptive dynamic programming (ADP) framework, a critic-only neural network (NN) is constructed as a cost-function approximator to address the HJ equation, which promotes the derivation of an approximate optimal control strategy. A Lyapunov-based analysis is provided to show that the trajectory tracking error of the robotic system is uniformly ultimately bounded. Experimental studies on a modular manipulator platform are included to evaluate the proposed approach.

title: PPO-NODE: End-to-End Vision-Based Obstacle Avoidance for UAVs in Unknown Complex Environments
authors: Chaolin Fan, Kaixin Xu, Jianan Yang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To address the challenge of autonomous obstacle avoidance for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in complex environments, we propose PPO-NODE, an end-to-end deep reinforcement learning method that integrates Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) with Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (NODE). To predict the motion of dynamic obstacles, NODE is incorporated into the feature extraction process to capture continuous temporal features from sequences of depth images. Experimental evaluations conducted in a PyBullet-based 3D simulation environment demonstrate that the proposed PPO-NODE algorithm achieves a collision rate of 11.3 % in complex environments, significantly reducing the collision probability for UAVs. This effectively resolves the critical issues of prior map dependency and delayed response to dynamic obstacles inherent in traditional approaches.

title: Disturbance Predictor-Based Fault-Tolerant Formation Control and Hierarchical Structure Collision Avoidance for Fixed-Wing UAVs: a Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Bo Meng, Ke Zhang, Jingping Xia, Bin Jiang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes an integrated cooperative fault-tolerant control scheme. It prevents fault propagation, reduces collision risk, and avoids delayed compensation in fixed-wing UAV formations with actuator-efficiency loss and unknown disturbances. First, a trajectory-planning virtual agent system is constructed to decouple formation information between faulty and healthy UAVs. By incorporating an artificial potential function into this virtual agent system, collision-free reference trajectories are generated for the physical UAVs. Subsequently, a disturbance prediction mechanism is established to compensate for composite disturbances. Based on the fully actuated system approach and the disturbance predictor, a fault-tolerant control protocol is developed. Finally, simulation results and comparative experiments are provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed fault-tolerant control framework.

title: Research on electron beam welded joints between zirconium copper and 316L stainless steel for copper vacuum chamber
authors: Bofeng Wang, Jinyu Zhao, Hongqi Zhang, Cong Wang, Zhi Quan Zhang, Xuhua Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Electron beam welding is widely used in the welding of dissimilar metals. Zirconium copper and 316 L stainless steel were welded by electron beam welding with appropriate process parameters for the application of copper vacuum chamber. The effects of the welding parameters on welding depth have been studied in the experiment. Microstructure properties of the welded joints were compared with base material. The welding joints have good performance without surface defects. The relative permeability at the weld is $\mu_{r} \leq1.02$ and the vacuum leakage rate is better than 2.9E-10Pa $\mathrm{m}^{3} / \mathrm{s}$.

title: Multi-Revolution Low-Thrust GTO-to-GEO Orbital Transfers Based on an Improved Reinforcement Learning Algorithm
authors: Wenbo Zhao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: With the increasing use of low-thrust spacecraft in orbital transfer and deep-space missions, low-thrust trajectory optimization faces challenges such as long transfer durations, continuous control, sparse rewards, and strong dynamical coupling. This paper investigates the multi-revolution low-thrust GTO-to-GEO transfer problem and proposes an improved soft actor-critic algorithm from the perspectives of training strategy and value-function approximation. For training, curriculum learning is combined with hindsight experience replay to decompose the task difficulty into stages and relabel trajectories with virtual goals, thereby improving exploration efficiency under sparse-reward conditions. For value-function approximation, a broad learning system is introduced as a lightweight critic approximator within the SAC framework to reduce the complexity of critic updates. Based on modified equinoctial elements, the orbital dynamics model and reinforcement learning environment are established. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves stable convergence and can satisfy the terminal constraints in the GTO-to-GEO transfer task. Under different orbital settings, the obtained transfer times are close to those of optimal-control methods and show better time performance than selected intelligent optimization methods.

title: Motion Planning for Loaders Based on Motion Primitives
authors: Xiangda He, Mao Liu, Jiannan Chen
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Articulated loaders operating in construction and mining sites require motion planning methods that satisfy both real-time performance and kinematic feasibility. This paper presents an optimization-based motion primitive framework for articulated loaders. Initial trajectories are generated from Dubins-Shepp curves, and the primitive generation problem is formulated as an optimal control problem and solved in an unconstrained optimization form under kinematic and dynamic limits. A cost-based primitive selection strategy and receding-horizon replanning scheme are then used for online navigation. Simulation and field experiments show that the proposed method improves planning success rate and path quality while maintaining real-time performance.

title: Multi-Preset-Trajectories-Based Preassigned Performance Lateral-Directional Attitude Control for Underactuated Aircraft
authors: Daiqi Yang, Wenxi Xu, Feng Tan, Mingzhe Hou
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Aiming at the lateral-directional attitude control problem of underactuated Bank-to-Turn (BTT) aircraft in the presence of mismatched disturbances, this paper presents a novel multi-preset-trajectories-based preassigned performance control (MPT-PPC) method. By introducing preset trajectories of tracking errors, the potential boundary singularity issue inherent in conventional preassigned performance control methods can be avoided. Meanwhile, dynamic surface control is adopted to eliminate the complexity explosion problem in conventional backstepping design. The proposed MPT-PPC method integrates the above two techniques and successfully extends their applications to high-order systems by employing multiple preset trajectories. Furthermore, the MPT-PPC scheme enables preassigned performance control for multiple system states rather than only the system output. Finally, simulation results are provided to verify the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy.

title: Practical Model Predictive Control for Underactuated 3-D Truck Cranes
authors: Rongjia Lin, Zhanxiang Wang, Shuang Wang, Junpeng Sun, Biao Lu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the control problem of 3-D truck cranes, which are underactuated nonlinear systems characterized by complex dynamics. Different from existing torque-based control methods that typically neglect practical constraints, this study proposes a novel model predictive control strategy that explicitly incorporates both input and state constraints into the controller design. By constructing a discrete-time model based on the unactuated subsystem dynamics, the approach effectively addresses payload positioning accuracy, swing suppression, and system constraints within a unified optimization framework. The proposed method significantly reduces computational complexity while maintaining constraint satisfaction, enabling real-time implementation. Simulation results demonstrate the controller's effectiveness in achieving precise motion control and robust performance, confirming its practical value for real-world truck crane applications.

title: Learning Soil-Adaptive Robotic Excavation via Diffusion Policy
authors: Zichun Ma, Jianqiao Zhang, Jianrong Li, Ning Zhang, Maoguo Li, Ziyi Xu, Shiyu Li, Shengxin Sun
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: A soil-adaptive robotic excavation framework using Diffusion Policy for autonomous excavation tasks in planetary environments with varying soil properties is proposed in this work. To enable adaptive excavation behavior, a soil encoder is designed to extract latent soil representations from temporal soil-tool interaction force signals and incorporate them into the policy observation space together with the robot states. A real-world experimental platform based on a Unitree Z1 Pro robotic manipulator equipped with a force sensor and a bionic sampling shovel is developed, and excavation datasets are collected across five types of simulated Martian soils with different particle size distributions. The experiments demonstrate that the proposed soil encoder achieves 96.07% classification accuracy with a macro F1-score of 0.956, while the diffusion policy achieves low action prediction errors with overall mean absolute error of 0.001679 rad across joints and successfully adapts excavation trajectories in unseen soil conditions. The main contribution of this paper is to provide an effective learning-based solution for robust soil-adaptive autonomous excavation in previously unseen environments.

title: Path Tracking Control of Articulated Wheel Loaders Based on Improved Adaptive Model-Free Approach
authors: Runze Yang, Chang Liu, Jiannan Chen, Bo Zhang, Kuo Chen, Jiafeng Zhou
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To address the path-tracking problem of articulated wheel loaders, this paper presents an enhanced model-free adaptive control method based on full-form dynamic linearization. The proposed method systematically handles the strong nonlinearity, parametric uncertainties, high energy consumption, and coupling between lateral and heading dynamics inherent to the system. To overcome the suboptimal local solutions caused by empirical parameter tuning, the particle swarm optimization algorithm is adopted for global pre-optimization of the core controller parameters. The framework is constructed as follows. First, the lateral and heading tracking errors are integrated into a unified performance metric, namely the preview deviation angle, by extracting practical operational experience, thereby simplifying the multi-error tracking task into a manageable regulation problem.Second, an energy-related penalty item is integrated into the input criterion function employed in the improved MFAC method, which achieves high tracking accuracy while reducing energy consumption. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed approach is verified through comprehensive simulations.

title: Preparation and Investigation Composite M-type Cathodes
authors: Xianmei Chen, Junhao Sun, Anran Jiang, Fei Liu, Yunfei Yang, Jinshu Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: In this paper, a cathode with a $\mathrm{Re}_{3} \mathrm{~W}$ phase barrier layer was prepared, and its emission performance and mechanism were investigated. The results show that S1 cathode exhibits the optimal service life and evaporation rate performance, with a service life of up to $12,000 \mathrm{~h}$ and an evaporation rate of $1.56282 \times 10^{-9} \mathrm{~g} \cdot \mathrm{~cm}^{-2} \cdot \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ at a temperature of $1050^{\circ} \mathrm{C} \mathrm{b}$.TEM analysis indicates that the phase composition of the cathode remains a single HCP solid solution phase and $\mathrm{Re}_{3} \mathrm{~W}$ phase during long-term operation, which may be the key reason for the cathode’s excellent service life.

title: Design and Performance Optimization of Efficient Compression Algorithm for Meteorological Satellite Data
authors: Peng Feng, Liu Yinghao, Lang Kaiwen, Shi Haoyang, Luo Weiwei
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: With the significant increase in the number of channels of new-generation infrared hyperspectral instruments such as IASI-NG, the storage, transmission, and processing of meteorological satellite data are facing enormous pressure. Traditional image compression methods have limitations such as low efficiency and large errors when dealing with hyperspectral data. To this end, this paper proposes an efficient compression algorithm combining principal component analysis dimensionality reduction and linear quantization, aiming to significantly reduce data volume while ensuring reconstruction accuracy. First, Principal component analysis based on singular value decomposition is used to reduce the dimensionality of hyperspectral data and eliminate inter-band redundancy. Second, floating-point data is mapped to low-bit integers through linear quantization, and further compressed by combining entropy coding. The experiment is verified using IASI L1C radiance data. The results show that when the variance contribution rate is 99.99 %, only 36 principal components need to be retained to compress the 1.8 GB original data to 40.5 MB, with a reconstruction root mean square error of 0.1115. Compared with traditional methods, the proposed algorithm has higher reconstruction accuracy and shorter reconstruction time at similar compression ratios, showing good comprehensive performance.

title: High-Power Dual-Band L-Band Traveling Wave Tubes
authors: Wolfgang Dürr, Philip Birtel, Erdogan Cakir
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: THALES has a long experience in L-& S-band TWTs [2], [3], [4] and has delivered more than $\mathbf{1 0 0 0 ~ L}$-& SBand TWTs to customers. In L-Band several satellite navigation systems are using THALES traveling wave tubes. A very high efficiency, very stable group delay, high robustness and the option for radiation cooling are key advantages for traveling wave tubes amplifiers. THALES is now improving its portfolio of high-power low-frequency L-Band TWTs, capable of broadband amplification from 1.15 GHz up to 1.3 GHz, thus reducing complexity and increasing flexibility of future navigation payloads.

title: A Complex Gaussian Mixture Model with Alpha-Stable Distribution Integration for Multichannel Acoustic Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Shichao Zhang, Honglong Li, Gang Tang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Although non-contact microphone arrays play an important role in bearing fault diagnosis, the effectiveness of beamforming methods heavily relies on the accurate estimation of the steering vector. In practice applications, uncertainties such as array positioning errors can degrade the accuracy of steering vector estimation, thereby significantly reducing the reliability of fault diagnosis. Although the Complex Gaussian Mixture Model (CGMM) has demonstrated promising performance in speech enhancement, its reliance on the Gaussian assumption limits its ability to capture the heavy-tailed and impulsive characteristics of bearing fault signals, thereby restricting its applicability in bearing fault diagnosis. Therefore, to address the aforementioned limitations, this study extends the CGMM framework by incorporating alpha-stable distribution modeling and proposes a novel microphone array signal processing method for effective signal enhancement and robust fault feature extraction. The proposed method employs the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to extract multi-scale features of the signal, thereby facilitating the accurate estimation of the global alpha parameter. Subsequently, the probability density function (PDF) of the alpha-stable distribution is incorporated into the CGMM framework, enabling more accurate characterization of the statistical properties of bearing fault signals and improving the stability of array steering vector estimation. Finally, the minimum variance distortionless response (MVDR) beamformer is applied to achieve enhancement of the bearing fault signal. Both simulation and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively enhance fault-related features while suppressing noise interference, thereby confirming its robustness and effectiveness in bearing fault diagnosis.

title: Highly Compact Dual-Band Four-Port Integrated Lens MIMO Antenna for 5G New Radio IoT Devices
authors: Bilal Tariq Malik, Shahid Khan, Jamal Nasir, Slawomir Koziel
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This article introduces a highly compact, dual-wideband four-port MIMO antenna with an integrated 3D-printed dielectric lens, tailored for 5G millimeter-wave (mmWave) new radio (NR) IoT applications. The antenna's gain and port isolation are significantly enhanced by placing a hemispherical dielectric lens atop the MIMO structure. The complete integrated lens MIMO antenna (ILMA) measures just 22 × 22 x 20 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>. Without the lens, the peak gain is limited to 6 dBi and 4.2 dBi at 28 GHz and 38 GHz, respectively. With the lens, gain improves to 10.5 dBi at 28 GHz and 9 dBi at 38 GHz. The antenna also achieves outstanding isolation, over 25 dB at 28 GHz and 30 dB at 38 GHz, with an envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) below 0.02 and diversity gain (DG) exceeding 9.9 dB across both bands. Link budget analysis confirms reliable communication up to 220 m at 28 GHz and 112 m at 38 GHz with 30 dBm transmit power. A fabricated prototype validates simulation results, showing strong agreement in S-parameters, isolation, gain, and radiation patterns. Compared to state-of-the-art designs, the proposed antenna offers superior gain, enhanced isolation, compact size, and cost-effective fabrication, making it well-suited for mmWave 5G NR bands such as n257, n260, and n261.

title: Design of a Self-Aligning Knee Exoskeleton for Minimizing Parasitic Forces During Assisted Ambulation_supp1-3693674.mp4
authors: Sheng Guo
publish: N/A
abstract: One major challenge in the application of exoskeletons is the misalignment between the biological and mechanical joint axes during use, which arises from human–robot kinematic mismatches and variability in wearing conditions. As a result, parasitic forces are generated at the physical human–robot interface and lead to significant relative sliding distance (RSD) between the exoskeleton and the human leg due to constrained fit. To address this issue, we propose a novel kinematically adaptive, self-aligning knee exoskeleton that eliminates sagittal-plane misalignment to reduce parasitic forces and removes the need for prealignment before use. Both platform and human–subject experiments were performed to evaluate its performance. The platform tests demonstrate that the exoskeleton can transmit effective pure assistive torque without introducing additional parasitic forces at the pHRI. Human–subject tests show that the exoskeleton maintains low RSD and parasitic forces across walking speeds up to 1.8 m/s and assistance levels up to 16 N<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\cdot$</tex-math></inline-formula>m; although both increased with speed and assistance level, peak differences remained below 6 mm and 8 N, respectively. During stand-to-sit transitions under constant assistive torques (up to 8 Nm) at large knee flexion angles, similarly low RSD and parasitic forces were observed, with torque transmission efficiency exceeding 87%.

title: Cooperative Attention U-Net: A Geometry-Aware Deep Model for Accurate Cloud Shadow Segmentation
authors: Rui Li, Panfei Fang, Jiaxin Tian, Deqing Liu, Congcong Piao, Jia Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Accurate cloud shadow segmentation is vital for optical remote sensing. However, it remains challenging due to spectral confusion with shadows from buildings, terrain, and vegetation. Without NIR and SWIR, RGB bands offer limited ability to distinguish cloud shadows under low illumination and complex surface conditions. To tackle this challenge, this study proposes a cooperative attention network (CAU-Net) that integrates geometric priors and deep supervision to improve cloud shadow segmentation. Specifically, a cloud-shadow displacement feature, derived from solar and satellite viewing geometry, is introduced as a geometric prior to provide structural guidance and compensate for the limited spectral information of RGB imagery. Building on this, CAU-Net adopts a densely connected encoder-decoder network as the main model to perform pixel-wise segmentation, while an auxiliary model based on a linear attention mechanism is designed to refine the results by enforcing geometric consistency. To this end, a Geometric Consistency Loss (GCL) is introduced to align the predicted cloud shadow regions with their physically plausible positions, effectively enhancing both the semantic accuracy and spatial coherence of the segmentation results. Extensive experiments on the GF1_WHU dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an overall accuracy of 0.9369, precision of 0.9226, recall of 0.8404, F1-score of 0.8726, and a mean Intersection over Union (MIoU) of 0.7905. Visual assessments and ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each component. The results confirm the superiority of CAU-Net in delivering robust and accurate cloud shadow segmentation using minimal spectral input, highlighting its practical potential for large-scale remote sensing applications.

title: Compact and High-Performance Dual-Polarization Electro-Optic Nonlinear Activation Functions Using Silicon Microrings
authors: Pengliang Zhang, Tao Jin, Pengjun Wang, Shixun Dai, Weiwei Chen, Yi Tang, Qiang Fu, Jun Li, Tingge Dai, Jianyi Yang
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: In this paper, dual-polarization electro-optic nonlinear activation functions using the subwavelength-grating assisted racetrack microring and double series-coupled racetrack microring based on parity-time symmetry are proposed, designed, and experimentally characterized. Measurement results show that, the fabricated subwavelength grating-assisted racetrack microring has an extinction ratio of 19.49 dB in the transverse-electric polarization and an extinction ratio of 20.38 dB in the transverse-magnetic polarization. The threshold power of the measured Sigmoid (ReLU) activation function in transverse-electric (transverse-magnetic) polarization is 0.1 mW. For the MNIST handwritten digit classification task, applying the measured Sigmoid and ReLU activation functions within the convolutional neural network results in validation accuracies of 97.03% and 97.14%. For the fabricated double series-coupled racetrack microring, the measured extinction ratios in transverse-electric and transverse magnetic polarizations are respectively 32.19 dB and 33.13 dB. Sigmoid and ReLU activation functions in this case show a threshold power of 0.1 mW, and their associated validation accuracies reach 97.79% and 97.73%.

title: Leveraging Large Language Models for Robust Speaker Verification
authors: Xuehua Zhang, Yuntao Sun, Sun Yanting
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Speaker verification (SV) has become increasingly crucial in voice authentication, security, and human-computer interaction systems. However, existing SV models often rely on task-specific architectures and require large amounts of speaker-labeled data, which limits their generalization to unseen acoustic environments or languages. With the recent emergence of audio large language models (ALLMs) such as Qwen-Audio, which exhibit strong cross-modal understanding and reasoning capabilities, a natural question arises: Can ALLMs be effectively adapted to perform speaker verification? In this paper, we explore this question and propose ALLM4SV, a novel framework that leverages Qwen-Audio for speaker verification. We reformulate the SV task as an audio-pair discrimination problem, where the model receives two speech segments and is prompted to determine whether they belong to the same speaker. To enable discriminative learning, we perform supervised fine-tuning (SFT) on large-scale speaker datasets, guiding the model to capture speaker-specific representations while preserving its general audio understanding ability. During inference, the predicted probabilities are used to compute the Equal Error Rate (EER) for quantitative evaluation. Extensive experiments are conducted on three widely used benchmark datasets, including CN-Celeb, VoxCeleb, and VoxSRC21. The results demonstrate that our method achieves competitive or even superior performance compared with traditional SV systems.

title: LAP: Controlling Text-to-Image Diffusion Models via Latent Alternation Projection
authors: He Wang, Dejiao Xue, Shaomeng Wang, Longquan Dai, Jinhui Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Text-to-image diffusion models have achieved remarkable success, while there is a growing need for conditioning techniques to control the generation process in various scenarios. Existing models have two main issues: some require task-specific retraining and extensive training time, while others avoid training but suffer from gradient estimates and slow inference. We introduce Latent Alternation Projection, a new framework that controls text-to-image diffusion models in latent space. We view image generation as a series of projections onto denoising sets, and add conditional control by defining condition sets and using alternation projection to find the intersection of these sets. Our method offers several benefits: 1) Model Agnosticism: Compatible with various diffusion model and sampling scheduler. 2) Multi- Condition Generation: Easily extends to multiple conditional sets for enhanced flexibility. 3) Efficiency: Comparable control with reduced training and sampling times. Extensive experiments demonstrate the superior performance of our method.

title: Bus Voltage Spikes Suppression with Energy Recovery in Current-Fed Buck Isolated DC-DC Converters
authors: Mingyu Liao, Zhihong Fu, Guang Deng, Feng Cheng, Zhiguo Zhang, Xuquan Hu, Wenjie You
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: To address the voltage spike caused by the current difference between the current-fed inductor and the transformer leakage inductance in the current-fed buck isolated dc-dc converter, a suppression method with energy recovery is proposed. In this method, the voltage spikes energy is absorbed by the clamping capacitor and subsequently transferred to the system control circuit through an auxiliary power supply. Different from the traditional voltage spike suppression methods, the proposed approach is decoupled from the main circuit and is realized only by using a logic circuit composed of a small number of discrete devices, thereby enabling simple control and facilitating high-power-density design. An 1800 W prototype with an input voltage range of 200-400 V and an output voltage of 28 V is built to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. Experimental results validate that the proposed strategy effectively suppresses bus voltage spikes, reducing the peak voltage to 65% of the original value. Furthermore, compared with the conventional resistor–capacitor–diode (RCD) suppression scheme, the maximum efficiency is improved by 3.2%, achieving a peak efficiency of 96.2% and a power density of 434 W/in<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>.

title: An RRT* Path Planning Method Satisfying Kinodynamic Constraints
authors: Yuhang Zhong, Yingjing Shi, Rui Li
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper addresses the drawbacks of conventional arc-based path optimization, which demands a large safety margin on both sides of the flight path near turns. In such methods, the straight-line segments of the planned route can be dangerously close to obstacles, leading to poor resistance to disturbances and an increased risk of collisions. To overcome these challenges, the paper introduces a three-arc transition approach. Theoretical analysis shows that, compared to the single-arc transition technique, this method requires a smaller safety margin on both sides of the flight path for fixed-wing UAVs and ensures safe flight during both turning and straight segments, thereby enhancing the UAV's mission success rate. Simulations using RRT* path planning with the three-arc transition, designed to meet the dynamic constraints of fixed-wing UAVs, were performed in urban environments featuring ring road systems. The results confirm that the proposed approach achieves a higher success rate and shorter path length than traditional arc-based path optimization methods.

title: Phase Controlled Bunch Sustainability for Enhanced Beam-Wave Interaction in RBWO
authors: Chitaranjan Jadab, Romesh Chandra, Amitava Roy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Efficiency enhancement in a relativistic backward wave oscillator (RBWO) under low guiding magnetic field condition, is strongly governed by the effectiveness of electron bunching and sustainability of these bunches throughout the interaction region. This work builds upon an earlier study that demonstrated enhanced bunch sustainability in a non-uniform RBWO using a sectioned slow wave structure (SWS), achieved by injecting the pre-bunched electron beam into the second section with an unfavorable phase. This phase manipulated injection leads to power enhancement of approximately $60 \%$ compared to the conventional configuration. The present work provides a theoretical framework using small signal analysis to explain the sustained bunching and prolonged beam-wave interaction observed in that configuration, without significant beam spreading or phase slippage.

title: Challenges in the Design of EPC for Space-borne Ku-Band Transmitter
authors: Neeraj Kumar, K Skekar, P. Sidhrathan, S.K. Datta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: An Electronic Power Conditioner (EPC) is designed for powering a Ku-Band Transmitter for Space application, delivering 300 W of Peak power with $20 \%$ (max.) duty cycle. The EPC provides conditioned power to the Solid-State Power Amplifier (SSPA) and a Travelling Wave Tube (TWT) in a single enclosure. All the power dissipating elements of the EPC are conduction cooled through the baseplate and the side walls. The EPC manages the sequencing, fault monitoring and protection apart from the communication interface of the Transmitter. This paper briefs the design challenges, the approach, and the mechanical design considerations of the EPC.

title: PERT-SAM: Progressive Enhanced Refinement and Tuning Defect Segmentation Method
authors: Jiamei Cheng, Chuhan Wang, Weichao Dong, Jialiang Wang, Huilai Fan, Lei Zhou
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Intelligent defect segmentation plays a pivotal role in ensuring industrial product quality. However, practical deployment is often hindered by the feature scale dilemma and the high visual similarity between defects and complex backgrounds. Although vision foundation models such as SAM demonstrate remarkable representation capability, a fundamental trade-off emerges between their massive parameterization and the limited annotated samples available in domain-specific scenarios, such as photovoltaic cells with intricate polycrystalline textures. To address this issue, we propose PERT-SAM, a Progressive Enhanced Refinement and Tuning defect segmentation framework built upon a “foundation model + lightweight modulation network” paradigm. By freezing the SAM 2 backbone to preserve generalized prior knowledge and incorporating a parameter-efficient feature modulation module, the proposed method effectively mitigates fine-tuning instability and overfitting risks under limited-data conditions, thereby establishing a practical paradigm for efficient domain adaptation in visual defect segmentation.

title: Photonic-based Multiple RF Signal Generation using Optical Frequency Comb for High-Power Millimeter-Wave Transmitter
authors: Minje Song, Ingeun Lee, Yoonseon Choi, Jinwoo Shin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: We design and numerically demonstrate a photonic-based multi-band millimeter-wave (mmW) transmitter capable of simultaneously generating multiple RF signals for high-power applications. The system utilizes a cascaded electro-optic frequency comb (EO-Comb) generator to generate stable multi wavelength optical carriers. In this paper, we focus on the multiple RF generation and verified that the generated signals exhibit high spectral purity and clear distinguishability across all bands. The multiple RF generation can be served in multi-band radar and 6G systems.

title: Finite-Time Adaptive Fault-Tolerant Control for Nonlinear Systems with State Constraints
authors: Xinxin Liu, Yang Cui
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper addresses the adaptive finite-time control problem for an uncertain non-strict feedback nonlinear system with sensor faults and state constraints. First, a continuous piecewise function is introduced in the controller design to effectively avoid potential singularity issues and reduce the complexity of the system design. To further tackle the state constraint problem, an asymmetric time-varying boundary layer function (BLF) is used, allowing the state constraint boundary to gradually decrease over time and be dynamically adjusted according to trajectory requirements. Meanwhile, compensation techniques and adaptive methods are introduced to handle sensor faults. Stability analysis shows that the proposed control strategy can make the tracking error converge to a small neighborhood around the system origin within finite time, and ensures the boundedness of all signals in the closed-loop system. Finally, the effectiveness of the control strategy is verified through simulation results.

title: DUAL-Calib: Joint LiDAR-Dual-Camera Extrinsic Calibration with Automatic Target Corner Detection
authors: Jianyu Zhang, Chong Wang, Jiannan Chen
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper presents the DUAL-Calib calibration framework, which enables joint constrained extrinsic calibration of a single LiDAR and two cameras. As an optimized extension of FAST-Calib for multi-camera scenarios, DUAL-Calib establishes a unified optimization framework with inter-camera rigid geometric constraints to enhance calibration consistency and solve the cumulative error problem caused by independent calibration. Furthermore, this paper proposes an automatic corner detection algorithm based on ArUco marker triangulation, which avoids manual on-site measurement of the target's physical dimensions and enables automated field calibration. It can directly reconstruct the target coordinate system from the detected markers, realizing semi-automated calibration with reduced manual measurement. Experimental verification was performed on a wheel loader platform equipped with the Lingmou S1 perception module (integrating a Livox Mid360 LiDAR and two wide-angle fisheye cameras), with the calibration target placed within the overlapping field of view of the sensors at a distance of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5-4.5 ~\mathrm{m}$</tex>. The results demonstrate that the point-to-point registration error of DUAL-Calib on both camera channels is less than 5.0 mm under normal conditions and remains below 5.8 mm even under vibration interference. Compared with FAST-Calib's independent single-camera calibration method, DUAL-Calib reduces the calibration errors of LiDAR-Camera A and LiDAR-Camera B by 37.5 % and 36.0 % respectively with significantly lower variance, and the proposed automatic corner extraction method achieves sub-centimeter accuracy compared with manual measurement, effectively bridging the practicality gap between laboratory calibration and on-site deployment of construction machinery vehicles.

title: A PCM Optimization Method for High-Power Sheet-Beam TWT under Dynamic Signal Input
authors: Shixuan Meng, Hao Li, Shuo Li, Yi Zeng, Yafen Shang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper proposes an optimization method for the magnetic field configuration in a periodic cusped magnet (PCM)system, aimed at enhancing the focusing performance for a sheet-beam traveling-wave tube (SBTWT) operating under high-frequency signals. Based on a beam voltage of 30 kV and the current of 0.73 A, the optimized PCM structure was integrated into CST for numerical simulation. Results indicate that within a beam tunnel of cross-section $6.8 \mathrm{~mm} \times 0.8 \mathrm{~mm}$, the sheet-beam transmission efficiency over a 185 mm length improves from 82.1% to 100%. The overall optimization process is completed within approximately 7-8 hours, demonstrating both high efficiency and practical applicability in the design of high-frequency SB-TWT focusing systems.

title: Reflection Suppression in a 0.34-THz Extended-Interaction Klystron Input Cavity Using a Stepped-Coupling Structure
authors: Wanli Shi, Guoxin Ren, Haiying Yuan, Shilong Zhu, Luanfeng Gao, Junhui Yin, Xiaofang Zhu, Bin Li, Yulu Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Low input reflection is essential for establishing a stable RF field in the input cavity of a terahertz extended-interaction klystron (EIK) and, consequently, for achieving efficient and repeatable beam velocity modulation. This paper proposes a stepped coupling-cavity scheme to suppress the input-port reflection of a $0.34-\mathrm{THz}, 9-$ gap EIK input cavity while preserving the desired longitudinal operating mode. In contrast to conventional approaches that modify the coupling aperture (e.g., tapered or multi-step apertures), the proposed design retains a standard rectangular coupling hole and introduces a compact, impedance-transforming stepped cavity between the WR-2 waveguide and the interaction structure. This configuration not only markedly reduces the input-port reflection but also alleviates key drawbacks of existing suppression methods, including low coupling efficiency, limited bandwidth, and stringent fabrication demands for gradual-transition structures, as well as the electric-field concentration, spurious-mode excitation, and restricted tuning flexibility often associated with conventional stepped-aperture designs. Three dimensional CST simulations show that the minimum $\left|S_{11}\right|$ near the operating frequency improves from -11.97 dB to -20.43 dB, with the operating mode maintained and only a small upward frequency shift that can be readily compensated through routine dimensional tuning.

title: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Anomaly Detection and Root Cause Identification in Multistage Industrial Processes via Causal Graph-Based Spatio-Temporal Learning
authors: Chensong Xu, Rui Wang, Peng Li, Yangkun Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: With the increasing integration of industrial production systems, multi-stage processes become more complex and exhibit strong stage heterogeneity and inter-stage error propagation, which limits conventional approaches for anomaly detection and root cause analysis. This paper proposes a causal graph neural framework for anomaly detection and root cause analysis in multi-stage industrial processes. The process is modeled as a hierarchical causal structure composed of a global graph and stagelevel subgraphs which are intended for interpretable fault propagation tracing under explicit process assumptions. Process prior knowledge is encoded as feasibility gates to constrain candidate edges, upon which learnable masks select effective connections. Global graph captures inter-stage causal dependencies, while masked subgraph learns intra-stage dynamic relationships. Then, the spatio-temporal layers with temporal encoding and graph convolution are employed for representation learning and forecasting. Residual-based anomaly detection then activates a hierarchical diagnosis pipeline, including stage-level localization via the global graph, intra-stage attribution of key parameters via dynamic subgraphs, and fault propagation path inference. Experiments on a multistage industrial dataset show that the proposed method demonstrates the feasibility of residual-based anomaly triggering and structured root-cause tracing and provides a structured and interpretable root cause analysis solution for complex manufacturing systems.

title: Investigation of Pulsed Power Oscillations in X-band Magnetron with Non-Incandescent Cathode
authors: Kostyantyn Ilyenko, Victor V. Zavertannyj, Vagan N. Gurdzhian, Valentyn P. Dzyuba, Tetyana Yatsenko
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: We report an investigation of pulsed power characteristics (pulse duration, duty factors, pulse repetition frequencies) in X-band magnetron with non-incandescent cathode, which employs field electron emission thin foil sharp blade-type sources to instigate the primary emission of electrons. Dependence of pulse power on pulse repetition rate is studied and discussed.

title: Surviving the Paper Deluge: A One-Year Study in Learning From Demonstration [Science and Technology Watch]
authors: Aude Billard, Renaud Detry, Nadia Figueroa, Maximilian Foriest, Dongheui Lee, Kunpeng Yao
publish: IEEE Robotics & Automation Magazine

title: Dual-Channel Event-Based Adaptive Control for Uncertain High-Order Fully Actuated Systems
authors: Wei Tian, Changchun Hua
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This work focuses on the problem of adaptive event-based control for a class of uncertain high-order fully actuated (HOFA) systems. Unlike the single-channel event-driven results for HOFA systems, we present a dual-channel triggering mechanism, in which the first channel governs control input and the second channel updates signals in adaptive laws, thus significantly reducing communication burden. To address the challenge caused by discontinuous adaptive laws, a decomposition technique is developed and the event errors can be bounded by differential terms. These terms are effectively counteracted via negative feedback terms within the controller. Then, through Lyapunov-based stability analysis, the boundedness of all closed-loop signals is rigorously verified under designed controller. Finally, a simulation example is illustrated to demonstrate the validity of the presented control method.

title: Data–driven identification of switched discrete–time linear systems
authors: Laura Menini, Corrado Possieri, Antonio Tornambe
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: This letter addresses the identification of discrete-time switched linear input–output systems from measured input–output data when the switching signal is unknown. The key idea is to eliminate the unmeasured switching sequence by constructing a single data–driven polynomial constraint that vanishes on the union of the mode–dependent regression hyperplanes; the local dynamics are then retrieved from the linear factors of such a polynomial. For the ideal case, i.e., in the absence of noise and assuming exact computations, two complementary identification routes are developed: an algebraic geometry approach based on ideals and Gröbner bases, which provides insights on the structure of the problem, and a linear algebra approach that builds the data-driven polynomial constraint via determinants of suitably constructed data matrices. An iterative approach is proposed to efficiently implement the latter method and to improve its robustness to the presence of measurement noise or rounding error.

title: A Millimeter-Wave Flat-Top Fan-Beam Antenna Array Based on a Geometry-Independent Resonant Cavity
authors: Evandro C. Vilas Boas, Sofia B. Vasconcellos, Arismar C. Sodré, Felipe A. P. De Figueiredo
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This work presents a novel antenna design capable of producing a flat-top fan-beam profile through the integration of slotted apertures and a geometry-independent resonator. Starting with a three-slot cavity-backed design, the radiator is reconfigured into a five-slot structure by reshaping its geometry while preserving the original cross-sectional area, converting a pencil beam into a fan-shaped beam without shifting the resonant operating frequency. The cavity is loaded with an FR4 dielectric slab to achieve impedance matching, and metallic rods are placed around the slab to suppress undesired mode coupling. In addition, a stepped profile is utilized around the slot apertures to increase the flat-top beamwidth. The prototype exhibits a measured −10 dB bandwidth over 1.11 GHz, a 1 dB beamwidth directivity with ripple below 0.71 dB over 60°−70°, a 3 dB beamwidth over 80°, a peak realized gain of 7.84 dBi, and sidelobe levels below −10.02 dB. The proposed design offers a compact and competitive size compared to existing fan-beam antenna solutions, with overall dimensions of 0.95λo × 2.33λo × 3.10λo, while maintaining a simple structural configuration.

title: Development and Validation of a Novel Handheld Spasticity Measurement System
authors: Leah O’Shea, Nina L. Suresh, William Zev Rymer, Sourav Chandra, Arthur Prochazka, Alexander Barry
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Spasticity, a neurologically mediated increase in muscle tone, imposes motor control barriers in individuals with a significant injury to the nervous system. Currently, there is not a widely accepted quantitative method of evaluating the magnitude of spasticity. We have developed a novel handheld device with the goal of quantifying the degree and severity of spasticity in persons following a stroke. The sensing capabilities of the device provide angular jointmeasurements and joint torques in a single standalone device. The sensors were validated with engineering grade instruments and correlated well in angular and force domains, r = 0.97 and r = 0.99, respectively. Clinicians donned the device with participants who had experienced a chronic stroke and performed clinical measurements of spasticity. The clinicians were surveyed after testing; concluding that the device demonstrated utility, innovation, and acceptability in a clinical setting.

title: Design and Experimental Validation of FOC With Symmetrical Optimum Tuning for PMSM-Based AWES Emulation
authors: Pankaj Kumar, Yashwant Kashyap, Anabalagan Karthikeyan, Manjunatha Sharma K, Debabrata Karmakar
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics
abstract: Kite based airborne wind energy system represents an innovative approach to harnessing strong and reliable winds at higher altitudes, providing greater power density compared to conventional wind turbines. However, the turbine's power and rotating speed vary frequently based on the Kite's movement in the air, which causes additional challenges in regulating the generator's speed. To overcome this issue, this paper proposes an adaptable electrical controlled hardware test bench system to replicate the dynamic behavior of a kite-based airborne wind power generation system (KAWPGS) using a permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM). The field-oriented control approach is utilized to achieve precise control over the speed and torque of the PMSM. A three-phase voltage source converter is used to drive the PMSM. A symmetrical optimal method is introduced for designing the controller gain, which generates modulating signals through the FPGA-based OP-4520 to interface with the hardware system. The system stability is evaluated using a Bode plot. The system's efficiency is verified using MATLAB Simulink for various wind velocity inputs and load conditions. Due to laboratory constraints, the 250 kW system has been scaled down to a 2.5 kW hardware prototype for practical implementation. Furthermore, a comparative analysis with existing state-of-the-art control strategies highlights that the proposed symmetrical optimum method based FOC (SOM-FOC) achieves the fastest transient response, lowest THD, and highest efficiency, validated in both simulations and hardware experiments.

title: Dynamic Dependencies of Fault-Related Variables Based Interpretable Fault Diagnosis Framework for Industrial Processes
authors: Liang Ma, Qikai Yang, Kaixiang Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Fault diagnosis plays a critical role in ensuring industrial safety, production efficiency, and product quality. In practical industrial processes, various process variables exhibit distinct sensitivities to different fault types. Traditional methods that directly model and analyze multivariate process data tend to introduce redundant information and ignore the dynamic interdependencies among variables, thereby deviating the fault diagnosis mechanism from actual industrial operating laws. Such issues inevitably impair the model interpretability and degrade the overall fault diagnosis performance. Therefore, to effectively utilize the multivariate information of industrial processes and enhance the model interpretability and fault diagnosis performance, in this paper, a dynamic dependencies of fault-related variables based interpretable fault diagnosis framework is proposed. Specifically, all process variables are screened based on the distribution differences between normal and fault samples for selecting fault-related variables. Subsequently, a new graph structure learning module is constructed to capture the dynamic dependencies among fault-related variables and build a graph, which is incorporated into feature enhancement and fusion process of fault-related variables to improve the representation capability of fault features. After that, fault diagnosis is performed based on the fused features, improving the diagnosis accuracy and interpretability. Finally, three case studies based on the hot rolling process are designed to validate the effectiveness, feasibility and generalization performance. Experimental results show that the proposed method achieves fault diagnosis accuracies of 97.67%, 95.5%, and 98.12% in three cases, respectively.

title: Competitive Decision-Making in a Memristor-Based Neuromorphic Circuit: A Bio-Inspired Approach for Robotics
authors: Yunlai Zhu, Zhongren Ye, Tao Jiang, Yong Zhang, Ke Wang, Xiaoling Wu, Chaotong Xie, Haotian Tang, Xing Cao, Qicheng Hu, Zuyu Xu, Zhe Feng, Lihua Xu, Wendong Lu, Zuheng Wu, Yuehua Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Endowing robots with human-like judgment and decision-making capabilities is a key objective in biomimetic robotics, enabling flexible adaptation to diverse scenarios. However, emulating the competitive decision-making mechanisms observed in biological neural pathways remains a significant challenge. Inspired by the competitive interaction governing social decisions in male mice, this work proposes a memristor-based neural circuit for competitive mode-switching decision-making. The circuit implements dual behavioral modes, termed “lover mode” and “brother mode”. The system integrates three core neuromorphic modules: a danger detection module, a mode conversion module, and a feedback module. The danger detection module identifies and classifies incoming danger signals, providing input to the mode conversion module. Leveraging the inherent nonlinear resistance characteristics of memristors, the mode conversion module implements a dual-pathway competitive decision model that dynamically switches between the “lover” and “brother” modes. To prevent prolonged activation of the “brother mode”, the feedback module generates a signal to facilitate adaptive circuit learning through synaptic weight modification. The functional validation of each module and the overall decision-making logic was comprehensively performed using the PSpice simulation platform. Simulation results demonstrate that the circuit effectively emulates the biological neural competition mechanism, offering a verifiable hardware implementation and theoretical foundation for intelligent decision-making systems in emotionally interactive robots.

title: Surrogate Management Strategies for Accelerating Parallel Algorithm Portfolio Construction_supp1-3703313.pdf
