title: AC Flashover Characteristics of Roof Insulators with Fan-Shaped Non-Uniform Pollution under High-Speed Airflow Conditions
authors: Yujun Guo, Yifei Meng, Jian Wang, Shuo Li, Dalin Li, Zihao Wang, Xueqin Zhang, Guangning Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Roof-mounted high-voltage equipment operates under sustained high-speed airflow, leading to a pronounced non-uniform pollution distribution between the windward and leeward surfaces. The combined effects of high-speed airflow and fan-shaped non-uniform pollution significantly affect the AC flashover characteristics and arc development behavior of roof insulators, posing a potential risk to train operation. Therefore, AC flashover tests were conducted on roof insulators with different degrees of non-uniform pollution under high-speed airflow conditions. The results show that the arc is progressively detached from the polluted surface by forward airflow and follows a characteristic “blow-off – elongation – reattachment” evolution. With increasing airflow velocity, the flashover voltage first increases and then slightly decreases, while at low airflow velocities it is more sensitive to the degree of pollution non-uniformity.

title: Improving Microwave Heating Uniformity by Inserting a Spark-Free Dielectric Metamaterial Reflector in a Single-Mode Waveguide
authors: Runhai Hu, Yu Mao, Shenwei Yin, Xi Peng, Yuhang Yang, Junke Xiao, Zhixiang Tang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Mechanical phase-shifting effectively improves microwave heating uniformity, but typically relies on metallic sliding shorts, which inherently pose arcing risks at high power. This letter proposes a spark-free alternative: a movable, all-dielectric reflector implemented by inserting a uniaxial metamaterial slab into a rectangular waveguide operating in the single-mode regime. By rotating the slab’s optic axis, the incident <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathrm {TE}_{10}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> mode is coupled into evanescent higher-order modes, yielding near-total reflection (with a measured reflection coefficient of −0.29 dB). Dynamically moving the slab shifts the spatial standing-wave pattern, time-averaging localized hot spots. Experiments with a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$700-\mathrm {W}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> prototype on various dielectric samples (potato, desiccant, salt) demonstrated significantly improved uniformity. Notably, the temperature coefficient of variation (COV) was reduced by up to 54.7%, achieved for the low-loss salt, compared with static heating. This work validates an effective, noncontact phase-control paradigm that avoids the moving electrical contacts associated with conventional metallic components.

title: On maximal positive invariant set computation for rank-deficient linear systems
authors: Bogdan Gheorghe, Daniel Ioan, Cristian Flutur, Ionela Prodan, Florin Stoican
publish: IEEE Control Systems Letters
abstract: The maximal positively invariant (MPI) set is obtained through a backward reachability procedure involving the iterative computation and intersection of predecessor sets under state and input constraints. However, standard static feedback synthesis may place some of the closed-loop eigenvalues at zero, leading to rank-deficient dynamics. This affects the MPI computation by inducing projections onto lower-dimensional subspaces during intermediate steps. By exploiting the Schur decomposition, we explicitly address this singular case and propose a robust algorithm that computes the MPI set in both polyhedral and constrained-zonotope representations.

title: Hybrid ASL-SC Converter with High Gain and Near Zero Input Current Ripple for Fuel Cell Vehicles
authors: Motiur Reza Mohammed, Vinod Khadkikar, Bashar Zahawi, Omar Alzaabi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: DC-DC converters employed in fuel cell vehicles (FCVs) are expected to demonstrate characteristics such as a wide voltage gain range, low input current ripple, and high voltage gain. To meet these specifications, this paper proposes a hybrid DC-DC converter comprising an active switched inductor (ASL) cell, a switched capacitor (SC) cell, and a zero current ripple (ZCR) cell. The proposed converter achieves high voltage gain, wide voltage gain range, low switch current and voltage stresses, and near zero input current ripple. The SC cell supports the output capacitor of the switches during the OFF periods, decreasing switch voltage stress and further enhancing voltage gain. The ZCR cell helps reduce input current ripple and lowers current stress in the switches. The paper includes a discussion on the operating principles of the proposed converter, steady-state analysis, parameter design consideration, closed-loop controller design and a comparison with other hybrid ASL-SC high-gain DC-DC converters. Finally, a 720W laboratory prototype is built and tested. Experimental results are presented to verify the operation of the proposed converter. The closed-loop performance is also validated experimentally under load and input voltage variations.

title: aSMO: An SMO-Style Solver with Linear Per-Iteration Updates for Large-Scale SVM+ Training
authors: José Daniel Fernández, Jesús Vega, Sebastián Dormido-Canto
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Support Vector Machines with Privileged Information (SVM+) leverage auxiliary data available only during training to improve generalization performance. However, their practical adoption remains limited due to the high computational cost of solving the associated quadratic programming (QP) problem, which scales poorly with dataset size. In this work, we propose Adaptive Sequential Minimal Optimization (aSMO), a specialized SMO-style solver designed for efficient optimization of the SVM+ dual formulation. Unlike classical SMO methods, aSMO operates on irreducible sets of two or three variables to satisfy the coupled equality and inequality constraints inherent to SVM+. The method incorporates a two-phase working set selection strategy and incremental gradient updates, enabling a per-iteration computational complexity that scales linearly with the number of training samples under precomputed kernel matrices. The proposed approach is evaluated on benchmark datasets from computer vision (MNIST) and vibration-based fault diagnosis (CWRU). Experimental results demonstrate that aSMO achieves substantial reductions in training time compared to a standard QP reference baseline, while reaching comparable dual objective values and producing similar test accuracy under identical hyperparameter settings. Furthermore, the method scales effectively to datasets with tens of thousands of samples, where conventional interior-point methods become computationally intractable. These results indicate that aSMO provides a practical and scalable alternative for training SVM+ models, facilitating the practical use of privileged information in large-scale learning scenarios.

title: Direction-of-Arrival Estimation via Null-Scanning Leaky-Wave Antennas
authors: Jirui Zhang, Dongze Zheng, Yan Zhang, Zhi Hao Jiang, Ke Wu
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: The natural frequency-angle mapping or frequency beam-scanning property possessed by leaky-wave antennas (LWAs) has enabled their potential use in low-cost single-channel direction-of-arrival (DoA) estimation. Conventional high-resolution LWA-based DoA estimation methods, such as those based on the Multiple Signal Classification (MUSIC) algorithm (referred to as LWA-MUSIC), typically rely on Sinc-function-like radiation beams, where the frequency-angle mapping is characterized by a frequency-scanned radiation peak and used to construct the angular manifold. However, the relatively smooth power variation around the radiation peak fundamentally limits angular sensitivity and resolution, particularly for closely spaced sources. In this work, we propose a DoA estimation method through a null-scanning LWA, which is characterized by a frequency-scanned radiation null. The NSLWA response is realized by superposing two -1<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">st</sup>-spatial-harmonic leaky-wave contributions with slightly detuned periodicities to form a stable frequency-scanned radiation null, and a corresponding MUSIC-based frequency-manifold framework is developed. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed NSLWA-MUSIC approach achieves sub-degree DoA estimation accuracy and provides improved angular resolution compared with conventional Sinc-function-beam LWA-MUSIC method under identical conditions. This work may provide a cost-effective solution for high-resolution DoA estimation, with potential applications in future sensing and localization systems.

title: Enhancing Wireless Sensing by Phased Array with Movable Antennas
authors: Mengyao Zeng, Haifan Yin, Weidong Li, Kewei Zhu
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: In this letter, we propose an enhanced wireless sensing approach using phased array with movable antennas (PAMA), which may improve the direction-of-arrival (DOA) estimation accuracy by optimizing antenna positions on the phased array. We analyze the estimation accuracy by deriving the Cram´er-Rao bound (CRB) and investigating the relationship between the CRB and antenna positions. We then propose an elite-guided hybrid swarm optimization (EGHSO) algorithm to minimize the CRB with the angular ambiguity function constraint. Our proposed algorithm takes advantage of particle swarm optimization and genetic algorithm, efficiently addressing the non-convex position optimization problem with fast convergence and improved performance. Simulation results show that our proposed PAMA-enhanced sensing achieves significant performance gains over conventional fixed-position antenna-based sensing.

title: Machine-Learning-Based Coordinated Beam Pairing in Dense MU-MIMO Deployments without Explicit Interference Information
authors: Natalia Yarkina, Dmitri Moltchanov, Mikko Valkama
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Multi-user multiple-input and multiple-output (MU-MIMO) is a fundamental enabler of high-data-rate access in 5G New Radio (NR) and future 6G systems. However, in dense deployments, strong inter-cell interference can drastically reduce the efficiency of this technology. Measuring and delivering real-time interference information involves significant overheads and imposes strict timing constraints, thus making MU-MIMO susceptible to performance degradation. To improve MU-MIMO performance under severe inter-cell interference, we propose a machine-learning-based beam pairing approach that relies upon implicit feedback about past user transmissions. To eliminate the need for explicit and timely interference reporting, the associated online learning procedure involves specifically designed mechanisms for gathering adequate training data. The proposed technique is comprehensively investigated via system-level simulation accounting for the major aspects of the 5G NR air interface technology. Our numerical results demonstrate that the proposed approach allows to reach 90–95% of the sum rate and 80% of the cell-edge rate attained by an idealized interference-aware scheduler, while preserving comparable fairness of rate allocations among users.

title: Three-dimensional coupled-wave analysis for photonic-crystal surface-emitting lasers with external optical feedback
authors: Shota Koga, Takuya Inoue, Menaka De Zoysa, Susumu Noda
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Quantum Electronics
abstract: Photonic-crystal surface-emitting lasers (PCSELs) enable large-area coherent oscillation by incorporating a photonic crystal resonator, thereby realizing high output power and excel lent beam quality. Advances in photonic-crystal design have led to enhanced functionalities, including beam steering, high-speed modulation, and narrow-linewidth operation. As a promising approach to further extending the functionality of PCSELs, self-injection optical feedback induced by an external reflector, which has been previously reported in conventional semiconductor lasers to achieve linewidth narrowing and expansion of the modulation bandwidth, is considered. In this paper, we develop a numerical method that enables the analysis of PCSELs with external optical feedback, and we conduct a theoretical investigation on its lasing characteristics based on this framework. First, we show that the lasing modes are formed through the coupling between the large area transverse resonant modes inside the photonic crystal and the longitudinal Fabry–Pérot modes introduced by the external reflector, while maintaining single-transverse-mode operation with a narrow divergence angle. We also reveal that single-longitudinal mode operation can be achieved by adjusting the feedback strength and the delay time, where frequency sweep operation can be also realized by controlling the reflection phase. These results suggest that external optical feedback can provide an effective approach for realizing advanced functionality of PCSELs.

title: FSSL-TANet: A Federated Semi-Supervised Temporal Attention Network for Personalized Early Detection of Age-Related Macular Degeneration in Consumer Electronics
authors: Jing Yang, Dayu Hu, Muhammad Attique Khan, Lucia Cascone, Mohammed Wasim Bhatt, Por Lip Yee, Hongyan Yao, Qianjie Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: The growing integration of consumer electronics in digital healthcare has opened promising pathways for personalized diagnosis of vision disorders, particularly Age-Related Macular Degeneration (AMD). Devices such as mobile retinal scanners and home-based OCT units now produce extensive time-stamped patient data. However, leveraging this data effectively is hindered by challenges, including privacy concerns, limited computational resources on edge devices, and the non-identical distribution of data across users, which make traditional centralized learning approaches less viable. To navigate these constraints, this study presents FSSL-TANet—a Federated Semi-Supervised Learning Temporal Attention Network tailored for multi-task AMD prediction using decentralized, longitudinal health data. The architecture integrates residual recurrent encoding, hierarchical temporal fusion, and a dual-channel attention mechanism to extract disease progression patterns from both labeled and unlabeled sequences. Personalization is achieved through an Adaptive Federated Temporal Aggregation (AFTA) mechanism that tailors updates based on client-specific behavior. Furthermore, a hybrid optimization strategy (AH-GTO) enhances training efficiency by reducing convergence time during hyperparameter tuning. An experimental evaluation on two public datasets—AMDP and ODIR—demonstrates the model’s strong performance, achieving 98.7% accuracy in AMD stage classification, a risk score RMSE of 0.038, and a time-to-AMD (TTAMD) estimation MAE of 1.32 months. The model also achieves a Temporal Progression Sensitivity (TPS) of 0.944, reduces overfitting by 19%, improves client fairness by 11%, and converges 24% faster compared to baseline optimizers. Designed for real-world deployment, FSSL-TANet is both lightweight and privacy-aware, making it well-suited for consumer-grade health tools such as mobile eye screening devices and home-based vision monitors. Its ability to deliver personalized AMD monitoring in everyday settings marks a meaningful advancement in the field of digital ophthalmic care.

title: Stochastic Approximation Approaches to Group Distributionally Robust Optimization and Beyond
authors: Lijun Zhang, Haomin Bai, Peng Zhao, Zhi-Hua Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: This paper investigates group distributionally robust optimization (GDRO) with the goal of learning a model that performs well over $m$ different distributions. First, we formulate GDRO as a stochastic convex-concave saddle-point problem and solve it using stochastic mirror descent (SMD) with $m$ samples per iteration, attaining a nearly optimal sample complexity. To reduce the number of samples required in each round from $m$ to 1, we cast GDRO as a two-player game, where one player conducts SMD and the other executes an online algorithm for non-oblivious multi-armed bandits, maintaining the same sample complexity. Next, we extend GDRO to address heterogeneous distributions that contain outliers. In such a scenario, we propose to optimize the average top-$k$ risk instead of the maximum risk, thereby mitigating the impact of outlier distributions. Similar to the case of vanilla GDRO, we develop two stochastic approaches: one uses $m$ samples per iteration via SMD, and the other consumes $k$ samples per iteration through SMD and an online algorithm for non-oblivious combinatorial semi-bandits. Moreover, we propose anytime versions of the proposed algorithms, which can return solutions at any time without predefining the number of iterations.

title: A Multi-temporal-scale Framework for Estimating Sub-Daily Soil Moisture by Fusing AMSR2 and SMAP Datasets_supp1-3703167.docx
authors: Peilin Song
publish: N/A
abstract: Accurate sub-daily soil moisture (SM) monitoring is critical for hydrological modeling, weather forecasting, and water resource management. However, current satellite missions such as SMAP and AMSR2 offer limited temporal resolution (≤2 passes/day), hindering detection of rapid SM changes. Multisensor fusion is often complicated by differences in overpass times, frequency bands, and retrieval algorithms. Although neural networks offer new opportunities, most existing approaches are constrained by temporal misalignment and limited regional adaptability. To address these challenges, we developed a deep learning framework that fuses SMAP and AMSR2 observations through a ten-day-scale training and daily-scale prediction design. This setup leverages the stability of SM patterns over tenday periods to mitigate temporal mismatches, while preserving intraday dynamics during prediction. The model was trained on multi-channel AMSR2 brightness temperatures and matched SMAP data (2015-2024), and then used to estimate daily SM from AMSR2 overpasses (01:30/13:30 local time). These estimates are combined with SMAP retrievals (06:00/18:00 local time) to generate a fused product with up to four daily observations. Validation confirms that the fused product maintains SMAP-level accuracy, with its in-situ validation ubRMSE differing from SMAP’s by 0.006 cm³/cm³, while improving temporal completeness and better capturing sub-daily variability. It effectively fills SMAP’s observation gaps and smooths long-term time series, enhancing its utility for time-sensitive applications such as flood early warning and drought monitoring. The framework is readily extensible to other sensors (e.g., SMOS, FY-3 series), supporting the development of a virtual satellite constellation for global, high-frequency SM monitoring.

title: Computationally Efficient Active Distributed Tracking via Dynamic Grid-Based Mutual Information Planning
authors: Jianing Ma, Xiaolei Li, Jiange Wang, Heling Yuan, Xu Fang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a computationally efficient active distributed tracking framework that leverages a dynamic gridbased mutual information approximation strategy. Building upon adaptive resolution-variant grid representations, the proposed algorithm transforms the complex entropy integration into linear matrix operations rather than expensive density estimation, thereby achieving real-time look-ahead planning capabilities. Unlike conventional Gaussian approximations that fail in nonlinear scenarios or particle-based methods burdened by intensive computational complexity, our algorithm enables analytical computation of information gain directly from the discrete grid structure, preserving non-Gaussian features without the heavy computational penalty. For active sensor control, we develop a multi-objective optimization mechanism that integrates information gain with physical constraints, effectively circumventing the trade-off between estimation precision and navigational safety. Comprehensive target tracking simulations demonstrate: 1) robust tracking accuracy in clutter and non-linear environments; 2) orders-of-magnitude reduction in planning time suitable for low-cost hardware; and 3) linear communication complexity scaling with network size via compressed grid exchange.

title: Adaptive Disturbance Estimation for Discrete-Time Nonlinear HOFA Systems Based on RBF Neural Network
authors: Shan Qiao, Kai-Xin Cui
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the tracking control problem for discrete-time nonlinear high-order fully actuated system (HOFAS) subject to unknown but bounded nonlinear disturbances, leveraging the fully actuated system theory. First, a general discrete-time HOFAS with unknown nonlinear disturbances is formulated. Second, an adaptive radial basis function (RBF) neural network is employed to approximate the unknown disturbances, and an adaptive law for the RBF network weights is derived based on the constructed Lyapunov function. Furthermore, a high-order disturbance observer is designed to enhance disturbance rejection. Through rigorous Lyapunov-based stability analysis, it is proven that the closed-loop system is uniformly ultimately bounded (UUB). Finally, the proposed control strategy is applied to address disturbance tracking problems in a second-order fully actuated system (FAS). Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed approach.

title: Dueling Double Deep Q-Network Based Energy Management Strategy for Hybrid Electric Vehicles
authors: Fan Li, Xuefang Li, Xiao-Dong Li
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In hybrid electric vehicles (HEVs), deep reinforcement learning (DRL) based energy management strategies often suffer from unstable training and poor charge-sustaining performance due to Q-value overestimation and limited value function representation. To address this issue, we propose D3QN (Dueling Double DQN)-an enhanced DQN algorithm that combines dueling and double architectures to improve learning stability and policy reliability. The method achieves more efficient power allocation, better fuel economy, and tighter SoC regulation with reduced variance compared to standard DQN. Its effectiveness is validated through simulations under diverse driving cycles and benchmarked against existing approaches.

title: Adaptive Prescribed-Time Control for Main Drive System of the Rolling Mill Based on the Fas Approach
authors: Peng Wang, Cheng Qian, Lingchen Zhu, Liuliu Zhang, Changchun Hua
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To address the issues of torsional vibration, parameter uncertainties, and nonlinear friction in the main drive system of cold rolling mills, this paper proposes an adaptive prescribed-time control strategy based on the Fully Actuated System (FAS) approach. First, a two-mass dynamic model of the main drive system incorporating nonlinear damping and unknown friction torque is established based on the Lagrangian equation and transformed into a FAS form. Second, a prescribed performance function is introduced to constrain the tracking error, ensuring that the error converges to a predetermined steady-state region within a finite time, independent of initial conditions. Meanwhile, adaptive control techniques are employed to estimate unknown parameters online, and a first-order filter is utilized to resolve the “explosion of complexity” problem inherent in traditional backstepping design. Lyapunov stability analysis proves that all signals in the closed-loop system are globally bounded, and the tracking errors asymptotically converge to zero. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy effectively suppresses torsional vibration caused by flexible coupling and achieves high-precision speed tracking even in the presence of nonlinear damping and unknown friction disturbances.

title: Adaptive Multi-Plan Semantic Graph Task Planning for Embodied Robots
authors: Rui Sun, Lei Lyu, Yifan Meng, Yubo Wu, Mengyang Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Robot task planning for domestic environments requires balancing semantic generalization capabilities with adaptability to dynamic object states. However, existing large language model-based planners typically generate solutions lacking explicit modelling of sub-task dependencies and object state transitions, resulting in limited adaptability to environmental changes. To address this, this paper proposes an Adaptive Multimodal Semantic Graph Task Planning (AMM-GTP) method. This framework integrates multi-solution task generation, semantic fusion, and state-aware graph optimization techniques. Specifically, the system first generates and fuses multiple candidate task planning schemes through the Multi-Plan Semantic Fusion (MPF) module to extract key objects and sub-goals. Subsequently, the State-Aware Structured Task Graph (S-DAG) module organizes these sub-goals into a state-constrained directed acyclic graph (DAG), enabling explicit modelling of task dependencies and object state transitions. Finally, the Local Semantic Replacement Module (SSR), based on semantic similarity, performs local semantic substitutions upon execution failure to avoid full re-planning. Comprehensive experimental validation demonstrates that this approach outperforms baseline solutions in task completion efficiency, environmental adaptability, and adjustment capability, confirming its practical deployment potential in dynamic domestic settings.

title: QP-Based Disturbed Transition-Process Safety Gain Assignment for Nonlinear Systems
authors: Kexin Hou, Xiaoming Su, Adiya Bao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the safety gain assignment problem for a class of nonlinear control-affine systems subject to external inputs. A control law is designed to ensure that the system under consideration achieves the desired safety gains. First, a trajectory-oriented notion of disturbed transition-process safety is introduced, and the corresponding safety gains are defined. On this basis, a safety filter in the form of quadratic programming (QP) is applied to enforce safety constraints while preserving nominal control performance as much as possible. Meanwhile, the feedback control law induced by the QP is proven to be bounded and locally Lipschitz continuous on a given compact set, thereby ensuring the forward completeness of the closed-loop system. Finally, by imposing additional constraints on the control law, the closed-loop system is guaranteed to achieve disturbed transition-process safety with the prescribed safety gains. The proposed constructive approach provides an effective tool for the safety control of interconnected systems.

title: Dielectric Sensor Based on a Nonlinear Split-Ring Resonator With Hysteresis-Boundary Tracking
authors: Hanaa El Moudden, Camilo Moncada, Franco Ramírez, Almudena Suárez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: We investigate a dielectric sensor based on a nonlinear split-ring resonator (SRR) loaded with a pair of unbiased varactor diodes. The resonator behaves as a one-port network and can exhibit a reflected-power minimum that is deeper and narrower than that observed under linear operation. Because the resonance frequency depends on the input amplitude, the response exhibits hysteresis versus the input frequency. Sensing can therefore rely either on the shift of the reflected-power minimum or on the shifts of the two infinite-slope points that bound the hysteresis cycle. As experimentally verified, these points exhibit sufficient repeatability to enable improved discrimination compared with linear sensors, whose performance is limited by the maximum finite slope of the resonance curve. When the input power varies, the relationship between hysteresis width and central frequency changes with the material under test (MUT). This behavior arises from the geometry of the bifurcation locus in the frequency-input-power plane and may be useful in applications where the received power is unknown. The sensor is analyzed using a driven control function that, unlike conventional harmonic balance, allows the response to be traced through the turning points of the resonance curve. Experimentally, the nonlinear regime increases the equivalent quality factor of the reflected-power minimum from about 153 in linear operation to more than 1350, while the hysteresis-boundary frequencies shift on the order of 1.1–1.6 MHz, depending on the input power level, from zero to one hole of diameter 0.3 mm, drilled in RO4003C. The standard deviation of the extracted hysteresis-boundary frequencies remains below 0.28 MHz over 20 repeated measurements.

title: A Method for Wind Deflection Fault Probability Assessment Based on Improved Monte Carlo
authors: Chaoqiang Miao, Nan Yang, Yiming Zhao, Yunliang Quan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Wind-induced corona discharge (WICD) is a critical factor affecting the safe operation of transmission lines during strong wind events, with its occurrence probability closely related to wind speed magnitude and wind direction distribution. Traditional methods based on average wind speed or simple statistical approaches struggle to characterize extreme wind conditions and wind-speed-direction coupling relationships, leading to conservative or distorted risk assessment results. To address these issues, this paper develops an improved Monte Carlo-based WICD risk assessment method using ERA5 reanalysis wind field data. The method employs weighted processing of high-speed wind samples to highlight the dominant role of extreme wind conditions in risk assessment. Simultaneously, a joint sampling strategy for wind speed and direction is adopted during stochastic simulations to maintain physical consistency with historical wind fields. Additionally, a statistical distribution-based dominant wind direction identification method is introduced to enhance the stability and engineering interpretability of wind deflection angle evaluation. Simulation analysis using real-world power grid regions demonstrates that the proposed method more accurately reflects WICD occurrence characteristics under high-risk wind conditions, providing effective support for transmission line operation risk assessment and disaster prevention decision-making.

title: Adaptive Neural Network Voltage Regulation for DGUs with Unknown ZIP Loads: A Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Peng Wang, Minrui Fei, Dajun Du, Yangjie Chen
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the voltage regulation problem for distributed generation units (DGUs) in DC microgrids subject to renewable energy fluctuations and unknown nonlinear ZIP loads. To address the complexity of system dynamics, the DGU model is first transformed into a fully actuated system (FAS) representation, which serves as the foundation for the subsequent controller design. Based on this framework, an adaptive neural network voltage regulation control strategy is proposed. Radial basis function neural networks (RBFNNs) are utilized to approximate the unknown nonlinear dynamics induced by ZIP loads, while specific adaptive laws are synthesized to estimate the unknown control gain and approximation error bounds online. Rigorous theoretical analysis based on Lyapunov stability theory guarantees that all signals in the closed-loop system remain bounded and that the output voltage tracks the reference trajectory asymptotically. Finally, numerical simulations are conducted to validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed control scheme under time-varying source and load conditions.

title: Fault Detection Based on AET-WTOD Protocol for Singularly Perturbed Systems over Gilbert-Elliott Channels
authors: Caihua Li, Tiantian Wu, Wenxin Zhang, Zhihui Wu, Yan Gao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper focuses on the fault detection (FD) problem for a class of nonlinear singularly perturbed systems (SPSs) over Gilbert-Elliott (GE) channels, where the adaptive event-triggered weight try-once-discard (AET-WTOD) protocol is proposed to determine the transmission of data. Specifically, an asynchronous fault detection filter (FDF) based on the transmitted information is designed with the aid of the hidden Markov model (HMM). Subsequently, by constructing the Lyapunov function that depends on the singular perturbation parameter (SPP) and channel mode, sufficient conditions are obtained to ensure that the residual dynamic system is stochastically stable (SS) and satisfies the prescribed <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$H_{\infty}$</tex> performance. In this case, the FDF gains are derived by means of the linear matrix inequality method. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed FD method is verified through a simulation.

title: IVEC 2026 Preface
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)

title: Fully Actuated Control for Synchronous Generator Excitation
authors: Yi Yu, Yi Huang, Levente Kovács, Guangren Duan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Low-frequency electromechanical oscillations pose a persistent threat to the stability and transmission capacity of modern power systems. While conventional power system stabilizers based on linear control theory are widely used, their performance often degrades under varying operating conditions due to the inherent nonlinearity of synchronous generators. Advanced nonlinear control strategies, particularly feedback linearization, offer theoretical global stability but suffer from control singularities at specific operating points, such as the no-load condition where the power angle approaches zero. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a robust nonlinear power system stabilizers design framework based on the fully actuated system theory. We derive a third-order fully actuated model for the single machine infinite bus system, explicitly establishing the relationship between the excitation voltage and the jerk of the power angle. Based on this model, a nonlinear control law is synthesized to ensure the asymptotic convergence of the power angle to its equilibrium. A rigorous stability analysis is presented, characterizing the region of attraction and proving that the closed-loop system is globally asymptotically stable for all initial states excluding the singularity itself. Furthermore, we analytically demonstrate that the singularity at the no-load equilibrium is removable, ensuring that the control input remains bounded and converges to a finite steady-state value. This approach effectively integrates the rigorous stability guarantees of nonlinear control with the implementation simplicity essential for practical power system operations.

title: Design and Development of a Field Emission Based Magnetron Using 3-D Conformal Finite-Difference Time-Domain Particle-in-Cell Simulations for Industrial Applications
authors: Kaviya Aranganadin, Hua-Yi Hsu, Ming-Chieh Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This study advances the research and development of field emission-based rising-sun magnetrons by transitioning from 2-D particle-in-cell (PIC) simulations to a fully 3-D design using conformal finite-difference time-domain (CFDTD) PIC methods. The 2-D CFDTD PIC simulations, which achieved an efficiency of $\sim 80 \%$ with a linear anode current density of 75.67 A/m under an applied voltage of 5.2 kV and a static magnetic field of 0.235 T, form the foundation for the 3-D implementation. The same voltage and magnetic field conditions are applied to the 3D model, which incorporates a coaxial output port to realize the optimized 2-D design in a realistic 3-D geometry. The rising-sun magnetron, featuring a cavity height of 10 cm, has been adapted for operation at the target frequency of 2.45 GHz. This work demonstrates the feasibility of transitioning from 2-D to 3-D designs while highlighting practical advantages of incorporating coaxial output port and reducing fabrication complexity.

title: Learning Domain-Invariant Features Through Channel-Level Sparsification for Out-of Distribution Generalization
authors: Haoran Pei, Yuguang Yang, Kexin Liu, Juan Zhang, Baochang Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Out-of-Distribution (OOD) generalization has become a primary metric for evaluating image analysis systems. Since deep learning models tend to capture domain-specific context, they often develop shortcut dependencies on these non-causal features, leading to inconsistent performance across different data sources. Current techniques, such as invariance learning, attempt to mitigate this. However, they struggle to isolate highly mixed features within deep latent spaces. This limitation prevents them from fully resolving the shortcut learning problem. In this paper, we propose Hierarchical Causal Dropout (HCD), a method that uses channel-level causal masks to enforce feature sparsity. This approach allows the model to separate causal features from spurious ones, effectively performing a causal intervention at the representation level. The training is guided by a Matrix-based Mutual Information (MMI) objective to minimize the mutual information between latent features and domain labels, while simultaneously maximizing the information shared with class labels. To ensure stability, we incorporate a StyleMixdriven VICReg module, which prevents the masks from accidentally filtering out essential causal data. Experimental results on OOD benchmarks show that HCD performs better than existing top-tier methods.

title: Fully Actuated System Approach for Effective Control of Superheated Steam Temperature Control Systems
authors: Wenyang Xu, Xuefei Yang, Kai Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Under the framework of high-order fully actuated (HOFA) system theory, this article investigates the control of superheated steam temperature in thermal power units which aims to stabilize the superheated steam temperature at setpoint. Using the fully actuated property, we derive the HOFA system mathematically from the rigorously developed mechanistic model of the superheated steam temperature system. Based on the HOFA framework, we design a control law to achieve a linear closed-loop system, enabling eigenstructure assignment for desired performance. The simulations demonstrate that the HOFAbased controller rapidly regulates the temperature with minimal overshoot, small steady-state error, and a small settling time. Comparative studies with conventional PID control highlight the superiority of the HOFA approach in handling nonlinearity and large inertia, significantly reducing settling time while improving stability.

title: GPU-Accelerated 3D Particle-in-Cell Simulation Algorithm for Pierce Electron Gun Design
authors: Shaojun Wen, Shenglong Guo, Wenjin Cai, Yong Xu, Tao Huang, Xiaolin Jin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Under complex scenarios such as high current density and precise beam control in the Pierce electron gun, the large scale of grid cells and particle numbers leads to low computational efficiency when using the Particle-in-Cell (PIC) algorithm for simulation. This paper develops a threedimensional PIC simulation algorithm accelerated by a Graphics Processing Unit (GPU). The algorithm integrates two core steps-particle pushing and electric field interpolation-to reduce memory access frequency and improve data utilization efficiency, thereby enhancing computational performance. Using the Pierce electron gun as a test case, the correctness and efficiency of the parallel algorithm are verified. The speedup factor of the parallel algorithm relative to the serial algorithm is tested, demonstrating satisfactory acceleration performance. This provides a reliable solution for high-efficiency simulation of the Pierce electron guns.

title: Broadband tunable narrow-linewidth laser based on scattering-enhanced fiber covering E-S-C-L bands
authors: Minzhi Xu, Zechun Geng, Da Wei, Yujia Li, Juntao He, Chaoze Zhang, Wei Du, Lei Gao, Leilei Shi, Ligang Huang, Jindong Wang, Guolu Yin, Tao Zhu
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: This work demonstrates a broadband tunable narrow-linewidth laser based on scattering-enhanced fiber, cov ering the E-S-C-L wavelength bands from 1337.47 nm to 1631.39 nm, with a total tuning span of 293.92 nm. The laser employs two semiconductor optical amplifiers (SOAs) centered at 1420 nm and 1550 nm, which are connected into a single ring resonator via polarization multiplexing. Wavelength selection and tunability is realized using an ultra-broadband tunable filter based on a blazed grating. To suppress side longitude modes, an 18-meter long fiber with enhanced random scattering is utilized inside the cavity as a feedback medium, yielding an output linewidths between 1.54 kHz and 2.61 kHz. Benefited from the fast response of the galvanometer mirror and short relaxation time of SOAs, wavelength switching time is less than 1 ms under different tuning channels among the wavelength range of near 300 nm. The stable single-longitude-mode operation is maintained across the entire tuning range. The exceptionally broad tuning range and high spectral purity of the laser endow it with significant application potentials across a wide range of fields.

title: LongTest: Test Prioritization for Long Text Files
authors: Xueqi Dang, Yinghua Li, Wendkûuni C. Ouédraogo, Maxime Cordy, Mike Papadakis, Jacques Klein, Tegawendé F. Bissyandé, Yves Le Traon
publish: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract: Text classification is a fundamental task in natural language processing (NLP) that involves assigning predefined labels to text files. While existing research has primarily focused on short texts (e.g., social media posts), the classification of long texts, such as legal documents and medical records, has gained increasing attention due to the rising demand for analyzing complex and information-rich documents. However, testing long text classification systems poses unique challenges due to the high manual labeling costs associated with annotating test inputs (long text files). Test input prioritization has emerged as a promising strategy to mitigate this challenge, which prioritizes potentially misclassified tests during the early stages of the testing process. By labeling these inputs earlier, it accelerates the debugging process and thus improves the efficiency of DNN testing. However, applying existing methods to prioritize long text files is constrained by several factors: 1) Confidence-based methods fail to leverage the rich semantic content of the test inputs (long text files); 2) mutation-based techniques are specifically designed for short texts, introducing small mutations that have negligible impacts on long text files; and 3) coverage-based methods are computationally expensive and less effective. To overcome these limitations, we propose LongTest, a novel test input prioritization approach specifically designed for long text files. LongTest introduces: 1) an embedding generation mechanism specifically designed to enhance the capture of information from the entire long text file, and 2) contrastive learning that enables more effective differentiation between misclassified and correctly classified samples, which optimizes the test prioritization process. The results demonstrate that LongTest outperforms all the compared test prioritization approaches. Specifically, compared with the existing test prioritization methods (i.e., DeepGini, VanillaSM, PCS, Entropy, and DATIS), LongTest achieves an average improvement of 9.61%~16.31%.

title: Octopus-Inspired Embodied Tactile Intelligence: A Universally Calibrated Miniature Vision-Based Suction Cup_supp1-3694100.mp4
authors: Rob B.N. Scharff
publish: N/A
abstract: The integration of high-resolution tactile sensors in suction cups is a key requirement for the realization of octopus-inspired control strategies in soft continuum robotic arms. However, existing solutions lack simultaneous force and orientation detection and either provide insufficient resolution or are too bulky for integration into soft continuum robotic arms. Moreover, inconsistent manufacturing often necessitates labor-intensive individual calibration for each suction cup. This article introduces a miniature suction cup (22 mm × 40 mm) with integrated vision-based detection of pressing force and contact orientation. Through consistent multimaterial additive manufacturing and a robust model architecture trained on augmented, cross-device datasets, this predictive model can be universally applied across devices of identical design, thus eliminating the need for individual calibration of each suction cup. High-resolution and high-accuracy orientation [pitch/roll mean absolute error (MAE): 1.43/1.45 degrees] and force detection (Fx/Fy/Fz MAE: 0.254/0.256/0.763 N) enable closed-loop, perpendicular approaches for optimal sealing. Real-world tests confirm robust handling of small, delicate, misaligned, and curved objects. These results represent an important step toward scalable, high-resolution tactile sensing for octopus-inspired soft robotic manipulation.

title: Data-Driven Soft Sensor Modeling via BLS-Based Algorithm Recommendation
authors: Kaixun He, Huadong Yuan, Yiting Song, Zihua Wang, Yaning Zhai, Pengfei Cao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Data-driven soft sensors have been widely utilized in complex industrial processes for the online prediction of difficult-to-measure variables. In the construction of soft sensor models, model selection is a crucial step; however, existing approaches are predominantly based on manual experience and trial-and-error strategies, which are time-consuming and labor-intensive. To address these limitations, a model recommendation method based on a broad learning system is proposed in this study. First, a feature mining technique tailored to industrial time-series data is designed, whereby recommendation features that are independent of process mechanisms yet strongly indicative of model performance are extracted. Second, a broad learning system is employed to establish a mapping between the extracted data features and the performance metrics of candidate models, thereby facilitating rapid model recommendation under target operating conditions. Finally, the soft sensor is constructed using the recommended model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method is effective and exhibits considerable potential for application to complex industrial data.

title: Prescribed-Time Output Feedback Stabilization of Uncertain Nonholonomic Systems With Measurement Faults
authors: Xiu You, Peng Chang, Yi Ma, Da Wang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: A prescribed-time output feedback control scheme is proposed for a general class of uncertain nonholonomic systems. Unlike conventional approaches that assume ideal output measurements, this paper proposes a more realistic scenario that asymmetric and bounded multiplicative measurement noise. By introducing a time-varying gain state transformation, this class of uncertain nonholonomic systems is reformulated as an uncertain linear time-varying control systems. Then by fully exploiting the properties of parametric Lyapunov equations, a time-varying high-gain output feedback controller is designed. And by constructing time-varying Lyapunov-like functions, the states and controller are proven to converge to zero at any prescribed time. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed scheme is verified through numerical simulation.

title: Research on 3D Cooperative Guidance Law Based on TRPO Method Under Multi-Constraint Conditions
authors: Ge Lan, Runle Du, Xiaojun Ban
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Aiming at the requirements of time consistency and impact angle for loitering munition swarms in cooperative strike missions, this paper first proposes a time-varying desired formation, transforming the guidance problem with multiple constraints into a cooperative tracking problem of the desired formation. To enable the swarm to reach the constrained state and strike the target under the guidance of the desired formation, a virtual leader is set for the swarm, and an intelligent guidance policy based on the Trust Region Policy Optimization (TRPO) algorithm is designed for the virtual leader, which ensures that the virtual leader can hit the target accurately under the impact angle constraint. The time-varying desired formation is composed of the state vectors of each member in the swarm relative to the virtual leader, where the direction of each vector is determined by the expected impact angle, and the modulus of all vectors converges to 0 at the moment when the virtual leader hits the target. The cooperative guidance command proposed for the swarm to track the time-varying desired formation will drive the linear system constituted by the swarm tracking errors to be stable. The results of numerical simulation show that the swarm tracking error always converges during the guidance process, which ensures that the swarm can hit the target at the expected impact angle simultaneously.

title: PPO Based Deep Reinforcement Learning Control of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles
authors: Bo Chen, Hao Wu, Baoyu Zhou, Yanling Shang, Fangzheng Gao
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To enhance the autonomous target tracking and control capability of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) in complex dynamic environments, this paper proposes a control strategy based on deep reinforcement learning. The target tracking task is modeled as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), in which the state space, action space, and reward function are carefully designed. A deep reinforcement learning algorithm is employed to train the control policy, and its performance is validated in a simulation environment. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can achieve stable target tracking and obstacle avoidance under different initial positions and target motion patterns, while exhibiting good generalization capability.

title: Adaptive Fuzzy Optimal Tracking Control for Multi-Input Systems
authors: Guangxin Shao, Yongfeng Lv, Yu Tang, Gengwu Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper designs an adaptive optimal prescribed performance controller based on the generalized fuzzy hyperbolic model (GFHM). The scheme employs the GFHM as a critic estimator, with the core goal of confining tracking errors within a bounded set while achieving adaptive optimal tracking. Firstly, a performance index related to tracking errors and energy consumption is established, and a general optimal tracking control framework for multi-input nonlinear systems is constructed based on adaptive dynamic programming. The designed optimal tracking controller consists of two parts: the steady-state part can be directly solved according to the tracking signal and system dynamic characteristics; the adaptive feedback part is updated via the generalized fuzzy hyperbolic critic network (GFHCN) based on the prescribed performance constraints. Finally, the stability of the controlled system is verified through Lyapunov stability theory analysis, and simulation verification is carried out using a dual-drive servo system as an example.

title: Study of the Core Oscillation Method for Ka Band High Efficiency Sheet Beam EIK
authors: Yuekai Yin, Jianxun Wang, Xinjie Li, Yixin Wan, Zihao Dai, Yong Luo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A Ka-band Sheet Beam Extended Interaction Klystron (SBEIK) employing the Core Oscillation Method (COM) is presented, which maximizes electronic efficiency by facilitating a bunching-then-debunching oscillation that effectively consolidates peripheral electrons into a central bunch for enhanced energy extraction. Compared to traditional klystron designs, which often struggle with efficiency in small configurations, this design operates at $35 \mathrm{GHz}(35 \mathrm{kV}, 3.5 \mathrm{~A})$ and leverages COM to maximize bunching efficiency within a restricted space. 3D PIC simulations demonstrate a peak output power of 40.1 kW and $32.7 \%$ efficiency with a gain of 56.3 dB within a total interaction length of only 85 mm. These results indicate that the proposed SBEIK is a robust solution for high-power applications subject to strict spatial constraints, and subsequent experiments will be conducted to validate the design.

title: The Analysis of the Sensitivity of Nonlinear Characteristics for Ku-band Space TWT
authors: Xinwen Shang, Wenkai Deng, Jianwei Li, Xiaodong Cui, Bing Han, Yanwei Li, Liu Xiao, Qiang Li, Yongzheng Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: The sensitivity of the nonlinear characteristics of the Ku-band space traveling-wave tube (TWT) is investigated in this paper. By analyzing the nonlinear behaviors of different pitch segments of the helix, a nonlinear characteristic calculation model is established. The proposed model and corresponding results not only clarify the influence of helix pitch variation on TWT nonlinearity but also provide valuable references for the design of high-efficiency wide-band Ku-band space TWTs.

title: An Electron Optical System with Low Radial Magnetic Fields for Multibeam Klystrons
authors: Deyong Li, Chuanchao Wang, Shaozhe Wang, Yongming Li, Huarong Gong, Zhaoyun Duan
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A reversible periodic magnetic focusing (RPMF) system with low radial magnetic fields and a matched multibeam electron gun are designed and analyzed. The RPMF system consists of six NdFeB permanent magnets forming five successive magnetic field reversal regions, generating axially quasi-uniform longitudinal magnetic fields. Simulation results show that the peak longitudinal magnetic field is approximately $1200 G$ in the main focusing region, while it is reduced to below $1000 G$ in the first region. The radial magnetic field component remains below $25 G$, accounting for less than $2 \%$ of the longitudinal field. A multibeam electron gun operating in a weak magnetic field environment of about $162 G$ is designed to suppress initial transverse beam expansion, thereby improving the beam transport stability. CST co-simulations demonstrate stable beam generation and transport, achieving a total beam current of $140 A$ with 7 A per beam at a cathode temperature of $1050^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ and a current density of $13.55 \mathrm{~A} / \mathrm{cm}^{2}$. These results provide a basis for the ongoing multibeam metamaterial-inspired klystron development.

title: Soft Actor-Critic Reinforcement Learning for Focusing Electrode Optimization in Sheet Beam Electron Gun*
authors: Hao Li, Jianxun Wang, Chenrui Wei, Zhenyu Cui, Yixin Wan, Wei Jiang, Guo Liu, Yong Luo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper introduces the optimization of focusing electrode spline curves in sheet beam electron guns using Soft Actor-Critic reinforcement learning. The proposed method employs a modular reward system and historical data pre-training to effectively balance exploration and exploitation. The experimental results demonstrate $67.6 \%$ average fitness improvement across five starting points, outperforming random perturbation by $50.4 \%$, showing that the algorithm can effectively learn parameter adjustment rules, replacing the fuzzy control method for spline curve optimization.

title: Duty-Cycle-Dependent Emission of M-Type Cathodes for Gyrotron Traveling-Wave Tubes
authors: Xinyue Wang, Han Wei, Qiang Zheng, Yong Luo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Impregnated dispenser cathodes are key electron sources for high-power millimeter-wave gyrotron traveling-wave tubes (gyro-TWTs), where duty-cycle capability strongly affects electron-gun operating margin and reliability. In this work, the duty-cycle-dependent emission performance of an M-type impregnated cathode is experimentally investigated. Emission measurements are performed using a close-spaced parallel-plate diode (CSPPD) and the magnetron injection gun (MIG) of a Ka-band gyro-TWT. The results show that increasing duty cycle leads to a systematic reduction in emission current under both test configurations. Although higher cathode temperature enhances the emission level, it cannot fully compensate for degradation at high duty cycles. These results demonstrate that duty-cycle control is a critical parameter for electron-gun design and stable operation of high-power millimeter-wave gyro-TWTs.

title: V- and E-band Millimeter Wave Traveling Wave Tubes employing Additive Manufacturing
authors: Larry Sadwick, Jennifer Hwu, Tomas Gutierrez, Yun-Jan Hwu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: We report V- and E-band Millimeter Wave Traveling Wave Tubes employing Additive Manufacturing. We have performed 3D ceramic printing and metallization of the 3D printed ceramics through additive manufacturing. We have also characterized the electrical and thermal properties of these additive manufactured layers and structures. The results including 3D printed assembly components and V- and E-band TWTs will be presented and discussed in this paper.

title: Prescribed-Time Unknown-Input Observer Design for Linear Systems with Arbitrary Relative Degrees
authors: Haifang Li, Yunxia Song
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper presents a prescribed-time unknown-input observer design for linear systems with arbitrary relative degrees. We develop a novel observer structure that guarantees exact state estimation within a user-defined finite time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T$</tex> by incorporating a periodic time-delay gain and leveraging higher-order output information. The proposed approach overcomes the limitations of traditional observer matching conditions, which restrict the relative degrees to unity, thereby extending the applicability to a broader class of systems. Through a systematic transformation, the observer is reformulated into a derivative-free implementation, enhancing its practical feasibility. Theoretical analysis establishes the convergence properties, demonstrating that the estimation error converges to zero precisely at the prescribed time <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$T$</tex> regardless of initial conditions.

title: Nonlinear Control for Boost Converter: A Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Lin Liu, Guang-Ren Duan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a fully actuated system (FAS) approach for controlling a boost converter. The converter's averaged model is first transformed into a sub-FAS form. Based on this model, a standard controller is designed to regulate the output voltage to a desired reference. Furthermore, the feasibility set and the corresponding region of exponential attraction (RoEA) are derived. The effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through simulations, which include a graphical representation of the RoEA.

title: Design and Optimization of a Magnetron Injection Gun for a G-Band Gyro-TWT
authors: Taotao Mao, Zhi Yi, Tao Song, Chenghui Zhu, Yuxuan Chai, Kaichun Zhang, Zhenhua Wu, Wei Wang, Diwei Liu, Chen Zhang, Xu Qi, Jiao Jiao, Na Yao, Yanyu Wei, Yubin Gong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: In this paper, a double-anode magnetron injection gun (MIG) is designed and optimized for a 220 GHz TE02 mode gyrotron traveling-wave tube (gyro-TWT) to meet the requirements of high-power terahertz sources. Simulation results demonstrate that the designed MIG operates stably at a beam voltage of 50 kV and current of 3 A. A high-quality electron beam is achieved with a pitch factor of 1.1 and a transverse velocity spread of approximately $\mathbf{2 \%}$.

title: Event-Triggered PI-Funnel Distributed Control with Neural Networks for Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Yu-Tong Hu, Ze-Yun Hu, Cui-Hua Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes an event-triggered neural network-based PI-funnel distributed control scheme for cooperative manipulators with uncertain Lagrangian dynamics and prescribed-performance requirements. A fixed-threshold event-triggered mechanism is embedded to reduce communication and computation costs while maintaining closed-loop performance, with control updates only when a predefined condition is violated. A global PI-funnel confines tracking errors within user-defined time-varying bounds; neural networks online approximate unknown nonlinear dynamics, and projection-based learning laws ensure bounded parameter estimates. Lyapunov analysis verifies bounded closed-loop signals, satisfied performance constraints, and Zeno-free behavior. Simulations confirm accurate tracking with significantly reduced control update frequency for cooperative manipulation tasks.

title: DRL-Driven Optimization for Beyond Diagonal-RIS Enhanced Backscatter NOMA-ISAC Networks
authors: Muhammad Danish Khattak, Muhammad Ayaan Qasmi, Yousuf Rehan, Ali Arshad Nasir, Haejoon Jung, Syed Ali Hassan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Sixth-generation (6G) wireless networks must address the unprecedented demand for ultra-dense connectivity, ubiquitous communication, and integrated sensing and communication (ISAC). This paper presents a novel architecture that integrates backscatter communication (BackCom), ISAC, non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA), and beyond-diagonal reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (BD-RIS) into a unified optimization framework. A dedicated continuous-wave transmitter illuminates the environment, exciting the passive backscatter nodes (BNs), which modulate information onto the reflected signal while simultaneously enabling energy harvesting (EH). The joint optimization of BD-RIS phase shifts, NOMA power allocation, and backscatter reflection coefficients is formulated as a nonconvex problem and solved via a two-agent sequential deep reinforcement learning (DRL) architecture: agent 1 (RIS–NOMA controller) optimizes wideband resource allocation, while agent 2 (EH manager) manages battery sustainability under a frozen agent 1 policy, provably eliminating multi-agent non-stationarity. A comprehensive comparison of five DRL algorithms including twin delayed deep deterministic policy gradient (TD3), soft actor-critic (SAC), truncated quantile critics (TQC), proximal policy optimization (PPO), and advantage actor-critic (A2C), benchmarked against exhaustive search, reveals that TD3 achieves near-optimal sum rate and near-minimum Cramér–Rao-bounded angle estimation error with more than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5000\times$</tex-math></inline-formula> lower inference latency than exhaustive search. The framework demonstrates robustness to phase quantization, phase noise, and CSI uncertainty, confirming its suitability for practical 6G deployments.

title: DIAMOND: Diffusion-Augmented Imitation Learning for Motion Planning in Autonomous Driving
authors: Wenxiao Ke, Wenjie Lu, Yi Xiao, Liang Hu, Xiao Li, Yanfu Zhang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Imitation-based planners for autonomous driving are prone to error accumulation due to the lack of recovery trajectories for out-of-distribution scenarios in expert datasets. This limitation, further compounded by the causal confusion problem, fundamentally constrains their performance in interactive, closed-loop driving environments. To tackle these challenges, we propose Diffusion-Augmented Imitation Learning for Motion Planning in Autonomous Driving (DIAMOND). DIAMOND leverages a diffusion-based data augmentation strategy to synthesize realistic and interaction-rich scenarios that lie off the expert data distribution, which occurred from the rollouts of an early-stage imitation policy in closed-loop environments. This data augmentation strategy enables DIAMOND to learn recovery behaviors from unseen states, thereby mitigating distribution shift. Furthermore, explicit causal auxiliary losses are incorporated to address causal confusion. Evaluated on the nuPlan benchmark, DIAMOND makes significant improvement in route completion rates <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(+13.4 \%)$</tex>. DIAMOND achieves the state-of-the-art closed-loop driving performance in interactive driving scenarios among imitation-based planners. The code is available at https://github.com/asddgfhzvxc/DIAMOND.

title: Author Index
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)

title: Fully Actuated Decoupled Sequential Convex Optimization for UAV Formation Reconfiguration
authors: Yang Gejun, Wang Xinhua, Sun Xiaoyun
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a Fully Actuated Decoupled Sequential Convex Programming (FA-DSCP) method for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) formation reconfiguration in complex environments. The Koopman operator is employed to approximate nonlinear dynamics into a linear prediction model, achieving the convexification of dynamic constraints. The decoupling strategy based on nominal trajectories is introduced to decompose the large-scale centralized optimization problem into multiple independent convex sub-problems that can be solved in parallel, effectively overcoming the curse of dimensionality. Compared with the existing methods, numerical simulations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method, generating collision-free and smooth trajectories under the dynamic constraints, while significantly improving computational efficiency and spatial utilization.

title: Observer-Based Reinforcement Learning Secure Control for Discrete-Time Nonstrict-Feedback Systems Under Deception Attacks
authors: Zhengwen Xie, Haibo Gu, Deyuan Liu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the optimal secure control problem for discrete-time nonstrict-feedback nonlinear systems subject to deception attacks based on the reinforcement learning (RL) framework. To address the challenge where malicious cyber-attacks invalidate standard coordinate transformations, an improved observer is developed to reconstruct states from the limited and compromised measurements without relying on future predictions. Subsequently, a customized system transformation of observer dynamics is derived to reformulate the observer dynamics into a cascaded form suitable for backstepping controller design. Furthermore, an observer-based optimal secure controller is designed with the transformed observer dynamics based on the actor-critic RL framework. In addition, the proposed optimal secure control strategy ensures that all signals within the closed-loop system are semiglobally uniformly ultimately bounded and the result is rigorously proven in the stability analysis. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed control strategy is verified by a numerical simulation.

title: Design and Development of Q-Band 65W Space Traveling Wave Tube
authors: Jun He, Qinglun Liu, Yuhui Sun, Linlin Cao, Haiqiang Li, Dehui Zhai, Mingguang Huang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper focuses on the design, testing, and experimentation of a Q-band helix traveling wave tube, aiming for high efficiency and high stability. By utilizing mature multi-gradient technology and composited tube shell high-frequency system, the Q-band traveling wave tube is developed. Test results indicate that within 2 GHz band, the output power is $\geq 68.8 \mathrm{~W}$, total efficiency is $\geq 48.8 \%$, and gain is $\geq 50.4 \mathrm{~dB}$. A series of mechanical and thermal environmental tests have validated that the Q-band traveling wave tube exhibits stable performance.

title: Build and Initial Testing of the 75XP4 Diode
authors: Brandon Weatherford, Erik Jongewaard, Julian Merrick, Bradley Shirley, Ann Sy
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: High power, high efficiency klystrons are an essential component of any normal-conducting $R F$ accelerator system. SLAC has restarted development of the XP series klystron, a 75MW, $60 \%$ efficient, X-band permanent magnet focused device, for deployment in a future accelerator science facility such as the Cool Copper Collider. The XP klystron was originally developed under the Next Linear Collider program, with several iterations of the device being built and tested. The latest design in the series, the 75XP4 has been finished, with several design improvements from the 75XP3, and it is currently under construction. A “diode” version of this klystron has already been completed and installed in a new high power test stand for these klystrons. This paper discusses simulations, mechanical design, and progress to date in testing the completed diode assembly.

title: MoiréMop: Lightweight and Pervasive Moiré Removal for Mobile Camera-to-Screen Interaction
authors: Zhaowei Wu, Jingyi Ning, Yanling Bu, Lei Xie
publish: Proceedings of the ACM on Interactive, Mobile, Wearable and Ubiquitous Technologies
abstract: None

title: Explainable Multimodal Modeling with KANs for Urban EV Charging Demand Forecasting
authors: Yi Zhao, Qianqian Ren, Boyuan Wang, Hui Xu
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Multimedia Retrieval

title: Beyond Post-hoc Fusion: Rethinking Cross-Modal Interaction Timing in Few-Shot Learning
authors: Liang Yang, Hongyuan Xiao, Songtao He, Ye Lin, Zhenchang Zhang
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Multimedia Retrieval
