title: Study on the Microwave Attenuation Effect in a Waveguide Plasma Limiter
authors: Shenglong Guo, Xiaolin Jin, Tao Huang, Xiaoqian Liu, Guanjie Lin, Wenjing Cai
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Plasma limiters based on the principle of gas discharge have shown great potential in protection applications against the risk of serious damage to electronic equipment caused by high power microwaves (HPM). This work investigates the limiting characteristics of a waveguide plasma limiter based on the Conformal Finite-Difference Time-Domain (CFDTD) method, the Particle-in-Cell (PIC) method, and the Monte Carlo Collision (MCC) method. The simulation model comprehensively considers the physical processes of microwave electron heating, gas breakdown under high field, electron avalanche evolution, and high-density plasma formation, and quantitatively analyzes the attenuation of microwave signals by the plasma. Simulation results indicate that under typical operating conditions with an incident microwave power of 1 MW and 0.5 Torr Argon gas filling, the limiter shows a nanosecond-level rapid response capability. The plasma began to significantly attenuate the microwave signal at 7.7 ns; subsequently, as the electron density rapidly increased, the limiter effectively limited the leakage power to below 50 W, with a maximum attenuation of $43 d B$.

title: Phase Coherence Evaluation in Array Antennas Driven by High-Power Millimeter-Wave Narrow Pulses
authors: Hanyi Li Tingjun Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Given that high-power millimeter-wave sources driven by vacuum electronic devices typically operate under narrowpulse conditions, we note that phase coherence in array antennas under narrow-pulse excitation significantly impacts direction-finding performance. This paper proposes a dual-channel intermediatefrequency equivalent measurement scheme for experimental investigation, systematically varying pulse width to analyze its effect on phase estimation performance. Experimental results demonstrate that extending the pulse width from 20 nanoseconds to 100 nanoseconds significantly reduces phase estimation variance. Nevertheless, phase stability within 0.04° is effectively maintained across both pulse widths, with wider pulses yielding superior stability. This research may provide valuable reference for designing highpower narrow-pulse millimeter-wave phased array systems and their direction-finding applications.

title: Possible Operation of a 140 GHz, ≈ 2.2 MW, CW Gyrotron
authors: M.V. Kartikeyan, M. Thumm, S. Yuvaraj, B. Ravinder, A.K. Jha
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper presents the feasibility studies and a possible operation of a $140 \mathrm{GHz}, 2.2 \mathrm{MW}, \mathrm{CW}$, gyrotron for plasma heating application in W7X-like machines. The present work includes a careful selection of the candidate modes followed by studies on mode competition, cold cavity design and initial RF-behavior computations.

title: Machinery Fault Diagnosis Based on Fusion of Event-Based Camera and Traditional Optical Camera
authors: Deming Kong, Ning Jiang, Qingqing Zhao, Chen Gao, Lifeng Ma
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Industrial fault diagnosis often relies on contact sensors, which may alter the dynamic characteristics of equipment and malfunction under harsh operating conditions such as high temperature or high pressure. To overcome these limitations, a non-contact fault diagnosis method fusing event-based and frame-based cameras is proposed. An event-triggered key frame extraction strategy is first developed: abnormal event streams captured by the event-based camera are used to precisely locate the corresponding frames in the conventional video through timestamp matching. To address motion blur and incomplete information, a low-rank regularized group sparse coding model with a novel relaxation strategy is adopted for image restoration. The region of interest is then extracted using a percentage thresholding method combined with anomaly coordinates derived from the event stream, thereby enabling accurate identification of anomalies. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves efficient and reliable fault diagnosis, providing an effective solution for non-contact visual inspection in industrial environments.

title: Optimization Design of Folded Waveguide Based on Neural Network Surrogate Model and Differential Evolution Algorithm
authors: Zhaohai Yuan, Xi Lin, Zhihao Xing, Jiasong Wang, Tao Tang, Huarong Gong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: An automated design framework is proposed in this paper: Python-controlled HFSS simulations are employed to collect the phase velocity and coupling impedance data under varied geometric configurations and frequencies, thereby establishing a comprehensive dataset. Subsequently, an artificial neural network is trained as a surrogate model to replace the computationally expensive electromagnetic simulation process. Based on this surrogate model, a constrained differential evolution algorithm is introduced to optimize the structural parameters, which ensures the matching of the normalized phase velocity while maximizing the minimum coupling impedance across the target frequency band. With an E-band folded waveguide traveling-wave tube (TWT) as the test case, simulation results demonstrate that the coupling impedance performance of the optimized structure is significantly improved in comparison with that of the initial structure.

title: The Thales TH1512: A New 117.5 GHz 1 MW Continuous-Wave Industrial Gyrotron for the DIII-D Tokamak
authors: Alberto Leggieri, Konstantinos A. Avramidis, Nikolai de Boucaud, Loannis Chelis, Francis Cometa, Alexandre Dupuy, Benjamin Eii, Lukas Feuerstein, Gerd Gantenbein, Yuri A. Gorelov, Clay Gray, Stefan Illy, Jianbo Jin, John Jelonne, François Legrand, Christophe Lievin, Gérald Lietaer, Rodolphe Marchesin, Cyrille Metayer, Moritz Misko, Perry Nesbet, David Quetel, Michael P. Ross, Tomasz Rzesnicki, George Sips, Jared P. Squire, Sebastian Stanculovic, Shawn Tang, Joe Tooker, Antonio C. Torrezan, Etienne Vallée, Manfred Thumm
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper presents the design, fabrication and preliminary assessment of the THALES TH1512 1 MW 117.5 GHz CW gyrotron, dedicated to the DIII-D fusion facility. Short pulse tests at KIT demonstrated 1.3 MW output and $48\%$ efficiency with readiness for long-pulse operation.

title: An LLM-KG Collaborative Traceability Question-Answering System for TWT Manufacturing
authors: Chenxi Zhu, Guoxin Ren, Zheng Tan, Luanfeng Gao, Shilong Zhu, Bin Li, Yulu Hu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Traveling-wave tube (TWT) manufacturing is characterized by high product variety, low batch volume, and complex process workflows. Under rapid delivery and frequent iteration, typical issues arise, including fragmented information, low query efficiency, and insufficient interaction flexibility. This paper focuses on traceability of key process problems in TWT manufacturing and presents an integrated question-answering (QA) system with deep collaboration between a knowledge graph $(K G)$ and a large language model (LLM). The system integrates natural language processing (NLP) with KG techniques to organize dispersed, unstructured manufacturing knowledge-such as anomalies, causes, simulation reproduction clues, and process stages-into a connected, evidence-chain network. On this basis, an LLMKG collaborative semantic layer is constructed, enhancing the ability to disambiguate restricted entities and complete information in multiple rounds for complex natural language queries, and structuring the retrieval results into evidence-based responses. By limiting the output to KG-hit evidence, this system improves traceability efficiency while maintaining verifiability.

title: Linearity of a Folded Waveguide TWTA at mmWave Frequencies
authors: Gabriel Wiggins, Richard Kowalczyk, Aaron Jensen, Blake Griffin, Diana Gamzina
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: High data throughput and spectral efficiency requirements in modern millimeter-wave (mmWave) communications systems place significant demands on power amplifier linearity. It was shown that folded waveguide TWTAs demonstrate high quality linearity performance for the high data rate communication systems. The integration of an equalizer or linearizer further improves performance. Linearity results are presented for a V-band TWTA. Furthermore, linearity results are presented for an equalized Q-band TWTA and a linearized V-band TWTA.

title: Anti-Windup Sliding Mode Control for Nonlinear Systems
authors: Qian Wang, Yuqi Jiang, Zhaoyang Leng
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In this paper, we study the anti-windup sliding mode control for the nonlinear system with disturbances, component fault and asymmetric input saturation, utilizing the advantages of using the fully actuated system approach (FASA) to handle nonlinear control. An anti-windup sliding mode control strategy is proposed. The influence of input saturation on the system is mitigated by the designed anti-windup compensator. Considering that the derivative of the reference signal is sometimes difficult to obtain, the application of the proposed method in trajectory tracking is studied. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed methods is verified by the examples.

title: Self-Robust RF Fingerprint Identification With 6G Agentic AI: Cloud-Edge Intelligent Collaboration
authors: Yi Liu, Haolin Zheng, Ning Gao, Jian Gong, Yongyong Chen, Michail Matthaiou, Xiao Li, Shi Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: The rapid proliferation of heterogeneous wireless devices in sixth-generation (6G) networks raises significant security challenges, necessitating continuous and zero-trust authentication. Radio frequency fingerprint identification (RFFI) is a promising solution, however, its performance deteriorates markedly under dynamic and unpredictable wireless environments. To address this issue, we propose DrffNet-X, a cross-modal RFF distillation framework to train a lightweight and self-robust RFFI model within a cloud-edge collaborative agentic artificial intelligence (AI) paradigm. In the proposed framework, wired and wireless signals are used to train a large teacher network and a small student network, respectively, enabling the student to learn channel-robust RFF representations. Specifically, we first develop the DrffNet-ResNet, which is based on the ResNet backbone and the spatial attention mechanism. The complexity of the proposed DrffNet-ResNet is analyzed, where the student network achieves a 27% reduction in parameters, but has the similar performance as the teacher network. Next, to prove the model-agnostic of the proposed framework, we also develop the DrffNet-MobileNet and evaluate the performance via the experiments. The experiment results demonstrate that the developed DrffNet-ResNet and DrffNet-MobileNet achieve an accuracy of above 95% and 93% on Long Range (LoRa) radio frequency (RF) datasets, respectively, under different channel effects and various signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs), and also achieve an outstanding performance on unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) RF datasets. The developed networks have significant advantages over the state-of-the-art deep learning (DL)-based RFFI methods in terms of accuracy, computation amount and universal applicability. Extensive system-level evaluations verify that the proposed cloud–edge collaborative RFFI framework enables efficient deployment, low-latency edge inference, and stable performance under continuous model updates.

title: LaVIDE: Language-Prompted Satellite Change Detection via Map-Image Alignment_supp1-3700146.pdf
authors: Fang Xu
publish: N/A
abstract: Remote sensing change detection based on a map reference and an up-to-date image boosts timely observation of the Earth's surface when earlier images are lacking for comparison. However, the semantic gap between high-level map categories and low-level image details hinders the extraction of homogeneous features for robust temporal association in change detection. Unlike conventional approaches that either compare pixel-level visual similarity or propagate segmentation errors, we propose a novel framework, Language-VIsion Discriminator for dEtecting changes, LaVIDE, which bridges the semantic gap between high-level map categories and low-level image details using language as an intermediary. Specifically, we intro duce restricted prompt learning to generate context-aware textual prompts that align map semantics with image content, and an object-aware embedding enhancement strategy to integrate object level attributes (e.g., shape, boundary) into map representations. These components enable robust cross-modal alignment within a unified language-vision feature space. Extensive experiments on four benchmarks, DynamicEarthNet, HRSCD, BANDON, and SECOND, demonstrate that LaVIDE outperforms state-of-the art methods by significant margins, achieving 18.4% and 5.2% improvements in IoU on multi-class and single-class change detection tasks, respectively. Our framework not only advances the accuracy of map-image change detection but also provides a practical solution for rapid map updating with minimal human intervention, promising broad impacts in urban planning, disaster assessment, and ecological conservation. Code and datasets are available at: https://github.com/ShuGuoJ/LAVIDE.git.

title: Dual-GAN Driven Multimodal Low-Altitude Semantic Communication
authors: Xiufang Li, Yiping Duan, Xiaoming Tao, Qigong Sun, Han Jiao, Yao Shi, Qianqian Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: To address the pressing need for efficient multimodal data transmission in low-altitude wireless networks, this paper proposes a Multimodal Semantic Compression and Reconstruction Model (MSCRM). This framework achieves semantic-aware transmission in bandwidth-constrained environments by exchanging only semantic information and model feature memory between the onboard encoding terminal and ground decoding terminal. The architecture employs dual multimodal generative adversarial networks at both the onboard encoding end and ground decoding end. These networks share a specialized Multimodal Feature Fusion Encoding Module (MFFEM) discriminator optimized for low-altitude communication, while their generators can be flexibly adapted to UAV data characteristics. At the onboard encoding terminal, the model enhances the feature discrimination capability of a lightweight MFFEM discriminator through dynamic environmental constraints, enabling real-time conversion of multimodal sensor data into structured semantic labels. The optimized feature memory from the MFFEM network is then fused with semantic labels to form joint information suitable for low-altitude wireless transmission. At the ground decoding terminal, the pre-trained MFFEM network serves as a fixed discriminator, working with received semantic labels to maintain semantic integrity and task usability under limited bandwidth conditions. Comprehensive experiments on multimodal datasets comprising remote-sensing and natural images demonstrate that the proposed model maintains SSIM <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\geq 0.8$</tex-math></inline-formula> and FID <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\leq 80$</tex-math></inline-formula> at bit-rates below 0.3 bpp, markedly surpassing current benchmarks in both compression efficiency and reconstruction fidelity.

title: Efficient Resource Allocation under Adversary Attacks: A Decomposition-Based Approach
authors: Mansoor Davoodi, Setareh Maghsudi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: We address the problem of allocating limited resources in a network under persistent yet statistically unknown adversarial attacks. Each node in the network may be degraded, but not fully disabled, depending on its available defensive resources. The objective is twofold: to minimize total system damage and to reduce cumulative resource allocation and transfer costs over time. We model this challenge as a bi-objective optimization problem and propose a decomposition-based solution that integrates chance-constrained programming with network flow optimization. The framework separates the problem into two interrelated subproblems: determining optimal node-level allocations across time slots, and computing efficient inter-node resource transfers. We theoretically prove the convergence of our method to the optimal solution that would be obtained with full statistical knowledge of the adversary. We further establish an O(√T log(nT)) regret bound, showing that the average per-round performance gap shrinks as O(1/√T). Extensive simulations demonstrate that our method efficiently learns the adversarial patterns and achieves substantial gains in minimizing both damage and operational costs, comparing three benchmark strategies under various parameter settings.

title: IEEE Robotics and Automation Society Announces 2026 Class of Fellows [Society News]

title: Improved A*-DWA Fusion Path Planning for Mobile Robots
authors: Jingjie Liu, Chenlu Liu, Yapeng Zhang, Hongchao Li
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes an integrated path planning scheme fusing an improved bidirectional A* algorithm with an enhanced Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) to address the limitations of low search efficiency, redundant path segments, and insufficient global guidance in traditional algorithms within complex dynamic environments. First, by introducing a bidirectional heuristic search mechanism coupled with a path pruning strategy, the global search space is significantly narrowed and redundant zigzag segments near the meeting point are eliminated, generating a smooth global path composed of sparse key points. Subsequently, in the local planning phase, a global path fitness evaluation function (Fitness) is integrated into the DWA objective function to strengthen global guidance by quantifying the alignment between predicted trajectories and the global path, while a collision prediction mechanism is introduced to assess real-time threats from dynamic obstacles, thereby reducing unnecessary avoidance maneuvers and optimizing travel distance. The results demonstrate that the proposed integrated algorithm achieves effective coupling between global optimality and local real-time performance, significantly enhancing the robot's obstacle avoidance stability and path quality while improving overall planning efficiency in complex dynamic scenarios.

title: Compact Auxiliary Power Supply Unit With Enhanced Galvanic Isolation for Medium-Voltage SiC MOSFET Gate Driver
authors: Jiakun Gong, Yuxi Liang, Yulei Wang, Mingrui Zou, Peng Sun, Liang Wang, Jianyu Pan, Zheng Zeng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The high blocking voltage and fast switching speed of emerging medium-voltage (MV) SiC MOSFET impose stringent requirements on gate driver, particularly on the insulation and common-mode immunity of the isolated auxiliary power supply (APS). In this paper, the realization of a compact APS with ultra-low coupling capacitance and high insulation capability is presented. By optimizing the magnetic core and PCB winding configuration, compact form factor and ultra-low coupling capacitance are achieved, while maintaining suitability for mass production. Furthermore, an edge-metallized PCB winding technique is introduced to optimize electric-field distribution within the transformer, thus enhancing the insulation performance. In terms of topology, LLC converter is adopted to ensure high efficiency and constant output. Test results show that the proposed APS achieves a coupling capacitance as low as 1.4 pF, while the partial discharge inception voltage (PDIV) exceeds 20 kVrms, within a compact volume of only 50 cm3. Double-pulse test under dv/dt exceeding 100 V/ns and continuous experiments based on 3.3 kV XHP packaged power modules are further conducted to demonstrate the reliability and practicality of the proposed compact APS in high density MV converters.

title: Signal-to-Noise Ratio Enhancement of Space Dim Star Images Based on Multi-Temporal Collaborative Strategy
authors: He Zhang, Jinzhen Mu, Yushen Guo, Fei Han
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To address the issue of extremely low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and target signals being easily submerged by strong dynamic noise in space dim star images during space exploration tasks, this paper proposes an SNR enhancement method based on a multi-temporal collaborative strategy. This method utilizes the evolutionary characteristics of continuous star image sequences in the time dimension. By constructing a local temporal evolution energy function and a structure tensor, it proposes an improved Non-Local Means (NLM) algorithm that mines spatiotemporal similarity regions. By introducing a structure adjustment factor and adaptive smoothing parameters to reconstruct the weight calculation mechanism, spatiotemporal collaborative filtering is achieved. Experimental results show that compared with classical algorithms, this method effectively suppresses complex background noise while significantly preserving the edge structure and energy distribution of dim stars, providing a high-quality data foundation for high-precision space target detection.

title: Robust Malicious Traffic Representation Under Instance-Dependent Noise via Entropy Selection and Semi-Supervised Learning
authors: Degang Li, Qingjun Yuan, Xi Chen, Baoquan Liu, Chunxiang Gu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Traffic labeling methods have inherent limitations, including the potential to mislabel hard samples and introduce noisy labels when annotating real-world malicious traffic. Training on such noisy-labeled samples significantly degrades both the training effectiveness and evaluation reliability of malicious traffic detection models. This study proposes MEn2SLe, a method for robust malicious traffic representation under instance-dependent noise (IDN) based on entropy-guided selection and semi-supervised learning. First, an algorithm for constructing traffic datasets with IDN labels is introduced. Subsequently, leveraging the distributional characteristics of IDN, an information entropy-based sample selection strategy is proposed to identify high-confidence samples by analyzing per-sample training stability. To achieve robust representations, MEn2SLe integrates three complementary training strategies: noise label correction, unsupervised inter-class similarity learning, and ground-truth sample contrastive learning. These strategies collectively mitigate the influence of noisy labels and prevent the model from overfitting to label noise. Extensive experiments were conducted to evaluate the performance of MEn2SLe. The results demonstrate that MEn2SLe surpasses baseline methods in both robust training and data pruning tasks across IDN, CCN, and SYM noise scenarios. Specifically, MEn2SLe achieves classification accuracies of 90% and 82% on the CIC-IDS-2017 and Malicious_TLS datasets, respectively, at a 50% noise level, outperforming the best baseline by 13.6% and 9.87%. To further evaluate generalizability, the impact of noisy labels generated by different labeling methods was also assessed. The results confirm that MEn2SLe effectively resists the adverse effects of noise introduced by diverse annotation approaches, demonstrating strong generalization capability.

title: Aerial Scene Recognition From Remote Sensing Images Based on Hybrid Multitemporal Information Fused Transformer-FRACTAL Network
authors: Irfan Haider, Muhammad Attique Khan, Saeed Iqbal, Jiaxin Li, Leila Jamel, Mohammad Alhefdi, Yunyoung Nam
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Aerial scene classification, which aims to automatically label an aerial image with a specific semantic category, is a fundamental problem for understanding high-resolution remote sensing imagery. For accurate classification of aerial scenes from remote sensing images, image classification must effectively integrate local spatial details, context-dependent information from global regions, and hierarchical representations of different scenes. This study proposes a hybrid multitemporal information fusion architecture for aerial scene recognition from Remote Sensing images. The proposed model combines the modified version of EfficientNet-B0 (truncated to a maximum number of convolutional layers), Vision Transformer (ViT), and FractalNet to leverage the strengths of these three models. Each model's branch runs in parallel and is fused via depth-wise feature concatenation. Extensive experiments are conducted on four benchmark datasets (EuroSAT, NWPU, EarthHazards, and Turkey Earthquake), demonstrating the integrated model's improved performance over traditional CNN and transformer models, as well as its compact nature (approximately 2.8 million parameters) compared to these models. Extensive ablation studies also validate the effectiveness of the combined approaches of block-depth selection, self-attention layer, and fusion strategy. The results from the combined model show that it is an effective and efficient approach to classify large numbers of Remote Sensing viewpoint scene types.

title: Finite-Time Adaptive Sliding Mode Trajectory Tracking Control for Traveling Mechanism of Excavators
authors: Wei Lv, Guopin Liu, Xi Luo, Yanqiao Wei
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: To address the low trajectory-tracking accuracy of the excavator traveling mechanism in complex road environments, which arises from the dead-zone nonlinearity of the electro-hydraulic proportional valve and unknown external disturbances, this paper proposes a finite-time adaptive integral sliding mode control (ISMC) method. First, an excavator dynamic model incorporating the proportional valve dead-zone characteristics is established. Based on this, a dual-loop control architecture is designed: the outer loop utilizes a kinematic model to calculate the desired velocity and angle commands, while the inner loop employs an integral sliding mode control strategy. This strategy ensures the fast convergence of system states within a finite time and effectively suppresses chattering. Additionally, to address the unknown upper bound of disturbances, an adaptive update law is designed. This control law is designed to estimate and compensate in real time for the bounds of lumped disturbances, which encompass the errors caused by dead-zone compensation. Lyapunov stability analysis validates the finite-time stability of the closed-loop system. Numerical simulation results are provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach.

title: An Integrated Perception and Control Navigation Strategy for USV in Complex Nearshore Environments
authors: Le Shi, Xi Luo, Junpeng Li, Hongrui Sun
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive navigation strategy that integrates perception and control for Unmanned Surface Vehicles (USV) in unstructured tidal and nearshore waters. The method constructs a semantically enhanced dynamic cost map by fusing camera and LiDAR data. Deep learning algorithms are used to identify navigable water areas in forward-looking images, which are then projected onto the water surface coordinate system via homography transformation. To overcome the geometric perception ambiguity in tidal flats, a semantic consistency verification mechanism is introduced to online evaluate the reliability of LiDAR point clouds using visual semantics, thereby eliminating vegetation-induced false alarms and accurately defining water-land boundaries. At the navigation level, a bidirectional feedback mechanism between global planning and local control is established. Cost gradients are used to guide the adaptive adjustment of the prediction horizon, and backward feedback logic is introduced to ensure real-time escape from dynamic deadlocks. Finally, a cost-sensitive adaptive MPC is adopted to achieve a dynamic balance between tracking accuracy and obstacle avoidance safety. Simulation experiments verify the perceptual robustness and practical application value of the proposed method in complex boundary environments.

title: Auditory Perception as a Surrogate for Auditory Imagery in EEG-Based BCI Training: Neural Evidence and a Feasibility Study
authors: Zhuohao Zhang, Haruto Hamada, Phurin Rangpong, Akima Connelly, Tohru Yagi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Brain-computer interface (BCI) systems are commonly classified as reactive or active. Reactive BCIs rely on responses to external stimuli, simplifying decoding but limiting direct user control. In contrast, active BCIs enable intuitive control through spontaneous mental activity but impose greater signal-processing challenges. Speech BCIs, a subset of active systems, hold promise for individuals with severe neurological impairments such as locked-in syndrome (LIS), potentially enabling communication through imagined speech. However, their adoption is hindered by the demanding pre-training required to collect imagery data, which often induces fatigue, and the difficulty many users face in generating consistent, high-quality imagery. To address these limitations, we propose a hybrid BCI framework that uses passive listening tasks as a surrogate for auditory imagery tasks during model training. As a first step, this proof-of-concept feasibility study examined neural responses to five Japanese vowels (/a/, /i/, /u/, /e/, /o/) under listening and imagery conditions in a limited experimental setting with healthy male participants, using event-related potentials (ERPs), topographical mapping, and event-related spectral perturbations (ERSPs), followed by statistical analysis of shared and distinct neural patterns. In the primary three-class and binary classification analyses, classification accuracies for imagery signals obtained from models trained solely on listening data were comparable to, and in some settings higher than, those trained directly on imagery data, with both outperforming chance level. These findings indicate that passive listening may provide effective training data for active auditory imagery BCIs within the tested setting, potentially reducing cognitive demands without compromising performance. Further validation in larger and more diverse cohorts is required before clinical application.

title: Optimal Receiver Processing for Passive UHF RFID in Pulsed-Wave Mode
authors: Romain Borgna, Julien Huillery, Yvan Duroc, Arnaud Bréard
publish: IEEE Journal of Radio Frequency Identification
abstract: It is well established that replacing the traditional continuous-wave signals with pulsed-wave signals improves the performance of passive UHF (Ultra High Frequency) RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) systems in terms of wireless power transfer. However, the impact of this change on the performance of these systems in terms of information transmission has been little analyzed. Based on the maximum ratio combining principle, this paper describes the optimal multi-carrier receiver processing that maximizes the information performance of passive UHF RFID systems in pulsed-wave mode. In addition to the theoretical description of the processing, numerical simulations are performed to test the estimators required for the proposed combination process. Finally, experimental measurements are performed to confirm the feasibility of the proposed processing with real data and verify the performance stated in the theory.

title: BAVDM: Behavioral-Pattern-Aware Value Decomposition for Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Baofu Fang, Mengyuan Fang, Hao Wang, Xiaohui Yuan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Emerging Topics in Computational Intelligence
abstract: Value decomposition is a powerful method for addressing credit assignment problems within the centralized training decentralized execution (CTDE) paradigm. However, existing value decomposition algorithms lack effective mechanisms to attribute individual agents’ contributions by modeling their dynamically emergent roles through specific behavioral patterns, thereby limiting the further enhancement of credit assignment and policy optimization. To address this gap, we propose Behavioral-pattern-Aware Value Decomposition for Multi-agent reinforcement learning (BAVDM). BAVDM integrates the roles that agents play under specific behavioral patterns as key information flows within an attention-based mixing network to directly derive their contribution, thereby achieving more efficient credit assignment. Specifically, a role-aware module is designed, which initially comprehends the agent’s behavioral patterns from its trajectory embeddings and generates role representations along with corresponding policy parameters. These role-based policy parameters connect the agent’s decisions to its contribution to the team, and contrastive loss ensures the effectiveness and stability of the learned role representations. During the credit assignment phase, by combining the agents’ individual role representations, local Q-values, and global state information, a value decomposition mixing network based on a multi-head attention mechanism is employed to effectively decompose the global Q-value. Experimental results in StarCraft II and MPE multi-agent cooperative scenarios demonstrate that BAVDM outperforms baseline methods. Specifically, in the challenging 5m_vs_6 m scenario from SMAC, BAVDM achieves nearly an 8% higher win rate than state-of-the-art approaches, showcasing its significant advantage.

title: Distributed Acoustic Sensor for Real-Time Fault Detection in Belt Conveyors: A Signal Processing Approach
authors: Yimin Luo, Qingwen Liu, Huanmo Zhou, Zuyuan He
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Distributed Optical-fiber Acoustic Sensor (DAS) systems exhibit significant potential for vibration monitoring in large electromechanical equipment such as belt conveyors due to their unique advantages. However, the inherent challenges of ultra-high data volumes (typically at Gb/s levels) and susceptibility to noise in long-distance and complex industrial environments pose severe difficulties for real-time anomaly detection. To address these issues, this study proposes an improved isolation forest algorithm that innovatively integrates two-dimensional Constant False Alarm Rate (CFAR) spatiotemporal filtering and the Neyman-Pearson (N-P) optimal threshold criterion. The proposed method effectively suppresses environmental noise while maintaining high detection accuracy, achieving a system response time of under 3 seconds. Field tests in coal mining environments demonstrate real-time identification of multiple typical fault signals, offering a reliable solution for online monitoring of long distance industrial conveyor systems.

title: A Path Reachability Approach to Necessary and Sufficient Stability of Switched Positive Systems
authors: Shuang An, Jie Lian, Qingyu Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper presents necessary and sufficient (N&S) conditions for exponential stability in discrete-time switched positive systems with marginally stable subsystems. Each subsystem's structure is represented as a digraph capturing interactions among state components. Through subsystem switching, the reachability from marginally stable to asymptotically stable components over finite time intervals is established as a N&S stability condition. Based on this characterization, cluster-based stabilization algorithms are developed to design switching strategies. The proposed approach overcomes the traditional conditions that mandate asymptotically stable components across the entire state space. The effectiveness of our approach is demonstrated through comparative simulations in a multi-vehicle formation control application.
