title: MTA-LSTMNet: A Multi-Task Attention LSTM Architecture for Continuous Spatio-Temporal Drought Forecasting in Andhra Pradesh
authors: Murali Krishna Challagulla, Emerson Ajith Jubilson
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Meteorological and hydrological droughts are known to have complex spatio-temporal dynamics that limit the effectiveness of traditional earlywarning systems. Machine learning models used for drought forecasting are mainly focused on solving binary classification tasks rather than reflecting the severity of drought and the temporal nature of climatic disruptions. Addressing these limitations, this study introduces the novel Multi-Task Attention LSTM (MTA-LSTMNet) architecture that enables concurrent predictions of SPEI-3, SPEI-6, and SPEI-12 drought severities. Training is based on a 26-year long (1995-2020) high-resolution spatial data set covering the state of Andhra Pradesh, India, with a historical window of six months and a collection of twelve Essential Climate Variables (ECVs). The presented architecture is characterized by a unique approach to integrating the effects of macro-oceanic teleconnections (Mean Sea Levels) and micro-atmospheric aerosol disruptions (PM2.5, CO2). Specifically, a new Temporal Attention mechanism has been developed to calculate the inertia of the regional water cycle, showing that the model’s accuracy is 63.7% dependent on the immediate preceding conditions, while still having a meaningful memory tail up to six months ago. The presented model showed highly competitive results for all three timelines – R2 of 0.919, 0.916, and 0.914, respectively, which is a significant improvement over traditional methods such as XGBoost and Random Forest. In terms of the feature importance, permutation analysis showed that the Arabian Sea Levelwas the global variable impacting regional droughts the most, while also identifyingPM2.5 concentration as a local factor. Finally, the spatio-temporal mapping proved the model capable of accurately reflecting asymmetric recovery in the coastal areas and detecting rain shadow drought vulnerability zones such as Rayalaseema.

title: Efficient Client Selection in Federated Learning: Integrating Data, Communication, and Cost Factors
authors: Preti Kumari, Hari Prabhat Gupta, Biplab Sikdar, Zehui Xiong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Federated learning addresses the requirements of modern communication networks, ensuring low latency, resource efficiency, and data privacy. However, challenges arise from data variability, device heterogeneity, and constrained resources. These challenges worsen with noise in the dataset and communication channel. Thus, the efficient selection of participating clients in federated learning considering all these factors is crucial. This paper proposes an efficient client selection approach integrating data quality (noise and heterogeneity), communication network quality between client and server, and cost budget considerations. Initially, we address noise in local datasets and communication channels, providing an expression to estimate the communication rounds required for convergence. Subsequently, we introduce a method to estimate the reputation of each client, considering heterogeneity in the dataset and devices. After that, we estimate the training cost and time for each client based on the calculated communication rounds and client reputation. Finally, a multi-constraint knapsack problem is formulated to select high-reputation clients within the given cost budget and time constraints. These obtained clients contribute to the federated learning process, ensuring maximum accuracy within the given constraints. The results show that with noise and heterogeneity levels surpassing 60%, the proposed approach incurs a cost at least 27% lower than existing approaches.

title: Rebuild of moisture equilibrium curves for transformer cellulose insulation immersed in oil: Influence of nitrogen content in TUK papers and comparison with aramid-based paper
authors: Oscar H. Arroyo-Fernandez, Patrick Picher, Esperanza Mariela Rodriguez-Celis, Brigitte Morin, Jocelyn Jalbert
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The moisture equilibrium curves between paper and oil in power transformers, originally developed by Oommen, are widely used to assess transformer insulation conditions. Oommen’s model is based on experimental data from Jeffries, in which wood pulp samples are conditioned at relative humidity levels above 5%. This study presents experimental results on the water content of insulation papers under low relative humidity conditions (1–5%). Kraft, thermally upgraded Kraft (TUK), and aramid papers are conditioned using a glove box and environmental chamber, and the moisture content is quantified via Karl Fischer titration. The influence of nitrogen content in TUK papers on moisture sorption IS examined, revealing that nitrogenous additives reduce water uptake, likely due to the formation of nitrogenous bases. Among the tested materials, aramid paper exhibits the lowest water absorption. Equilibrium curves are generated and compared with the Jeffries-based model, showing differences in both moisture content and bubble inception temperature (BIT). The findings of this study contribute to more accurate modeling of moisture equilibrium under low-humidity conditions and facilitate the development of more robust and predictive tools for transformer insulation monitoring.

title: Localization and Containment Control of the Multiwheeled Robot System Based on Local Relative Measurement
authors: Yunkai Lv, Die Shao, Huaicheng Yan, Youmin Zhang, Hao Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: This article delves into the finite-time localizability and controllability of multiwheeled robot systems, utilizing only local relative measurement data. Investigating the localization of a multiagent system with arbitrary motion is not only more comprehensive but also presents greater technical challenges. From the standpoint of deterministic systems, a novel distributed finite-time localization algorithm is designed. This method relies solely on local relative measurements and interagent communication, enabling precise localization even in scenarios where agents lack a shared orientation and exhibit complex motion trajectories. Concurrently, a distributed finite-time state observer is devised to complement the localization process. Leveraging the outputs of the localization and observation mechanisms, a distributed containment control strategy is proposed. In contrast to conventional approaches, the main advantage of the proposed methodology lies in its independence from uniform motion constraints. By circumventing the need for global information, it seamlessly integrates distributed localization and control functions. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated through both numerical simulations and real-robot experiments.

title: Leader-Following Consensus of Multi-Agent Systems with Markov Process Under Communication Link Faults
authors: Ying Yang, Kaibo Shi, Ke Tang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates leader-following consensus of multi-agent systems with Markov process under communication link faults. Firstly, a continuous-time Markov process is introduced to characterize random variations of the interaction topology, while time-varying multiplicative perturbations are used to describe faulty follower-follower and leader-follower communication links. Secondly, to compensate for the loss of reliable leader information, a distributed resilient observer is designed for each follower. Based on the observer estimates and local faulty interaction information, an adaptive tracking control protocol is proposed. Thirdly, by combining Riccati-based gain design with Lyapunov analysis, sufficient conditions are derived to guarantee mean-square leader-following consensus with respect to the Markov switching process. Finally, numerical simulation experiments are given to verify that the controller achieves consensus tracking of the leader by the followers.

title: Quantized Innovation-Based State Estimation in CPSS Under Ideal Communication Channels
authors: Pengyu Li, Kaixin Cui, Kaijing Jin
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Innovation-based state estimation with quantized communication provides an effective solution for cyber-physical systems operating under stringent bandwidth constraints. While extensive studies have demonstrated the feasibility of quantized innovation filtering, the statistical characteristics induced by multi-level quantization have not been fully characterized in an analytical form. This paper investigates quantized innovation-based estimation under ideal communication conditions, where quantized innovations are transmitted without channel noise. A symmetric <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(2 N+1)$</tex>-level quantization scheme with dead zones is considered to balance communication efficiency and estimation accuracy. By explicitly analyzing the probability distribution of quantized innovations, closed-form expressions for the estimation error covariance are derived for vector systems. The results reveal how quantization parameters directly influence second-order estimation performance and provide a tractable analytical benchmark for the design of quantized innovation-based estimators. Simulation results based on a UAV model are presented to validate the theoretical analysis.

title: An Expressed and Private Opinion Dynamics with Appraisal Strategy
authors: Cong Lu, Qingsong Liu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Within social networks, the evolution of interpersonal evaluations and personal opinions is not an independent process, but rather an intertwined one. Whether two people like or dislike each other is often determined by the similarity of their opinions on a topic, and existing social psychology literature indicates that individuals sharing similar beliefs, behaviors, and values engage in closer communication and interaction. However, in existing models of expressed and private opinion dynamics, the influence matrix is often independent of individual opinions. Therefore, this paper proposes an expressed and private opinion dynamics model based on a homophily-based appraisal mechanism. We assume that individuals' opinions are updated through their interpersonal appraisal networks, which in turn are updated based on individuals' expressed opinions via the homophily-based appraisal mechanism. Based on structural balance theory, we establish a bipartite consensus criterion for the multidimensional expressed and private opinion dynamics model. Additionally, we examine convergence criteria for opinions in two specific scenarios: discussions focused solely on a single topic and those involving interrelated topics. We provide explicit representations of both final expressed opinions and private opinions in each case. Finally, the validity of the theoretical results is verified through numerical simulations.

title: Output-Feedback Control for Spacecraft: A Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Qi Zheng, Mingwei Jin, Yue Fang, Kangkang Sun, Jianbin Qiu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the modeling and output-feedback tracking control issues for the attitude-orbit integrated spacecraft based on the fully actuated system approach. As traditional spacecraft attitude and orbit control schemes treat the attitude and orbit motions as isolated subsystems, they ignore the coupling characteristics between the attitude dynamics and the orbit dynamics, failing to improve the control performance. Although attitude-orbit integrated control systems enable the integrated description and control of the six degrees-of-freedom (DoF) pose systems, they have strong nonlinearities which bring difficulties to the control design and analysis. In practical engineering circumstances, the physical conditions and the measurement accuracy make part of the system states unknown, which limits the application of the state-feedback control techniques. The development of the fully actuated control theory provides solutions to the above issues. Hence, the twistor, variable elimination, and order elevation techniques are adopted to transform the spacecraft relative dual quaternion model into the fully actuated attitude-orbit integrated system. For the output-feedback tracking control objective, a state-observer is designed to estimate the unknown states, and the tracking control law is given based on the estimation signals. A Lyapunov function is selected to analyze the stability of the closed-loop system, and the estimation and tracking errors are proved to be asymptotically convergent. Finally, a numerical simulation is carried out to verify the effectiveness of the proposed control scheme.

title: Information-Secure Distributed State Estimation for Nonlinear Complex Networks Subject to DoS Attacks via Chaotic Encryption
authors: Bingxin Lei, Jiaying Li, Jun Hu, Weiru Chen, Chong Tan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the information-secure distributed state estimation (SE) problem for nonlinear complex networks subject to denial-of-service (DoS) attacks. To address potential eavesdropping on measurement data during transmission, a quantization-assisted chaotic encryption scheme is incorporated into the distributed estimation framework to enhance the confidentiality and security of transmitted information. Through dynamic encryption and synchronized decryption driven by chaotic sequences, the transmitted signals are effectively protected, ensuring that intercepted data cannot reveal the true measurement information. Meanwhile, the impact of DoS attacks is explicitly incorporated into the design of the estimation method. On this basis, a novel information-secure distributed SE method is developed to achieve reliable estimation performance under DoSinduced communication disruptions, where the estimator gain matrix is designed according to a derived upper bound on the estimation error covariance. Finally, simulation results based on a multi-robot localization scenario demonstrate the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed secure distributed SE scheme.

title: Scalable 3D PIC Simulation of High Power Klystrons Using The Parallel Code ACE3P
authors: Mohamed A. K. Othman, Sean Ramirez, Brandon R. Weatherford, Ng Cho-Kuen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: We report on the application of the finite-element-code ACE3P to perform fully-consistent end-to-end simulation of high-power klystrons. This approach represents a new paradigm for the next generation of high efficiency klystrons requiring high fidelity PIC simulation to resolve the complex rf and beam physics.

title: Prescribed-Time Reduced-Order Observer for Linear Systems with Unknown Inputs and Measurement Disturbances
authors: Haifang Li, Zhe Zhang, Yunxia Song
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates the prescribed-time unknown input observer (UIO) problem for a class of linear uncertain systems subject to both unknown inputs and measurement disturbances. By introducing a disturbance-decoupled measured output through a generalized inverse-based transformation, the influence of the measurement disturbance channel is eliminated from the observer design stage. Under an observer matching condition and a strong observability condition, a reduced-order observer with a specifically constructed time-varying gain is developed to guarantee that the estimation error converges to zero at a user prescribed time T. The time-varying gain is explicitly given and depends on a smooth periodic weighting function and an associated nonsingular matrix defined by an integral expression. A numerical example is provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed prescribed-time UIO.

title: Joint Resource Allocation and Routing for Truck-Drone Collaborative Delivery in Complex Urban Environments
authors: Ze Li, Zhenyu Gao, Ge Guo
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper addresses the resource allocation and route coordination problem in a truck-drone collaborative delivery system (TDCDS) under complex urban environments, and a genetic algorithm (GA)-based optimization framework is developed to jointly optimize customer-to-vehicle assignment, truck routing on a directed road network with turn constraints, and drone task grouping. The proposed method integrates a truck path planner based on A* algorithm and an UAV path planner that groups drone-served customers into feasible sorties under payload and range limitations. By incorporating hard constraints such as parcel weight, and item fragility, the algorithm ensures operational feasibility while minimizing total system cost. Numerical experiments are conducted on a simulated urban delivery instance with 220 customers, and the results show that the proposed GA-based coordination strategy significantly outperforms the truck-only delivery scheme, reducing the total cost by 59.1 % and the mission time by 60.2 %. These findings verify the effectiveness of the proposed method in enabling efficient and scalable truck-drone collaboration.

title: Full-State Constrained Control of Unknown Nonlinear Systems with Asymmetric Prescribed Performance Functions
authors: Shuang Wu, Changchun Hua, Ranxin Dong
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes an adaptive control scheme for strictly feedback nonlinear systems with unknown dynamics and full-state constraints. By introducing a smooth switching mechanism, the nonlinear transformation function is enhanced to accommodate asymmetric and sign-varying predefined performance boundaries, ensuring zero equilibrium points when constraint boundaries have opposite signs. Additionally, a dynamic boundary adjustment mechanism is designed to autonomously expand the boundaries when state constraint conflicts may lead to instability, thereby preventing excessive control gains or constraint violations. Theoretical analysis demonstrates that the proposed scheme guarantees boundedness of all closed-loop signals and satisfaction of time-varying state constraints. Finally, two simulation validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Sample Inherent Correlation Mining for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Jia Feng, Fanxin Kong, Liansuo Wei, Junping Zhang, Xinnian Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Graph convolutional networks (GCNs) have recently shown great promise in hyperspectral image classification (HIC) by effectively modeling long-range correlations among samples. However, existing methods often rely on sample similarity for graph construction while neglecting intra-class variability, thereby limiting the robustness of the extracted features. This paper introduces a novel HIC method that leverages sample inherent correlations to enhance feature representation and improve classification performance. Specifically, for the labeled sample set, two distinct intra-class sample graphs are constructed: the intra-class sample similarity graph (ISSG) for capturing class cohesiveness, and the intra-class sample discrepancy graph (ISDG) for modeling variability within the same class. These complementary graph structures are integrated into a dual-branch GCN model, where the ISSG-based branch acts as the primary classifier and the ISDG-based branch serves as an auxiliary component. During training, contrastive learning is employed to enhance feature alignment and generalization. For testing, category prediction is performed on the primary branch using a graph structure constructed from an augmented sample set, comprising training samples, test samples, and the spatial neighbors of the test samples within a predefined local window based on similarity metrics. Finally, the class label for each test sample is determined as the dominant semantic category within its local spatial window. Experimental results on four public HSI datasets demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art techniques and existing graph-based approaches. The code will be available at https://github.com/fengjia116/SICM.

title: Distributed-Observer-Based Control for Interconnected High-Order Fully-Actuated Affine Nonlinear Systems Via Full-Measured-Canonical-Form
authors: Yuyan Li, Shuai Liu
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: Considering that most practical engineering systems naturally exhibit high-order dynamics, and the high-order fullmeasured form offers clear advantages for observer design, this paper successfully extends the full-measured canonical form (FMCF) framework to distributed forced nonlinear systems despite challenges including nonlinearities, failure of the separation principle, and model mismatches. A method for designing distributed observers for affine nonlinear systems based on FMCF is presented, thereby broadening the applicability of the FMCF framework. Furthermore, a distributed-observer based control law is proposed, extending the direct parametric control of high-order fully actuated (HOFA) systems to distributed scenarios and overcoming the difficulty that local agents cannot access full state information. A class of sufficient conditions is also established, guaranteeing that the error dynamics of the distributed observer and the state trajectories of the closed-loop system converge to arbitrarily small invariant sets. The effectiveness of the proposed method is finally validated through numerical simulations.

title: Quantitative Assessment of Cloud-Penetration Capability for Spaceborne Photon-Counting Lidar in Surface Observations
authors: Xinyuan Liu, Huiying Zheng, Zhiyu Zhang, Jian Yang, Pufan Zhao, Song Li, Yue Ma, Songhua Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Clouds, covering two-thirds of the Earth, are the primary obstacle to the development of all-weather spaceborne photon-counting lidars (SPLs) by severely attenuating signals and increasing noise levels, thereby complicating signal detection and extraction. Consequently, most data applications of SPLs, such as those from the Ice, Cloud, and land Elevation Satellite-2 (ICESat-2), typically avoid cloudy scenarios, limiting the full potential of SPLs. To quantitatively model and estimate the cloud-penetrating capability of SPLs, we integrate the radiative transfer model, the detection model, and the signal extraction performance into a standardized and rigorous framework, and transform the issue into signal extraction capability under different signal-to-noise ratios (SNRs). First, we derive a radiative transfer model for SPLs in complex atmospheric environments using Monte Carlo simulations to calculate cloud optical parameters and considering three surface types: ocean, snow-free land, and snow-covered land. The accuracy of the model is verified by twelve ICESat-2 tracks worldwide, with an average mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 27.56% for signal photon counts and 19.64% for background noise rates. Second, we develop an assessment model that integrates hardware-based detection and algorithm-based extraction for SPL performance, deriving theoretical relationships between signal and noise levels, photon-counting detector characteristics, algorithm parameters, and the F-score. The cloud-penetration capability of ICESat-2 is estimated across three typical surfaces, with discrepancies between theoretical and measured values below 15% and a mean error of 9.0%. A quantitative analysis evaluates the impact of system and environmental parameters on cloud-penetration capability, complemented by seasonal analysis of ICESat-2 globally. The strong beam of ICESat-2 can penetrate clouds with optical depths of approximately 0.7-0.9 over oceans, 0.3-0.8 over snow-free land, and 1.35–1.55 over snow-covered land. The proposed models provide critical scientific insights and practical value for SPL development in breaking through climate limitations, enhancing data quality control, and optimizing system design.

title: Revisiting angle stability in power systems with grid-forming power converters
authors: Régulo E. Ávila-Martínez, Javier Renedo, Luis Rouco, Aurelio Garcia-Cerrada, Lukas Sigrist
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Systems
abstract: This letter presents a comprehensive analysis of the stability phenomenon related to the ability of generators to remain in synchronism when subjected to small or large disturbances, in power systems with both synchronous machines and grid-forming voltage source converters (GFM-VSC). This phenomenon is associated with two stability classes in the IEEE/PES classification, namely, rotor-angle stability (when involving synchronous machines) and slow-interaction converter-driven stability (when involving power converters). However, this work shows that this phenomenon is fully characterised with the slow dynamics of the angle difference between the voltage sources connected to the power system, regardless of whether they are synchronous machines (with rotors) or GFM-VSCs. Therefore, we suggest using the term angle stability to refer to this phenomenon, while slow-interaction converter-driven stability should only include slow interactions of different nature involving power converters.

title: FedDK: Towards Adaptive Personalized Federated Learning via Decoupled Knowledge Transfer under Heterogeneous Environments
authors: Seungjin Han, Changwoo Lee
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables collaborative training without sharing raw data, however, its performance typically degrades in heterogeneous client environments due to non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data, model disparity, and conflicting objectives between personalization and global generalization. To address this issue, we propose a novel federated decoupled knowledge (FedDK), a personalized FL framework that introduces a dual-head architecture with explicit role separation. FedDK factorizes the output layer into a balanced head that learns from both private supervision and globally distilled knowledge to preserve client-specific adaptation, and a global head that is optimized exclusively with server-aggregated soft targets on proxy data to maintain client-agnostic global knowledge. This decoupled design mitigates optimization interference between local and global objectives while avoiding full parameter exchange via proxy-based knowledge transfer. Extensive experiments on the EMNIST, CIFAR-100, and SVHN under strong, intermediate, and weak non-IID settings and cross-domain proxy scenarios, demonstrate that FedDK exhibits high and consistent performance across diverse heterogeneous environments, including domains with very limited proxy data. Overall, FedDK provides a lightweight and practical framework for balancing personalization and global consistency in heterogeneous federated learning.

title: A Comprehensive Survey of Knowledge-Based Visual Question Answering Systems: The Lifecycle of Knowledge in Visual Reasoning Task_supp1-3699946.pdf
authors: Guandong Xu
publish: N/A
abstract: Knowledge-based Visual Question Answering (KB-VQA) extends general Visual Question Answering by requiring external knowledge beyond the provided visual and textual inputs, facilitating more complex real-world applications. KB-VQA introduces unique challenges, including the alignment of heterogeneous information from diverse modalities and sources, the retrieval of relevant knowledge from large-scale and noisy repositories, and the execution of complex reasoning to infer answers from the combined context. With the advancement of large language models, KB-VQA systems have undergone a notable transformation, where LLMs serve as powerful knowledge repositories, retrieval-augmented generators and strong reasoners. Despite substantial progress, there is a lack of a recent, systematic survey that organizes and reviews the evolving landscape of existing KB-VQA methods. This survey aims to fill this gap by establishing a structured taxonomy of KB-VQA approaches and decomposing mainstream systems into three fundamental stages: knowledge representation, knowledge retrieval, and knowledge reasoning. Through an examination of existing techniques employed at each stage, this survey identifies persistent challenges and outlines promising future research directions, providing a foundation for advancing KB-VQA models and their applications.

title: A Coarse-to-Fine Registration Algorithm for the Remote Sensing Optical Image using Improved KAZE and DE-Optimization
authors: Aarthi Musku, Sourabh Paul, Dheeren Ku Mahapatra
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Sub-pixel accuracy is a key requirement in optical remote sensing image registration, especially in applications such as change detection, data fusion, and image mosaicing where even minute misalignments significantly affect subsequent analysis. However, achieving reliable subpixel precision is still a critical task due to illumination variations, and geometric distortions. In this paper, we propose a coarse-to-fine registration framework that achieves a subpixel accuracy by combining an improved version of KAZE (IKAZE) algorithm and Differential Evolution (DE) optimization. The I-KAZE is utilized to perform the feature-based coarse registration between the images. Subsequently, DE optimization is employed along with MI to perform fine registration. Experimental results on optical remote sensing datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves registration accuracy compared to conventional state-ofthe-art algorithms. The proposed method is able to achieve the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) value in the range of 0.57 to 0.82 pixels.

title: In situ and Real Time Characterization of Epitaxial, Ultrasmooth and High Quantum Efficiency Photocathodes Using Synchrotron X-rays
authors: Mengjia Gaowei, Kali Prasanna Mondal, Elena Echeverria, Chad Pennington, Yilin Yang, John Walsh, Rudy Begay, Kenneth Evans-Lutterodt, Siddharth Karkare, Jared Maxson, John Smedley
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: Alkali-based semiconductor photocathodes are extensively used as electron sources and photon detectors. The performance characteristics, such as quantum efficiency and thermal emittance, are fundamentally influenced by the properties of these materials, including crystallinity and surface roughness. Epitaxial growth of alkali-based photocathodes can be achieved on latticematched substrates using controlled growth techniques like molecular-beam epitaxy. At BNL, high quantum efficiency, ultrasmooth, and epitaxial thin film photocathodes of $\mathrm{K}_{2} \mathrm{CsSb}, \mathrm{Cs}_{3} \mathrm{Sb}$ and Cs<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>Te were developed on various latticematched substrates, utilizing in situ and real time synchrotron x-ray characterization techniques to gain insights into the stoichiometry, surface roughness and crystal structure of the photocathode materials.

title: Model-Free Adaptive Control for High-Order Fully Actuated System
authors: Haobo Wen, Xiaoyun Sun, Qiang Shen, Ju Jiang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This article investigates the model-free adaptive control scheme under the background of the high-order fully actuated system. The general nonlinear non-affine dynamics are converted into the fully actuated subsystem form, and the uncertainties are estimated by the iterative parameter updating law and the fuzzy-logic system. Using the invertible properties of the input parameter matrix in the fully actuated subsystem, the fully actuated model-free adaptive control framework is developed, and the closed-loop performance is verified both by theoretical analysis with the aid of the contract mapping principle and simulation results in the condition of a type of coaxial dual-rotor unmanned autonomous vehicle dynamics.

title: Progress in Development of a Multiple Frequency Stability Analysis Framework Based on the 2.5D Large-signal Code TESLA-ZM
authors: Igor A. Chernyavskiy, Alexander N. Vlasov, Thomas M. Antonsen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: We report on our progress in development of the multiple frequency version of the NRL 2.5D large-signal code TESLA-ZM (or TESLA-Z earlier) suitable for modeling of large-signal operation and non-linear stability analysis of VE amplifiers. This involves an extension of the previously developed single-frequency TESLA-ZM algorithm by addition into it support for multiple frequencies, including the main fundamental working frequency together with its harmonics, frequency of perturbation signal and frequencies of their inter-modes. Extended in such way code TESLA-ZM algorithm is capable to take into account non-linear processes, which arise as result of a large-signal regime of operation in the given VED. The current efforts involve validation of the newly developed multiple frequency version of the code TESLA-ZM by comparisons of its predictions with the predictions of the other codes and available to us measurements. The future application of this algorithm will include modeling of Drive Induced Oscillation (DIO) instability, which is the main limiting factor in the operation of the high-gain, high-power Coupled-cavity TWTs.

title: W-Band Traveling Wave Tube Amplifier Beamstick Fabricated with Precision Alignment Techniques
authors: Philipp Borchard, Aaron Jensen, Brian Beaudoin, Thomas Antonsen, John Petillo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A W-band Serpentine waveguide Traveling Wave Tube (TWT) beamstick using elastic averaging, kinematic coupling and quasi-kinematic coupling precision mechanical alignment techniques was fabricated. Single micron level alignment precision was demonstrated at the component level; electron gun and millimeter-wave (mm) circuit, and at the device level for the electron beam transport system.

title: Design of a Ka-band Helix Traveling Wave Tube
authors: Shasha Qiu, Marc L. Barsanti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A compact, Ka-band helix traveling-wave tube is under development to address applications requiring lightweight, broadband millimeter-wave sources with substantial continuous-wave output power. The TWT is designed to deliver 150 W CW output power over 13.5 GHz instantaneous bandwidth while operating at a relatively low beam voltage of 8.8 kV. Broadband performance is achieved using a helix slow-wave structure employing T-shaped BeO supporting rods. Circuit stability is enhanced through a two-section interaction circuit incorporating a sever and distributed RF loss, along with an output pitch taper to improve circuit efficiency. The electron gun and PPM focusing stack are designed to generate and transport a laminar pencil beam with minimal helix interception through the interaction circuit. Ongoing work includes fabrication, assembly, and experimental validation of the tube.

title: Study of a Comb TWT with Low Phase and Group Delays for 0.66 THz Communication Systems
authors: Chiyang Chen, Jingrui Duan, Yang Dong, Zhigang Lu, Shaomeng Wang, Yubin Gong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: This paper analyzes a 0.66 THz traveling-wave tube (TWT) using a comb-type slow-wave structure with a normalized phase velocity of 0.237 c and interaction impedance of $\mathbf{0. 1 3 ~} \Omega$. Driven by a 15.1 $\mathrm{kV}, 15 \mathrm{~mA}$ electron beam, the results show a saturated output power of 2.16 W, a gain of 33 dB, and a 19 GHz 3-dB bandwidth. Within $0.640-0.692 \mathrm{THz}$, phase delay and group delay remain below 0.03 ns and 0.62 ns, demonstrating potential for high-fidelity broadband THz communications.

title: Predefined Time Attitude Tracking Control for Rigid Spacecraft Using Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Jindou Xu, Minjuan Huang, Guangren Duan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: For rigid spacecraft systems subject to external disturbances, this paper proposes a predefined-time attitude tracking control method based on fully actuated system (FAS) theory. Firstly, a fully actuated spacecraft attitude tracking error system is established. Then a nonlinear extended state observer is designed to rapidly estimate disturbance external disturbances. By integrating FAS feedback with predefined-time sliding mode control, a novel control law is constructed using the estimation for feedforward compensation. Furthermore, rigorous stability analysis via Lyapunov theory verifies that all closed-loop signals converge to a small neighborhood within a predefined time. Finally, simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Design of Intelligent Warehouse Inspection Car Control System
authors: Aili Li, Jiaxin Jin, Pengyu Luo, Zhengyang Tan
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: With the rapid development of logistics and transportation industry, “unmanned+intelligent” has gradually replaced the traditional manual warehouse inspection mode. In this context, an intelligent warehouse inspection car integrating security and inspection functions is designed. The design integrates environmental perception, convenient interaction and intelligent control. Through multi-sensor fusion, it can detect the temperature and humidity, smoke concentration, light intensity and other data in the warehouse environment in real time, and take audible and visual alarm and necessary emergency measures to remind the abnormal state of the environment. At the same time, the infrared photoelectric sensor and ultrasonic sensor are used to transmit the obstacle information to the system to realize the control of autonomous obstacle avoidance and automatic tracking along the tracking line. Use “Wi-Fi+5G” dual-mode data transmission to realize remote monitoring through interactive connection with APP. The car is widely used in warehouses, logistics, assembly workshops, production lines and other different scenarios, greatly reducing the loss of human, material and financial resources, high safety index, high reliability and strong stability, and realizing the intelligent perception control of the inspection car.

title: High-Precision Intelligent Modeling for Strip Crown Prediction in Hot-Rolling Processes
authors: Minghan Qi, Xiaoyu Wang, Na Zhou, Shuzhi Wang, Wen Peng
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: High-precision strip crown prediction is essential for the dimensional quality of hot-rolled steel, yet complex non-linearities and stochastic disturbances challenge traditional modeling. This paper proposes an integrated framework combining K-means clustering with a Whale Optimization Algorithm (WOA) optimized XGBoost model. A K-means-based strategy partitions raw data into steady-state subsets to filter transient fluctuations, while the WOA automates hyperparameter tuning for the XGBoost regressor. Experimental results show the framework significantly outperforms baseline and Bayesian-optimized models, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}=0.944, M A E=2.01 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R M S E=3.12 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>. Cluster-specific modeling further enhances accuracy to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}>0.95$</tex>, with absolute errors concentrated within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 10 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex>. This framework offers a robust, precise solution for real-time industrial shape control and steel quality improvement.

title: Observer-Based Safe Trajectory Tracking Control for Unmanned Helicopters Via Control Barrier Functions and Fully Actuated System Approach
authors: Fangzheng Li, Zhenhua Zhao, Bin Jiang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: In this article, we investigate the safe trajectory tracking control problem of the unmanned helicopter (UH) subject to multisource disturbances and actuator faults. Firstly, by transforming the trajectory tracking problem into the commands tracking in position and attitude loops, a second-order fully actuated system model of the UH is established. Secondly, a high-gain input disturbance observer and a high-order sliding mode observer are adapted to estimate the lumped disturbances in position loop, the derivatives of the attitude angle tracking errors and the lumped disturbances in the attitude loop, respectively. And then, the composite tracking controller is designed by incorporating the estimation information. Moreover, considering the impact of disturbances and obstacles in the environment on system safety, an observer-based control barrier function is developed, and the corresponding quadratic programming problem is constructed to further obtain the closed-form solution of the safety controller. Finally, numerical simulations are performed to validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: High-Frequency Characteristic Investigation of a Terahertz Slow Wave Structure by Machine Learning: Random Forest and Neural Networks
authors: Yong Luo, Shaomeng Wang, Yan Shao, Yubin Gong
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: To address the problems of low efficiency and hi gh computational cost in the design of conventional slow-wav e structures (SWS), this paper proposes a forward-backward j oint modeling framework based on random forest and neural network. Specifically, for forward modeling, a multi-output ra ndom forest regression model is adopted to realize the predict ion of dispersion characteristics from structural parameters. For backward modeling, first, Pearson correlation analysis is employed to screen the key structural parameters, and then a constrained Neural Network decoder is utilized to complete t he inversion of structural parameters from dispersion charact eristics. The results show that compared with CST simulation, the simulation speed of the proposed model is increased by 8 4 times.

title: Design and Development of Metallic Hole Array Frequency Selective Surface Lens to Enhance the Gain of Pyramidal Horn Antenna for High Power Microwave Applications
authors: R. Venkateswaran, P. Srikrishna, D. Kannadassan, S. K. Datta
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: A compact, high power, horn antenna with a fully metallic frequency selective surface (FSS) lens to enhance the gain of horn for High Power Microwave (HPM) applications is designed and developed at X band. The FSS consists of two different unit cells of circular holes to handle higher power levels and the array of holes constitute the metallic planar lens. Analysis of unit cell, pyramidal horn with 3 layered FSS lens were performed using 3D electromagnetic simulation software CST Microwave Studio. Radiation parameters of the fabricated antenna was measured and compared with simulation results. Gain of the horn antenna was enhanced by 7.25 dBi by FSS lens. Return loss of $\lt-\mathbf{1 5} \mathbf{~ d B}$ with an impedance bandwidth of $\mathbf{3 1. 6 \%}$ was obtained. The proposed lens antenna exhibits a gain 17.2 dBi at $\mathbf{X}$ band in a miniaturized structure with more power handling capability will be a suitable choice for compact high power microwave systems.

title: Fully-Actuated-System-Based Fault-Tolerant Spacecraft Attitude Control with ESO
authors: Tao Huo, Yang Yu, Chengfei Yue, Xueqin Chen
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper investigates fault-tolerant attitude control for rigid spacecraft subject to actuator faults and external disturbances. A novel control scheme combining the fully actuated system (FAS) approach and a linear extended state observer (ESO) is proposed. Actuator faults and disturbances are treated as a lumped term, which is accurately estimated by the ESO and compensated in the FAS-based control law. The input-to-state stability (ISS) of the closed-loop system is rigorously established. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme provides robustness and high-precision attitude control performance under multiple uncertainties.

title: Characterization of ECAM-Fabricated Millimeter-Wave Circuits
authors: Alan M. Cook, Jr Benjamin Spence Albright, Matthew H. Krohn, Justin A. Reiss, Samuel Luttinger, Greg Bower, Nichole Romine, Frank N. Wood
publish: 2026 IEEE International Vacuum Electronics Conference (IVEC)
abstract: We discuss progress on additive manufacturing (AM) methods for the fabrication of millimeter-wave (mmW) vacuum electronic (VE) devices. Copper electrochemical additive manufacturing (ECAM) is discussed for fabrication of high-power VE circuits in solid metal, with integrated RF components such as waveguide power couplers. We present RF characterization of an ECAM-fabricated traveling-wave tube (TWT) circuit in the W-band frequency range. $R F$ data demonstrates frequency response with passband similar to design simulations. The measured $R F$ loss is consistent with an effective conductivity $\sim 1 / 10$ that of the DC conductivity of bulk copper, indicating that surface roughness is a significant factor.

title: Dynamic Event-Based Adaptive Set-Point Tracking Control for Overhead Crane with Unknown Load Mass
authors: Xinlei Gao, Jiangbo Yu, Zhongcai Zhang, Lulu Wu, Qiqi Yang
publish: 2026 5th Conference on Fully Actuated System Theory and Applications (FASTA)
abstract: This paper proposes a dynamic event-based set-point tracking control scheme for a two-dimensional overhead crane system with unknown parameters and limited communication resources. Firstly, the dynamic model is converted into a standard lower-triangular form by linearizing the system and performing coordinate transformations. Then, for the transformed system, the unknown parameters are estimated by using the adaptive backstepping technique. Furthermore, to conserve communication resources, an adaptive event-triggered set-point tracking controller is constructed based on event-triggered control (ETC). It is shown that the proposed control scheme guarantees that all the closed-loop signals are globally bounded and the tracking error converges to zero. Moreover, Zeno behavior can be excluded. Simulation results are presented to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: A Low-Profile Circularly Polarized Millimeter-Wave Dense Dielectric Patch Antenna with an Embedded Resonant Probe
authors: Wen-Jian Sun, Hang Wong
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A low-profile circularly polarized (CP) millimeter-wave (mm-wave) dense dielectric patch antenna (DDPA) array based on a rotated resonant probe feed is proposed. Different from a conventional microstrip patch antenna, the DDPA allows an embedded resonant probe to introduce an additional resonant path under a strict low-profile constraint, thereby enhancing the bandwidth without increasing either the antenna profile or the lateral footprint. CP radiation is realized by rotating the resonant probe excitation without modifying the ceramic patch or using sequential-rotation feeding. The operating mechanism is clarified through modal analysis, and a 4×4 prototype array is fabricated and measured to validate the concept. The measured results show a 14.4% overlapping impedance and 3-dB axial-ratio bandwidth and a peak gain of 16.8 dBic, and an overall profile of 0.08 λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub>.

title: Auto-Calibrated Handheld Microwave Thermometer with Low-Noise and Enhanced EMI Immunity for Deep Tissue Sensing
authors: Neenu Vymeliyath Sajeev, Kavitha Arunachalam
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: A handheld low-noise, and high gain microwave radiometer operating over 2.7-3.1 GHz with excellent immunity to ambient electromagnetic (EM) interference is presented for passive tissue thermometry. Dual reference switch circulator Dicke configuration was implemented to realize a handheld sensing device with auto-calibration capability for compensating measurement instabilities. Full wave EM simulations of the multilayer printed circuit board (PCB) were carried out to design a field deployable handheld radiometer with reduced circuit parasitic, transmission line loss, and cross talk. Furthermore, a custom metal enclosure was designed to improve the radiated immunity of the hand-held radiometer. The fabricated miniaturized radiometer (70×50 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) has 50 dB gain, very low noise figure of 1.01 dB at 3 GHz, ‒3 dB bandwidth of 408 MHz, and thermal sensitivity of 0.30 K. Radiometer measurements demonstrated system stability, immunity to external EM interference and, ability to auto-calibrate thermal measurements for large variations in the sensor input impedance. Measurements also indicated better thermal sensitivity than the conventional single reference Dicke radiometer thereby demonstrating its potential field deployment for passive deep tissue thermometry.

title: Small-Signal Model and Equivalent Circuit Representation of Dual-Phase Coupled-Inductor Buck Converter with Peak-Current Control
authors: Zhihao Wang, Yingyi Yan, Bo Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive small-signal model of a dual-phase coupled-inductor Buck converter with peak current mode (PCM) control over the entire duty cycle range. The model is derived using the describing function (DF) method, and the resulting infinite-order model is reduced to a simplified pole–zero form. Based on the obtained finite-order model, systematic analyses of the stability boundary, the minimum required external ramp and audio susceptibility are conducted. Additionally, a simple equivalent circuit representation is proposed to provide physical insight and enable fast simulation in commonly used circuit simulators. The accuracy and effectiveness of the proposed model are validated through SIMPLIS simulations and experimental measurements. The model provides practical design guidelines for PCM controlled dual phase coupled inductor Buck converters under wide operating conditions and captures the key system dynamics, offering useful insights for converter design and optimization.

title: The F-ELV-based Privacy-Preserving Framework for Intrusion Detection in SDN-IoT Network
authors: Abdullah Kamaruddin, Tan Saw Chin, Jaime Galán-Jiménez, Irfan-Ullah Awan, Muhammad Younas
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Network intrusion detection in Software-Defined Networking (SDN)-enabled Internet of Things (IoT) increasingly relies on machine learning techniques. However, existing federated learning solutions predominantly focus on deep learning. This approach is impractical for resource-constrained SDN-IoT networks. No prior Federated Learning (FL) frameworks systematically federate classical machine learning models with algorithm-specific aggregation. Classical algorithms are better suited to such networks because they are lightweight, computationally efficient, and easier to interpret. This paper presents a novel Federated Enhanced Learning Voting (F-ELV) framework that enables resource-efficient and accurate intrusion detection by federating heterogeneous classical machine learning algorithms. The F-ELV framework introduces strategies tailored dynamically to each model type, including Decision Trees, Naive Bayes, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Logistic Regression, and ensemble methods. We implement stratified federated data distribution to ensure balanced class representation across clients. This prevents model bias and maintains detection accuracy for minority attack classes. A comprehensive evaluation across three cybersecurity datasets demonstrates that F-ELV outperforms centralized and standard federated learning approaches. It preserves privacy and computational efficiency. Detection accuracies range from 79% to 100% across different algorithms and datasets, with minimal federated learning degradation (0.1–6.45%) for tree-based methods. F-ELV also provides an empirical framework for evaluating training and detection times in federated cybersecurity environments. Its design and evaluation highlight strong potential for real-time deployment within SDN-IoT intrusion detection systems.

title: An Incremental Contrastive Learning Method for Compound Fault Diagnosis of Rolling Bearings
authors: Jiongyi Liu, Yuanguo Bi, Rao Fu, Jun Liu, Xiaoling Zhang, Liang Zhao, Ammar Hawbani
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Compound faults, which arise from the interaction of multiple simultaneous failures, pose significant risks to the maintenance of industrial machinery in the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), leading to complex and unpredictable system failures. Traditional models tend to misclassify emerging compound faults as known categories due to a bias toward seen data. Additionally, the delayed emergence of compound faults relative to single faults hampers prompt sample collection. Furthermore, compound fault datasets typically exhibit a long-tailed distribution. Driven by these challenges, we propose an incremental contrastive learning model based on cross-modal contrastive embedding (ICLCFD) to achieve an incremental fault diagnosis from single faults to compound faults.Firstly, we employ Symmetrized Dot Pattern (SDP) image transformation to convert vibration signals into visual representations. We extract high-dimensional visual features from these SDP images using a Multi-Scale Residual Convolutional Neural Network (MS-ResCNN) and generate low-dimensional semantic features based on vibration signals to obtain richer feature information. Subsequently, a novelty detection mechanism is developed using the predicted number of fault sources as a count-based indicator to identify newly emerging faults. Furthermore, we incorporate a difficulty-aware class rebalancing sampling strategy to prioritize hard-to-diagnose samples during incremental updates, mitigating the excessive impact of head-class samples on the diagnosis results. Experiments on three open-source bearing datasets (CWRU, PU, and XJTU-SY) demonstrate that ICLCFD achieves a state-of-the-art accuracy of 96.58 ± 0.28%, outperforming existing methods by approximately 2%. The results confirm its effectiveness in handling unknown and compound faults in dynamic IoT maintenance pipelines.

title: Multi-Temporal UAV Imagery Feature Fusion for Paddy Yield Prediction in Tropical Smallholder Farms: Integrating RGB Color, Texture, and NDVI Indicators Across Growth Stages
authors: Zico Pratama Putra, Arief Abdurrakhman, Dian Kusumaningrum, Mohammad Berel Toriki, I Putu Eka Widya Pratama, Ahmad Radhy
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate paddy (Oryza sativa) yield prediction is essential for food security planning and precision agriculture. While most existing approaches rely on single-phase observations near harvest, they fail to capture the cumulative growth dynamics that determine final productivity. This study presents a multi-temporal framework for predicting paddy yield using UAV-derived RGB and multispectral (NDVI) imagery across three critical growth stages—Vegetative, Reproductive, and Ripening—spanning 514 experimental plots in Kedungwaras, Lamongan, East Java. The field was cultivated with a locally adapted early-maturing (genjah) rice variety. Hand-crafted features including color moments, GLCM texture descriptors, and NDVI statistics were extracted and fused temporally. An extensive comparison was conducted between traditional Machine Learning models (Support Vector Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost) and Deep Learning baselines (Multi-Layer Perceptrons). Our results demonstrate that SVR using the fusion of Reproductive and Ripening phases achieves the best predictive performance among tested configurations (R² = 0.2083, MAE = 0.1277 kg/m²), representing a 24.7% relative improvement over single-phase ripening prediction. Notably, traditional ML models significantly outperformed tabular MLP architectures on this dataset scale. SVR feature importance rankings were explored using Kernel SHAP and compared with Random Forest Tree SHAP (Spearman rank correlation rho = 0.217, p = 0.1151). Leave-One-Petak-Out spatial block cross-validation was implemented, revealing a drop in R² (best RF R² = 0.0642) due to spatial autocorrelation and localized microclimates. These findings highlight the value of temporal feature fusion for identifying critical monitoring windows in tropical smallholder agriculture, while underscoring the challenges of spatial generalizability.

title: Ho:YAG Thin-Disk Laser with 230 W Multimode and 152 W Single-Mode Output
authors: Xiyi Wang, Xudong Yan, Weichao Yao, Yujie Peng, Yuxin Leng
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: We report a continuous-wave Ho:YAG thin-disk laser operating at the hundred-watt power level. In multimode operation, the laser delivers a maximum output power of 230 W, with a slope efficiency of 35.6% and an optical-to-optical conversion efficiency of 35.3%. In single-mode operation, an output power of 152.3 W is achieved, with beam quality factors M2 of 1.08 and 1.06 in the horizontal and vertical directions, respectively.

title: Safety-Critical Space-and-Time-Synchronized Cooperative Formation: Virtual-Structure-Based Framework
authors: Peng Wang, Weilin Cheng, Xiuhui Peng, Haibin Wang, Weidong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a three-dimensional (3D) safety-critical cooperative guidance and control strategy for a group of vehicles aiming at simultaneous formation around a target. First, a reference relative trajectory is constructed using piecewise B<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\acute {e}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>zier curves to define a time-independent spatial path, along which a space-and-time synchronization strategy is employed to ensure that all vehicles reach their designated relative positions simultaneously. Next, the dimensionless residual path and velocity in reference relative motion are taken as coordination variables in the space-and-time-synchronized cooperative guidance, and a distributed coordination strategy with upper and lower bound constraints on path velocity is designed. Finally, fixed-time controllers with integrated extended state observers (ESOs) are designed for kinematic and kinetic systems to track the reference trajectory under control saturation, while safety constraints are enforced via control barrier function (CBF)-based compensation. Numerical simulations demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed cooperative guidance law.

title: FlexiTensor: Adaptive Multi-Task Deployment of LLMs on Resource-Constrained Heterogeneous Edge Devices
authors: Bowen Zhang, Jiahui Hou, Junyang Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: The drive for privacy-preserving and low-latency artificial intelligence necessitates executing Large Language Models (LLMs) directly on heterogeneous, resource-constrained edge devices. This paradigm presents a challenge: efficiently running large models across multiple end/edge devices under a strict energy budget. Especially, the problem becomes more complicated when it comes to orchestrating multiple complex tasks using large models at the same time. To address this, we introduce FlexiTensor, an offline planning and deployment system for LLM inference, scaling from a single task to multiple concurrent tasks. FlexiTensor first considers LLM inference for a single task under a strict energy budget. We design a heuristic optimization algorithm to minimize the latency under a strict energy budget. Specifically, we select an optimized subset of devices and tensor allocations. Based on the selection, latency can be reduced by using non-uniform tensor parallelism with quantization. FlexiTensor can be extended to multi-task cases. We model the execution structure of multi-agent workflows as pre-defined Directed Acyclic Graphs (DAGs) and reformulate the problem with energy consumption constraints. FlexiTensor introduces a novel hybrid evolutionary algorithm to address joint task scheduling and resource allocation in multi-task cases. This approach navigates the vast search space of task placement, tensor splitting, and thread assignment to minimize the overall workflow makespan. All optimization algorithms run offline before deployment, using pre-profiled device characteristics to compute a static execution plan. Extensive experiments on a physical testbed of heterogeneous edge devices demonstrate that FlexiTensor significantly outperforms baselines. We accelerate single-task inference by up to 50%. For multi-task workflows, we consistently find superior scheduling solutions, achieving speed improvements by an average of 50% against competitive baselines and by up to 100% in certain scenarios, showcasing its effectiveness and adaptability for real-world edge LLM-based applications.

title: Height-Aware Navigation Framework for Single-DOF Deformable Mobile Robots Based on 3D Encoding
authors: Feiqiang Wang, Yukuan Chen, Haobo Huang, Zufeng Shang, Jun Zhang, Fufu Yang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: A deformable mobile robot with single-degree-of freedom (single-DOF) can autonomously navigate and avoid obstacles in complex environments by altering its three dimensional dimensions through deformation. This capability allows it to traverse passages that fixed-size robots cannot, such as height-restricted or narrow channels, thereby improving task efficiency. This paper proposes a height-aware navigation framework that projects the varying 3D geometric constraints into a grid map and costmap, enabling coupled motion deformation planning for single-DOF robots. To achieve this, the height restrictions in complex three-dimensional environments are projected as grayscale information into a two-dimensional grid map. Using these grayscale values, we design a costmap layer to reflect the robot's footprint dimensions in different deformation states. An A* algorithm with height encoding then obtains a global path containing the robot's height constraints. Finally, an enhanced Dynamic Window Approach (DWA) algorithm is proposed to calculate the robot's movement and deformation speeds, enabling it to utilize its single-DOF deformation capability to traverse height-restricted or narrow obstacle passages. To validate the effectiveness of each core module and highlight the advantages over traditional methods, simulations and experiments were conducted using a Miura-ori based single-DOF deployable robot.

title: Insect-Scale Magnetic Wheel-Based Climbing Robot with Micro Ultrasonic Motors and 3D Printed Planetary Gears_supp1-3703263.mp4
authors: Mohamed M. Khalil
publish: N/A
abstract: Miniaturization of climbing robots is of interest for accessing narrow and complex structures that are inaccessible to conventional robots. This paper presents the design, fabrication, and experimental validation of an insect-scale climbing robot with magnetic wheels. The robot integrates two key technologies: a micro ultrasonic motor, which is one of the smallest electricity-driven motors, and a micro planetary gear train with a gear ratio of 64, fabricated using a micro-stereolithography 3D printer. The combination of these technologies enables the miniaturization of climbing robots. With a differential steering mechanism driven by two geared motors, the prototype robot measures 13 mm×13 mm and weighs 0.77 g. To obtain a suitable magnetic adhesion force, the magnetic wheels are designed and optimized to ensure reliable locomotion on vertical surfaces. Experiments demonstrate that the robot can climb vertically while carrying a payload exceeding five times its own weight. The steerability tests confirm the robot's ability to perform turns along a desired trajectory, although some errors remain. These experiments have demonstrated the feasibility of the smallest magnet-wheel-based climbing robot, providing insights for future studies on micro-scale robotic exploration in constrained environments.

title: Multi-View Stenosis Classification Leveraging Transformer-Based Multiple-Instance Learning Using Real-World Clinical Data
authors: N. Cenikj, Ö. Turgut, A. Müller, A. Steger, J. Kehrer, M. Brugger, D. Rueckert, E. Martens, P. Müller
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Coronary artery stenosis is a leading cause of cardiovascular disease, diagnosed by analyzing the coronary arteries from multiple angiography views. Although numerous deep-learning models have been proposed for stenosis detection from a single angiography view, their performance heavily relies on expensive view-level annotations, which are often not readily available in hospital systems. Moreover, these models fail to capture the temporal dynamics and dependencies among multiple views, which are crucial for clinical diagnosis. To address this, we propose SegmentMIL, a transformer-basedmulti-viewmultiple-instance learning framework for patient-level stenosis classification. Trained on a real-world clinical dataset, using patient-level supervision and without any view-level annotations, SegmentMIL jointly predicts the presence of stenosis and localizes the affected anatomical region, distinguishing between the right and left coronary arteries and their respective segments. SegmentMIL obtains high performance on internal and external evaluations and outperforms both view-level models and classical MIL baselines, underscoring its potential as a clinically viable and scalable solution for coronary stenosis diagnosis. Our code is available at https://github.com/NikolaCenic/mil-stenosis.

title: SemanticST: Semantics-enhanced Spatio-Temporal Modeling for Ejection Fraction Estimation in Echocardiography
authors: Dashun Zheng, Jiaxuan Li, Mingyuan Meng, Tengfei Ma, Wei Li, Zhifan Gao, Patrick Pang, Tao Tan
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Estimating left ventricular Ejection Fraction (EF) from echocardiography is critical for cardiac systolic function assessment and clinical risk stratification. Unfortunately, existing EF estimation methods are limited by (i) insufficient modeling of temporal clues embedded in video frames and (ii) inadequate exploitation of clinical semantics in easily accessible textual reports. In this study, we propose a unified segmentation and EF estimation framework with Semantics-enhanced spatio-temporal modeling (named SemanticST), integrating spatio-temporal consistency modeling with structured clinical semantic priors. Our SemanticST introduces a spatio-temporal & text-guided neighborhood correlation mining (STT-NCM) encoder, which captures both short- and long-range temporal dependencies via text-modulated 3D neighborhood attention. Further, a text-guided pixel-level semantic projection module (TextSP) is designed to map the key clinical cues extracted by a large language model (LLM) into pixel-level guidance features, enabling the alignment of semantic priors with visual context for optimized EF estimation. Extensive experiments on two public datasets (CAMUS and EchoNet-Dynamic) demonstrate that our SemanticST outperforms state-of-the-art methods in segmentation accuracy, temporal consistency, and EF estimation correlation.

title: Tomahawk Ultra: Ultra Low Latency, High Bandwidth Ethernet Switch for HPC & AI/ML applications
authors: Asad Khamisy, Mohan Kalkunte, Niranjan Vaidya, Ian Cox
publish: IEEE Micro
abstract: This article presents the architecture and innovations of the Broadcom Tomahawk Ultra Ethernet switch introduced in 2025. Tomahawk Ultra is the first Ethernet Switch to address the needs of High Performance Computing Fabrics and Scale Up Network for Artificial Intelligence / Machine Learning XPU Clusters by delivering high bandwidth and ultra-low latency.
